text: <s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.1 spoken by धृतराष्ट्र. [/INST] Verse 1.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by धृतराष्ट्र, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धरमकषतर करकषतर समवत ययतसव ममक पणडवशचव कमकरवत सञजय 11
Translations:
1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sanjaya what did my people and the Pāṇḍavas do gathered together on the holy field of Kurukshetra eager for battle
2. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said O Sanjaya what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do when they assembled on the sacred field of Kurukshetra eager for battle
3. Dhritarashtra said What did my people and the sons of Pandu do when they had assembled together eager for battle on the holy plain of Kurukshetra O Sanjaya
4. Dhritarashtra said O Sanjaya what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do in the Kurukshetra the field of righteousness where the entire warring class had assembled
5. The King Dhritarashtra asked O Sanjaya What happened on the sacred battlefield of Kurukshetra when my people and the Pandavas gathered
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.2 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दषटव त पणडवनक वयढ दरयधनसतद आचरयमपसङगमय रज वचनमबरवत 12
Translations:
1. Sanjaya said King Duryodhana upon seeing the Pandava army in battle array approached his teacher Drona and said these words
2. Sanjaya said But then seeing the army of the Pandavas in battle array King Duryodhana approached the teacher Drona and spoke
3. Sanjaya said Having seen the army of the Pandavas drawn up in battle array King Duryodhana approached his teacher Drona and spoke these words
4. Sanjaya said Seeing the army of the sons of Pandu marshaled in military array the prince Duryodhana approached the teacher Drona and spoke these words at that time
5. Sanjaya replied When Prince Duryodhana saw the Pandavas army paraded he approached his preceptor Guru Drona and spoke these words
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.3 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पशयत पणडपतरणमचरय महत चमम वयढ दरपदपतरण तव शषयण धमत 13
Translations:
1. Behold O teacher this mighty army of the Pandavas arrayed by the son of Drupada your intelligent disciple
2. O teacher behold this vast army of the sons of Pandu arrayed for battle by the intelligent disciple of Drupada
3. Behold O Teacher This mighty army of the sons of Pandu arrayed by the son of Drupada thy wise disciple
4. O teacher Behold this mighty army of the sons of Pandu marshaled in a military array by Drupadas son your intelligent pupil
5. Revered Father Behold this mighty host of the Pandavas paraded by the son of King Drupada your wise disciple
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.4 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अतर शर महषवस भमरजनसम यध ययधन वरटशच दरपदशच महरथ 14
Translations:
1. There in that army are heroes great bowmen such as Bhima and Arjuna Yuyudhana Virata and Drupada a mighty warrior
2. Here are the heroes wielding great bows who in battle are peers of Bhima and Arjuna Yuyudhana Satyaki and Virata and the maharatha great chariotrider Drupada
3. Here are heroes mighty archers equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna Yuyudhana Satyaki Virata and Drupadaall mighty warriors
4. Here are the heroes and mighty archers comparable in war to Bhima and Arjuna Yuyudhana the king of the Virata country and Drupada the mighty warrior
5. In it are heroes and great bowmen the equals in battle of Arjuna Bheema Yuyudhana Virata and Drupadaall great soldiers
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.5 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धषटकतशचकतन कशरजशच वरयवन परजतकनतभजशच शबयशच नरपङगव 15
Translations:
1. Dhrstaketu Cekitana the valiant king of Kasi Purujit Kuntibhoja and Saibya the best among men
2. Dhrstaketu Cekitana and the valiant king of Kasi Varanasi Purujit Kuntibhoja and Saibya the choicest of men
3. Dhrishtaketu Chekitana the valiant king of Kasi Purujit Kuntibhoja and Saibyathe best of men
4. Dhrstaketu Cekitana the valorous king of Kasi Kuntibhoja the conqueror of many and the Sibi king the best among men
5. Dhrishtaketu Chekitan the valiant king of Benares Purujit Kuntibhoja and Shaibyaa master of many
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.6 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यधमनयशच वकरनत उततमजशच वरयवन सभदर दरपदयशच सरव एव महरथ 16
Translations:
1. Yudhamanyu the valiant one and Uttamaujas the strong one and also the son of Subhadra as well as the sons of Draupadi all mighty warriors
2. And the valiant Yudhamanyu and the chivalrous Uttamaujas the son of Subhadra Abhimanyu and the sons of Draupadiall of them are indeed maharathas
3. The strong Yudhamanyu and the brave Uttamaujas the son of Subhadra Abhimanyu the son of Subhadra and Arjuna and the sons of Draupadi all of them great charioteers great heroes
4. And Yudhamanyu the heroic and Uttamaujas the valiant the son of Subhadra and the sons of Draupadiall of them are indeed mighty warriors
5. Yudhamanyu Uttamouja Soubhadra and the sons of Droupadiall famous men
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.7 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: असमक त वशषट य तननबध दवजततम नयक मम सनयसय सजञरथ तनबरवम त 17
Translations:
1. Know O best of Brahmanas those who are important on our sidethose who are the commanders of my army I shall name them to refresh your memory
2. But O best among the Brahmanas please be appraised of those who are foremost among us the founders of my army I speak of them to you by way of illustration
3. Know also O best among the twiceborn the names of those who are the most distinguished amongst ourselves the leaders of my army these I name to you for your information

4. O best among the twiceborn Please take note of the most distinguished amongst us who are the generals of my army and who are accepted as leaders by the heroes in my mighty army I shall name them to you
5. Further take note of all those captains who have ranged themselves on our side O best of Spiritual Guides The leaders of my army I will name them for you
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.8 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भवनभषमशच करणशच कपशच समतञजय अशवतथम वकरणशच समदततसतथव च 18
Translations:
1. Yourself Bhisma Karna the victorious Krpa Asvatthama Vikarna and Jayadratha the son of Somadatta
2. They are Your venerable self Bhisma Karna and Krpa who is ever victorious in battle Asvatthama Vikarna Saumadatti and Jayadratha
3. Thou thyself Bhishma Karna Kripa the victorious in war Asvatthama Vikarna and Bhurisrava the son of Somadattaall these are ready for battle
4. Your good self Bhisma Karna Krpa Salya Jayadratha Asvatthaman Vikarna and Somadattas son the valiant ones
5. You come first then Bheeshma Karna Kripa great warriors Ashwaththama Vikarna and the son of Somadhatta
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.9 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनय च बहव शर मदरथ तयकतजवत ननशसतरपरहरण सरव यदधवशरद 19
Translations:
1. And there are many other heroes who are determined to give up their lives for my sake They are all experts in using manifold missiles and dexterous in battle
2. There are many heroes who have dedicated their lives to me possessing various kinds of weapons and missiles all of whom are skilled in battle
3. And also many other heroes ready to give up their lives for my sake armed with various weapons and missiles all wellskilled in battle
4. And many other heroes giving up their lives for my sake fighting with various weapons all highly skilled in different forms of warfare
5. And many others all ready to die for My sake all armed all skilled in warfare
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.10 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अपरयपत तदसमक बल भषमभरकषतम परयपत तवदमतष बल भमभरकषतम 110
Translations:
1. Our force is inadequate guarded by Bhisma while their force is adequate guarded by Bhima
2. Therefore our army under the complete protection of Bhisma and others is limitless But this army of the enemies under the protection of Bhima and others is limited
3. Our army marshalled by Bhishma is insufficient whereas theirs marshalled by Bhima is sufficient
4. Thus the army guarded by Bhima is unlimited for us whereas the army guarded by Bhisma is sufficient for them the Pandavas
5. Yet our army seems weaker though commanded by Bhishma their army seems stronger though commanded by Bhima
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.11 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अयनष च सरवष यथभगमवसथत भषममवभरकषनत भवनत सरव एव ह 111
Translations:
1. Therefore all of you taking your places firmly in your respective divisions should guard Bhisma at all costs
2. However venerable sirs all of you without exception while occupying all the positions in the different directions as allotted to you respectively please protect Bhisma in particular
3. Therefore do all of you stationed in your respective positions in the several divisions of the army protect Bhishma alone
4. Stationed firmly in all their respective paths each one of them should guard Bhisma above all without exception
5. Therefore let the rank and file stand firm in their posts according to battalions and all you generals around Bheeshma
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.12 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसय सजनयनहरष करवदध पतमह सहनद वनदयचच शङख दधम परतपवन 112
Translations:
1. Then the valiant grandsire Bhisma the seniormost of the Kuru clan roared like a lion and blew his conch with the intention of cheering up Duryodhana
2. The valiant grandfather the eldest of the Kurus loudly sounding a lions roar blew the conch to raise his Duryodhanas spirits
3. His glorious grandsire the oldest of the Kauravas roared like a lion to cheer Duryodhana and blew his conch
4. Generating joy in him the powerful paternal grandfather Bhisma the seniormost among the Kurus roared like a lion and blew his conchshell loudly
5. Then to enliven his spirits the brave Grandfather Bheeshma the eldest of the Kuru clan blew his conch its sound resembling that of a lions roar
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.13 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तत शङखशच भरयशच पणवनकगमख सहसवभयहनयनत स शबदसतमलऽभवत 113
Translations:
1. Then suddenly conchs kettle drums trumpets tabors and blow horns blared forth and the sound was terrific
2. Just immediately afterward conch shells kettledrums tabors trumpets and cowhorns blared forth creating a tumultuous sound
3. Then suddenly conches kettledrums tabors drums and cow horns blared forth from the Kaurava side and the sound was tremendous
4. Then all of a sudden the conchshells drums tambourines trumpets and cowhorns sounded in unison the sound was tumultuous
5. And immediately all the conches drums trumpets and horns blared forth in a tumultuous uproar
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.14 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तत शवतरहयरयकत महत सयनदन सथत मधव पणडवशचव दवय शङख परदधमत 114
Translations:
1. Then Sri Krishna and Arjuna stationed in their great chariot yoked with white horses blew their divine conchs
2. Then Madhava Krishna and the son of Pandu Arjuna stationed in their magnificent chariot with white horses yoked to it blew their divine conchs loudly
3. Then Madhava Krishna and the son of Pandu Arjuna seated in the magnificent chariot yoked with white horses blew divine conches
4. Then mounted on a mighty chariot yoked with white horses Madhava Krishna and the son of Pandu Arjuna blew their heavenly conch shells
5. Then seated in their spacious war chariot yoked with white horses Lord Shri Krishna and Arjuna sounded their divine conch shells
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.15 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पञचजनय हषकश दवदतत धनजय पणडर दधम महशङख भमकरम वकदर 115
Translations:
1. Sri Krishna blew his conch Pancajanya Arjuna his Devadatta and Bhima of terrible deeds blew his great conch Paundra
2. Hrsikesa Krsna blew the conch Pancajanya Dhananjaya Arjuna blew the conch Devadatta and Vrkodara Bhima of terrible deeds blew the great conch Paundra
3. Hrishikesha blew the Panchajanya Arjuna blew the Devadatta and Bhima the wolfbellied doer of terrible deeds blew the great conch Paundra
4. Hrsikesa Krsna blew the Pancajanya Dhananjaya Arjuna blew the Devadatta and Bhima of the terrible deeds blew the mighty conchshell Paundra
5. Lord Shri Krishna blew his Panchajanya and Arjuna blew his Devadatta brave Bheema blew his renowned shell Poundra
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.16 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अननतवजय रज कनतपतर यधषठर नकल सहदवशच सघषमणपषपक 116
Translations:
1. King Yudhisthira the son of Kunti blew his conch Anantavijaya and Nakula and Sahadeva blew their conchs Sughosa and Manipuspaka
2. King Yudhisthira son of Kunti blew the Anantavijaya Nakula and Sahadeva blew the Sughosa and the Manipuspaka respectively
3. King Yudhishthira the son of Kunti blew the Anantavijaya Nakula and Sahadeva blew the Sughosha and the Manipushpaka
4. Kuntis son the king Yudhisthira blew the Anantavijaya Nakula and Sahadeva blew the Sughosa and Manipuspaka respectively
5. The King Dharmaraja the son of Kunti blew the Anantavijaya Nakula and Sahadeva the Sughosa and Manipushpaka respectively
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.17 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कशयशच परमषवस शखणड च महरथ धषटदयमन वरटशच सतयकशचपरजत 117
Translations:
1. And the King of Kasi the supreme bowman Sikandi the mighty warrior Dhrstadyumna and Virata and Satyaki the invincible
2. And the King of Kashi wielding a great bow and the great chariotrider Sikhandin Dhrstadyumna Virata and Satyaki the unconquered
3. The king of Kasi an excellent archer Sikhandi the mighty carwarrior Dhrishtadyumna Virata and Satyaki the unconquered
4. And the king of Kasi a great archer and Sikhandin a mighty warrior Dhrstadyumna and the king of Virata and the unconquered Satyaki
5. And the Maharaja of Benares the great archer Shikhandi the great soldier Dhrishtayumna Virata and Satyaki the invincible one
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.18 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दरपद दरपदयशच सरवश पथवपत सभदरशच महबह शङखनदधम पथकपथक 118
Translations:
1. Drupada and the sons of Draupadi as well as the strongarmed son of Subhadraall O King blew their respective conchs again and again
2. Drupada and the sons of Draupadi as well as the mightyarmed son of Subhadra all of them together O king blew their respective conchs
3. Drupada and the sons of Draupadi O Lord of the Earth and the son of Subhadra the mightyarmed blew their conches each separately
4. The Pancala king a mighty archer and Draupads five sons as well as the mightyarmed son of Subhadra each blew their own conchshells individually
5. And O King Drupadas sons Droupadis sons and the great soldier Soubhadra blew their conches
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.19 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: स घष धरतरषटरण हदयन वयदरयत नभशच पथव चव तमल वयननदयन 119
Translations:
1. And that tumultuous uproar reverberating through heaven and earth rent the hearts of Dhrtarastras sons
2. That tremendous sound pierced the hearts of the sons of Dhrtarastra as it reverberated through the sky and the earth
3. The tumultuous sound rent the hearts of Dhritarashtras party reverberating through both heaven and earth
4. Resonating in both the sky and the earth the tumultuous sound shattered the hearts of Dhrtarastras men
5. The tumult rent the hearts of the sons of Dhritarashtra shaking heaven and earth with its echoing violence
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.20 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 1.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथ वयवसथतन दषटव धरतरषटरनकपधवज परवतत शसतरसपत धनरदयमय पणडव 120
Translations:
1. Then Arjuna whose banner crest was Hanuman on beholding the sons of Dhrtarastra arrayed took up his bow while missiles were beginning to fly

2. O King thereafter seeing Dhrtarastras men standing in their positions with all the weapons ready for action the son of Pandu Arjuna who had the insignia of Hanuman on his chariotflag raised his bow and said the following to Hrsikesa

3. Then seeing the people of Dhritarashtras party standing arrayed and the discharge of weapons about to begin Arjuna the son of Pandu whose ensign was a monkey took up his bow and said the following to Krishna O Lord of the Earth
4. O king Then observing Dhrtarastras men arrayed when the armed clash had virtually begun at that time Pandus son the monkeybannered one Arjuna raising his bow spoke these sentences
5. Then upon beholding the sons of Dhritarashtra drawn up on the battlefield ready to fight Arjuna whose flag bore the image of Hanuman
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.21 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: हषकश तद वकयमदमह महपत सनयरभयरमधय रथ सथपय मऽचयत 121
Translations:
1. And he spoke O Lord of the Earth these words to Sri Krishna Arjuna said Draw up my chariot O Krishna between the two armies
2. Arjuna said O Acyuta please place my chariot between both the armies

3. Arjuna said O Krishna place my chariot in the middle between the two armies so that I may behold those who stand here desirous to fight and know with whom I must fight when the battle is about to commence

4. Arjuna said O Acyuta Please halt my chariot at a central place between the two armies so that I may scrutinize these men who are standing with a desire to fight and with whom I have to engage in this great wareffort
5. Raising his bow he spoke this to the Lord Shri Krishna O Infallible Lord of the earth Please draw up my chariot between the two armies
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.22 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यवदतननरकषऽह यदधकमनवसथतन करमय सह यदधवयमसमनरणसमदयम 122
Translations:
1. So that I may have a good look at those who are standing eager to fight and know with whom I must fight in this enterprise of war
2. Until I survey those who stand intent on fighting and those who are about to engage in battle with me in this impending war

3. Arjuna said O Krishna place my chariot in the middle between the two armies so that I may behold those who stand here desirous to fight and know with whom I must fight when the battle is about to commence
4. I shall scrutinize those who are ready to fight who have assembled here and are eager to achieve in the battle what is dear to the evilminded son of Dhrtarastra
5. So that I may observe those who must fight on my side and those who must fight against me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.23 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतसयमननवकषऽह य एतऽतर समगत धरतरषटरसय दरबदधरयदध परयचकरषव 123
Translations:
1. I wish to see those gathered here ready to fight in this battle in order to please the evilminded son of Dhrtarashtra
2. These who have assembled here and want to accomplish in the war what is dear to the perverted son of Dhrtarastra I find them to be intent on fighting
3. For I desire to observe those who are assembled here to fight wishing to please in battle the evilminded Duryodhanathe son of Dhritarashtra

4. Sanjaya said O descendant of Bharata Dhrtarastra Thus instructed by Gudakesa Arjuna Hrsikesa halted the best chariot at a place in between the two armies in front of Bhisma and Drona and all the rulers of the earth He then said O son of Prtha Behold these Kurus assembled
5. And gaze upon this array of soldiers eager to please the sons of Dhritarashtra who are sinful
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.24 spoken by संजय. [/INST] Verse 1.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by संजय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एवमकत हषकश गडकशन भरत सनयरभयरमधय सथपयतव रथततमम 124
Translations:

1. Sanjaya said Thus addressed by Arjuna Sri Krishna drew up the best of chariots between the two armies in full view of Bhisma Drona and all the other kings O Dhrtarastra He then said O Arjuna behold these assembled Kauravas

2. Sanjaya said O scion of the line of Bharata Hrishikesa being told so by Gudakesa Arjuna placed the excellent chariot between the two armies in front of Bhishma and Drona as well as all the rulers of the earth and said O Partha behold these assembled Kurus
3. Sanjaya said Thus addressed by Arjuna Krishna stationed the best of chariots O Dhritarashtra in the midst of the two armies

4. Sanjaya said O descendant of Bharata Dhrtarastra Thus instructed by Gudakesa Arjuna Hrsikesa halted the best chariot at a place in between the two armies in front of Bhisma and Drona and all the rulers of the earth He then said O son of Prtha Behold these Kurus assembled
5. Sanjaya said Having listened to Arjunas request Lord Shri Krishna drew up His bright chariot in the midst between the two armies
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.25 spoken by संजय. [/INST] Verse 1.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by संजय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भषमदरणपरमखत सरवष च महकषतम उवच परथ पशयतनसमवतनकरनत 125
Translations:

1. Sanjaya said Thus addressed by Arjuna Sri Krishna drew up the best of chariots between the two armies in full view of Bhisma Drona and all the other kings O Dhrtarastra He then said O Arjuna behold these assembled Kauravas

2. Sanjaya said O scion of the line of Bharata Dhrtarashtra Hrsikesa being told so by Gudakesa Arjuna placed the excellent chariot between the two armies in front of Bhisma and Drona as well as all the rulers of the earth and said O Partha Arjuna behold these assembled people of the Kuru dynasty
3. In front of Bhishma and Drona and all the rulers of the earth he said O Arjuna son of Pritha behold these Kurus gathered together
4. There in both the armies the son of Prtha observed his fathers paternal grandfathers teachers maternal uncles brothers sons sons sons and comrades fathersinlaw and also friends
5. Where Bheeshma and Drona had led all the rulers of the earth they spoke thus O Arjuna Behold these members of the family of Kuru are assembled
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.26 spoken by संजय. [/INST] Verse 1.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by संजय, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.40 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.42 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.43 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 1.46 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 1.46 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

5. Arjuna argued My Lord How can I when the battle rages send an arrow through Bheeshma and Drona whom I should revere
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.5 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 2.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: गरनहतव ह महनभवन शरय भकत भकषयमपह लक हतवरथकमसत गरनहव भञजय भगन रधरपरदगधन 25
Translations:

1. It is better even to live on a beggars fare in this world than to slay these most venerable teachers If I were to slay my teachers though they may be degraded by desire for wealth I would only be enjoying bloodstained pleasures here

2. Rather than killing the nobleminded elders it is better in this world to live even on alms But by killing them we shall only be enjoying here the pleasures of wealth and desirable things drenched in blood

3. Better it is indeed in this world to accept alms than to slay the most noble teachers But if I were to kill them even in this world all my enjoyments of wealth and fulfilled desires would be stained with their blood

4. It is indeed good to go about begging in this world without killing the elders of great dignity however I would not enjoy with greed for wealth the bloodstained objects of pleasure by killing my elders

5. Rather I would be content with a beggars crust than to kill these teachers of mine these precious noble souls To slay these masters who are my benefactors would be to stain the sweetness of lifes pleasures with their blood
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.6 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 2.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न चतदवदम कतरनन गरय यदव जयम यद व न जयय यनव हतव न जजवषम सतऽवसथत परमख धरतरषटर 26
Translations:

1. We do not know which of the two is better for us whether our vanquishing them or their vanquishing us The very sons of Dhrtarastra whom if we were to slay we would not wish to live even they are standing in array against us
2. We do not know which is better for us whether we shall win or they shall conquer us Those very sons of Dhrtarastra whom we do not wish to kill stand in confrontation
3. I can hardly tell which would be better that we should conquer them or that they should conquer us Even the sons of Dhritarashtra whom we do not wish to slay stand facing us

4. Whether we should conquer in the battle or they should conquer uswe do not know which is better for us For having killed whom we would not wish to live at all the same persons stand before us as Dhrtarastras men

5. Nor can I say whether it would be better for them to conquer me or for me to conquer them since I would no longer care to live if I killed these sons of Dhritarashtra who are now preparing for battle
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.7 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 2.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करपणयदषपहतसवभव पचछम तव धरमसममढचत यचछरय सयननशचत बरह तनम शषयसतऽह शध म तव परपननम 27
Translations:

1. With my heart stricken by the fault of weak compassion my mind perplexed about my duty I implore you to tell me for certain what is good for me I am your disciple teach me who have taken refuge in you
2. With my nature overpowered by weak commiseration my mind bewildered about duty I supplicate You Tell me for certain what is better I am Your disciple having taken refuge in You Instruct me
3. My heart is overpowered by the taint of pity my mind is confused as to my duty I ask Thee Tell me decisively what is good for me I am Thy disciple instruct me who has taken refuge in Thee

4. With my very nature overpowered by the taint of pity and with my mind utterly confused as to the right action at this present juncture I ask you Tell me definitely what would be good for me I am your pupil please teach me who is taking refuge in You
5. My heart is oppressed with pity and my mind is confused as to what my duty is Therefore my Lord tell me what is best for my spiritual welfare for I am Your disciple Please direct me I pray
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.8 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 2.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न ह परपशयम ममपनदय दयचछकमचछषणमनदरयणम अवपय भमवसपतनमदधमर जय सरणमप चधपतयम 28
Translations:
1. Even if I were to win unchallenged sovereignty of a prosperous earth or even lordship over the Devas I do not feel that it would dispel the grief that withers up my senses
2. For I do not see anything that even after acquiring on this earth a prosperous kingdom free from enemies and even sovereignty over the gods can remove my sorrow that is blasting the senses
3. I do not see that this sorrow that burns up my senses would be removed even if I were to attain prosperous and unrivaled dominion on earth or lordship over the gods

4. I do not clearly see what would drive out my grief the scorcher of my senses even after achieving a prosperous and unrivaled kingship on this earth and also the overlordship of the gods in the heavens
5. For even if I were to attain the monarchy of the visible world or the sovereignty over the invisible world it would not drive away the anguish that is now paralyzing my senses
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.9 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 2.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एवमकतव हषकश गडकश परनतप न यतसय इत गवनदमकतव तषण बभव ह 29
Translations:
1. Sanjaya said Having thus spoken to Sri Krsna Arjuna the conqueror of sleep and the scorcher of foes said I will not fight and became silent
2. Sanjaya said Having spoken thus to Hrsikesa Krsna Gudakesa Arjuna the afflictor of foes verily became silent telling Govinda I shall not fight
3. Sanjaya said Having spoken thus to Hrishikesha the Lord of the senses Arjuna the conqueror of sleep and destroyer of foes said I will not fight and became silent
4. Sanjaya said O scorcher of foes Dhrtarastra Having spoken to Hrsikesa the master of senseorgans Govinda in this manner and having declared I will not fight Gudakesa became silent
5. Sanjaya continued Arjuna the conqueror of all enemies then told the Lord of AllHearts that he would not fight and became silent O King
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.10 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 2.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तमवच हषकश परहसननव भरत सनयरभयरमधय वषदनतमद वच 210
Translations:
1. O King to him who was thus sorrowing between the two armies Sri Krishna spoke the following words as if smiling by way of ridicule
2. O descendant of Bharata Hrsikesa mocking as it were said these words to him who was sorrowing between the two armies
3. To him who was despondent in the midst of the two armies Krishna smiling O Bharata spoke these words
4. O descendant of Bharata Hrsikesa as if smiling spoke to him who was sinking in despondency between two armies
5. Thereupon the Lord with a gracious smile addressed him who was so much depressed in the midst of the two armies
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अशचयननवशचसतव परजञवदशच भषस गतसनगतसशच ननशचनत पणडत 211
Translations:
1. The Lord said You grieve for those who should not be grieved for yet you speak words of wisdom The wise grieve neither for the dead nor for the living
2. The Blessed Lord said You grieve for those who are not to be grieved for and you speak words of wisdom The learned do not grieve for the departed nor for those who have not departed
3. The Blessed Lord said You have grieved for those who should not be grieved for yet you speak words of wisdom The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead

4. While lamenting for those who cannot be lamented on and those who do not deserve to be lamented on you do not speak like a wise man The learned do not lament for those who have departed life and those who have not departed life
5. Lord Shri Krishna said Why grieve for those for whom no grief is due yet professing wisdom The wise grieve neither for the dead nor the living
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न तववह जत नस न तव नम जनधप न चव न भवषयम सरव वयमत परम 212
Translations:
1. There never was a time when I did not exist nor you nor any of these kings Nor will there be any time in the future when all of us shall cease to exist
2. But certainly it is not a fact that I did not exist at any time nor you nor these rulers of men And surely it is not that we all shall cease to exist after this
3. Nor at any time was I not nor thou nor these rulers of men nor verily shall we ever cease to be hereafter
4. Never indeed at any time did I not exist nor you nor these kings and never shall we all not exist hereafter
5. There was never a time when I was not nor you nor these princes were not there will never be a time when we shall cease to exist
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दहनऽसमनयथ दह कमर यवन जर तथ दहनतरपरपतरधरसततर न महयत 213
Translations:
1. Just as the self associated with a body passes through childhood youth and old age so too at death it passes into another body A wise man is not deluded by that
2. As boyhood youth and decrepitude are to an embodied being in this present body so is the acquisition of another body Therefore an intelligent person does not get deluded
3. Just as the embodied soul passes through childhood youth and old age in this body so too does it pass into another body the steadfast one does not grieve over this
4. Just as boyhood youth and old age come to the embodied soul in this body so too is the attainment of another body the wise man is not deluded by this
5. As the soul experiences infancy youth and old age in this body so finally it passes into another the wise have no delusion about this
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मतरसपरशसत कनतय शतषणसखदखद आगमपयनऽनतयसतसततकषसव भरत 214
Translations:
1. The contact of the senses with their objects O Arjuna gives rise to feelings of cold and heat pleasure and pain They come and go never lasting long Endure them O Arjuna
2. But the contacts of the organs with the objects are the producers of cold and heat happiness and sorrow They have a beginning and an end and are transient Bear them O descendant of Bharata
3. The contact of the senses with the objects O son of Kunti which causes heat and cold pleasure and pain has a beginning and an end they are impermanent endure them bravely O Arjuna
4. O son of Kunti The touches with the matras cause feelings of cold and heat pleasure and pain they are of the nature of coming and going and are transient Bear them O Descendant of Bharata
5. Those external relations that bring cold and heat pain and happiness come and go they are not permanent Endure them bravely O Prince
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य ह न वयथयनतयत परष परषरषभ समदखसख धर सऽमततवय कलपत 215
Translations:
1. For he whom these do not affect O chief of men and to whom pain and pleasure are equal that steadfast man alone is worthy of immortality
2. O Arjuna who is foremost among men verily the person whom these do not torment the wise man to whom sorrow and happiness are the samehe is fit for immortality
3. That firm man whom surely these afflictions do not O chief among men to whom pleasure and pain are the same is fit for attaining immortality
4. O best among persons That wise person becomes immortal whom these situations do not trouble and to whom pleasure and pain are equal
5. The hero whose soul is unmoved by circumstances who accepts pleasure and pain with equanimity is only fit for immortality
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नसत वदयत भव नभव वदयत सत उभयरप दषटऽनतसतवनयसतततवदरशभ 216
Translations:
1. The unreal never comes into being the real never ceases to be The truth about these two is seen by the seers
2. There is no being of the unreal the real has no nonexistence Yet the nature of both has been realized by the seers of Truth
3. The unreal has no being there is no nonbeing of the real the truth about both has been seen by the knowers of the truth or the seers of the essence
4. Birth does not happen to what is nonexistent and destruction to what is existent the finality of these two has been seen by the seers of reality
5. That which is not shall never be that which is shall never cease to be These truths are selfevident to the wise
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवनश त तदवदध यन सरवमद ततम वनशमवययसयसय न कशचत करतमरहत 217
Translations:
1. Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded No one can cause the destruction of this immutable
2. But know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded No one can bring about the destruction of this immutable
3. Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded No one can cause the destruction of that the Imperishable
4. And know That to be indestructible by Which all this universe is pervaded no one is capable of causing destruction to this unchanging One
5. The Spirit which pervades all that is seen is imperishable Nothing can destroy it
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनतवनत इम दह नतयसयकत शररण अनशनऽपरमयसय तसमदयधयसव भरत 218
Translations:
1. These bodies of the Jiva the embodied self are said to have an end while the Jiva itself is eternal indestructible and incomprehensible Therefore fight O Bharata Arjuna
2. These destructible bodies are said to belong to the everlasting indestructible and indeterminable embodied One Therefore O descendant of Bharata join the battle
3. These bodies of the embodied Self which are eternal indestructible and immeasurable are said to have an end Therefore fight O Arjuna

4. These physical bodies which have an end and suffer destruction are declared to belong to the eternal embodied Soul which is indestructible and incomprehensible Therefore fight O descendant of Bharata
5. The material bodies that this Eternal Indestructible and Immeasurable Spirit inhabits are all finite therefore fight O valiant man
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य एन वतत हनतर यशचन मनयत हतम उभ त न वजनत नय हनत न हनयत 219
Translations:
1. He who considers It the self to be the slayer and he who thinks of It as slainboth are ignorant For the self neither slays nor is being slain
2. He who thinks of this One as the killer and he who thinks of this One as the one being killed both of them do not know This One neither kills nor is It killed
3. He who takes the Self to be the slayer and he who thinks it is slain neither of them knows It does not slay nor is it slain
4. Whoever views This as the slayer and whoever believes This to be the slain both of them do not understand This does not slay nor is This slain
5. He who thinks that the Spirit kills and he who thinks of it as killed are both ignorant The Spirit neither kills nor is it killed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न जयत मरयत व कदच ननय भतव भवत व न भय अज नतय शशवतऽय परण न हनयत हनयमन शरर 220
Translations:
1. It is never born it never dies having come into existence once it never ceases to exist Unborn eternal perpetual and primordial it is not destroyed when the body is destroyed
2. Never is this One born nor does It ever die nor having come into existence does It cease to be This One is birthless eternal undecaying and ancient It is not killed when the body is killed
3. It is not born nor does it ever die after having been it again does not cease to be unborn eternal changeless and ancient it is not killed when the body is killed
4. This is neither born nor ever dies nor having not been at one time will it come to be again It is unborn destructionless eternal and ancient and is not slain even when the body is slain
5. It was not born it will never die nor once having been can it cease to exist Unborn eternal everenduring yet most ancient the spirit does not die when the body is dead
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वदवनशन नतय य एनमजमवययम कथ स परष परथ क घतयत हनत कम 221
Translations:
1. He who knows this self to be indestructible unborn unchanging and thus eternalhow O Arjuna does he cause anyone to be killed and whom does he kill
2. O Partha he who knows this One as indestructible eternal birthless and undecayinghow can that person kill or cause to be killed whom
3. Whoever knows it to be indestructible eternal unborn and inexhaustible how can that person slay O Arjuna or cause to be slain
4. Whoever realizes this to be changeless destructionless unborn and immutable how can that person be slain How can they slay anyone else O son of Prtha
5. He who knows the Spirit to be indestructible immortal unborn and always the same how should he kill or cause to be killed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वसस जरणन यथ वहय नवन गहणत नरऽपरण तथ शररण वहय जरण नयनयन सयत नवन दह 222
Translations:
1. As a man casts off wornout garments and puts on new ones so does the embodied self cast off its wornout bodies and enter into new ones
2. As after rejecting wornout clothes a man takes up other new ones likewise after rejecting wornout bodies the embodied one unites with other new ones
3. Just as a man casts off wornout clothes and puts on new ones so too the embodied Self casts off wornout bodies and enters others that are new
4. Just as a man rejects tattered garments and takes on new ones so too the embodied Self discarding wornout bodies rightly obtains new ones
5. As a man discards his threadbare robes and puts on new ones so the Spirit throws off its wornout bodies and takes on fresh ones
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नन छनदनत शसतरण नन दहत पवक न चन कलदयनतयप न शषयत मरत 223
Translations:
1. Weapons cannot cleave It fire cannot burn It waters cannot wet It and wind cannot dry It
2. Weapons cannot cut It fire cannot burn It water cannot moisten It and air cannot dry It
3. Weapons cannot cut it fire cannot burn it water cannot wet it wind cannot dry it
4. Weapons cannot cut This fire cannot burn This water cannot make This wet and the wind cannot make This dry
5. Weapons cannot cleave it fire cannot burn it water cannot drench it and wind cannot dry it
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अचछदयऽयमदहयऽयमकलदयऽशषय एव च नतय सरवगत सथणरचलऽय सनतन 224
Translations:
1. It cannot be cleaved It cannot be burned It cannot be wetted and It cannot be dried It is eternal allpervasive stable immovable and primordial
2. It cannot be cut burned moistened or dried up it is eternal omnipresent stationary unmoving and unchanging
3. This Self cannot be cut burned wetted nor dried up it is eternal allpervasive stable immovable and ancient
4. This cannot be cut this cannot be burnt this cannot be made wet nor dried this is eternal allpervading stable immovable and everlasting
5. It is impenetrable it cannot be drowned scorched or dried It is eternal allpervasive unchanging immovable and most ancient
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवयकतऽयमचनतयऽयमवकरयऽयमचयत तसमदव वदतवन ननशचतमरहस 225
Translations:
1. This self is said to be unmanifest inconceivable and unchanging Therefore knowing it thus it does not befit you to grieve
2. It is said that This is unmanifest This is inconceivable This is unchangeable Therefore having known This thus you ought not to grieve
3. This Self is said to be unmanifested unthinkable and unchangeable Therefore knowing this to be so you should not grieve
4. This is declared to be nonevident imponderable and unchangeable Therefore understanding this as such you should not lament
5. It is named the Unmanifest the Unthinkable and the immutable Therefore knowing the Spirit as such you have no cause to grieve
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथ चन नतयजत नतय व मनयस मतम तथप तव महबह नव शचतमरहस 226
Translations:
1. Or if you consider this self as constantly being born and constantly dying O mightyarmed one it still does not become you to feel grief
2. On the other hand if you think this One is born continually or constantly dies even then O mightyarmed one you should not grieve
3. But even if thou thinkest of It as constantly being born and constantly dying even then O mightyarmed one thou shouldst not grieve
4. On the other hand if you deem this as being born constantly or as dying constantly even then O mightyarmed one you should not lament
5. Even if thou thinkest of it as constantly being born and constantly dying O Mighty Man thou still hast no cause to grieve
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जतसय ह धरव मतयरधरव जनम मतसय च तसमदपरहरयऽरथ न तव शचतमरहस 227
Translations:
1. For death is certain for the born and rebirth is certain for the dead therefore you should not grieve for what is inevitable
2. For the death of anyone born is certain and the rebirth of the dead is certain Therefore you should not grieve over an inevitable fact
3. For the born death is certain and for the dead birth is certain therefore you should not grieve over the inevitable
4. Death is certain indeed for what is born and birth is certain for the dead Therefore you should not lament over something that is unavoidable
5. For death is certain for that which is born just as birth is certain for that which is dead Therefore do not grieve for what is inevitable
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवयकतदन भतन वयकतमधयन भरत अवयकतनधननयव ततर क परदवन 228
Translations:
1. O Arjuna beings have an unknown beginning a known middle and an unknown end What is there to grieve about in all this
2. O descendant of Bharata all beings remain unmanifested in the beginning they become manifested in the middle After death they certainly become unmanifested What lamentation can there be for them
3. Beings are unmanifest in their beginning manifest in their middle state O Arjuna and unmanifest again in their end What is there to grieve about
4. O descendant of Bharata The beings have an unmanifest beginning a manifest middle and an unmanifest end Therefore why mourn
5. The end and beginning of beings are unknown we only see the intervening formations So what is there to grieve about
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आशचरयवतपशयत कशचदन मशचरयवदवदत तथव चनय आशचरयवचचनमनय शरणत शरतवपयन वद न चव कशचत 229
Translations:
1. One regards This self as a wonder another speaks of It as a wonder yet another hears of It as a wonder and even after hearing of It one does not know It
2. Someone visualizes it as a wonder someone else talks of it as a wonder someone else hears of it as a wonder and yet someone else does not even realize it after hearing about it
3. One sees this the Self as a wonder another speaks of it as a wonder another hears of it as a wonder yet having heard none understands it at all
4. This someone observes as a wonder similarly another speaks of This as a wonder another hears This as a wonder but even after hearing not even one understands This
5. One hears of the Spirit with surprise another thinks it marvelous the third listens without comprehending Thus though many are told about it scarcely is there one who knows it
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दह नतयमवधयऽय दह सरवसय भरत तसमतसरवण भतन न तव शचतमरहस 230
Translations:
1. The self in the body O Arjuna is eternal and indestructible This is the case for all selves in all bodies Therefore it is not fitting for you to feel grief for any being
2. O descendant of Bharata this embodied Self existing in everyones body can never be killed therefore you ought not to grieve for all these beings
3. This indweller in the body of everyone is ever indestructible O Arjuna therefore you should not grieve for any creature
4. O descendant of Bharata This embodied One in the body of everyone is ever incapable of being slain Therefore you should not lament over all beings
5. Do not be anxious about these armies the spirit in man is imperishable
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सवधरममप चवकषय न वकमपतमरहस धरमयदध यदधछरयऽनयतकषतरयसय न वदयत 231
Translations:
1. Further considering also your own duty it does not befit you to waver For to a Kshatriya there is no greater good than a righteous war
2. Even considering your own duty you should not waver since there is nothing better for a Ksatriya than a righteous battle
3. Further having regard to your duty you should not waver for there is nothing higher for a Kshatriya than a righteous war
4. Further considering your own duty you should not waver Indeed for a Kshatriya there is no duty superior to fighting a righteous war
5. You must look at your duty Nothing can be more welcome to a soldier than a righteous war Therefore wavering in this resolve is unworthy O Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यदचछय चपपनन सवरगदवरमपवतम सखन कषतरय परथ लभनत यदधमदशम 232
Translations:
1. Happy are the Ksatriyas O Arjuna to whom such a war comes of its own accord it opens the gates to heaven
2. O son of Partha happy are the Kshatriyas who come across such a battle which presents itself unsought and is an open gate to heaven
3. Happy are the Kshatriyas O Arjuna who are called to fight in such a battle that comes of its own accord as an open door to heaven
4. O son of Prtha By good fortune Ksatriyas desirous of happiness get a war of this type which has come of its own accord and which is an open door to heaven
5. Blessed are the soldiers who find their opportunity this opportunity has opened for thee the gates of heaven
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथ चततवमम धरमय सगरम न करषयस तत सवधरम करत च हतव पपमवपसयस 233
Translations:
1. But if you do not fight this righteous war you will be turning away from your duty and honor and will incur sin
2. On the other hand if you do not fight this righteous battle then you will incur sin forsaking your own duty and fame
3. But if you will not fight this righteous war then having abandoned your own duty and reputation you will incur sin
4. On the other hand if you do not fight this righteous war then you will incur sin by shirking your own duty and forfeiting your fame
5. Refuse to fight in this righteous cause and you will be a traitor losing fame incurring only sin
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अकरत चप भतन कथयषयनत तऽवययम सभवतसय चकरतरमरणदतरचयत 234
Translations:
1. Further people will speak ill of you for all time and for one accustomed to being honored dishonor is worse than death
2. People also will speak of your unending infamy and to an honored person infamy is worse than death
3. People will also recount your everlasting dishonor and for one who has been honored dishonor is worse than death
4. The creatures will speak of your endless illfame and for one who has been highly esteemed illfame is worse than death
5. Men will talk forever of thy disgrace and to the noble dishonor is worse than death
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.35 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.35 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भयदरणदपरत मसयनत तव महरथ यष च तव बहमत भतव यसयस लघवम 235
Translations:
1. The great warriors will think that you have fled from battle out of fear Those who held you in high esteem will now speak of you disparagingly
2. The great chariotriders will think of you as having desisted from the fight out of fear and you will fall into disgrace before them whom you had been estimable to
3. The great chariotwarriors will think that you have withdrawn from the battle out of fear and you will be held in low esteem by those who have held you in high regard
4. The mighty charioteers will think of you as having withdrawn from the battle out of fear having been highly esteemed by these men you will be viewed lightly
5. Great generals will think that you have fled from the battlefield out of cowardice though once honored you will seem despicable
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.36 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.36 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवचयवदशच बहन वदषयनत तवहत ननदनतसतव समरथय तत दखतर न कम 236
Translations:
1. Your enemies slandering your prowess will use words that should never be uttered What could be more painful than that
2. And your enemies will speak many indecent words while denigrating your power What can be more painful than that
3. Your enemies scoffing at your power will speak many abusive wordswhat could be more painful than this
4. Your enemies will slander your abilities and speak of you in many sayings that should not be spoken Is there anything more painful than that
5. Your enemies will spread scandal and mock your courage Is there anything more humiliating
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.37 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.37 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: हत व परपसयस सवरग जतव व भकषयस महम तसमदततषठ कनतय यदधय कतनशचय 237
Translations:
1. If slain you will win heaven or if victorious you will enjoy the earth Therefore arise O Arjuna resolved to fight
2. Either by being killed you will attain heaven or by winning you will enjoy the earth Therefore O Arjuna rise up with determination to fight
3. Slain you will obtain heaven victorious you will enjoy the earth therefore stand up O son of Kunti resolved to fight
4. If you are slain you shall attain heaven or if you conquer you shall enjoy the earth Therefore O son of Kunti stand up with resolution made in favor of fighting the battle
5. If you are killed you will attain Heaven if you are victorious you will enjoy the kingdom of earth Therefore arise O Son of Kunti and fight
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.38 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.38 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सखदख सम कतव लभलभ जयजय तत यदधय यजयसव नव पपमवपसयस 238
Translations:
1. Holding pleasure and pain gain and loss victory and defeat as equal gird yourself up for the battle Thus you shall not incur any sin
2. Treating happiness and sorrow gain and loss and conquest and defeat with equanimity then engage in battle Thus you will not incur sin
3. Having made pleasure and pain gain and loss victory and defeat equal engage in battle for the sake of battle thus you shall not incur sin
4. Viewing pleasure and pain gain and loss victory and defeat alike you should get yourself ready for the battle Thus you will not incur sin
5. Look upon pleasure and pain victory and defeat with an equal eye Be prepared for the combat and you will commit no sin
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.39 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.39 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एष तऽभहत सखय बदधरयग तवम शरण बदधययकत यय परथ करमबनध परहसयस 239
Translations:
1. This Buddhi concerning the Self Sankhya has been imparted to you Now listen to this with regard to Yoga by following which you will get rid of the bondage of Karma

2. O Partha this wisdom has been imparted to you from the standpoint of Selfrealization But listen to this wisdom from the standpoint of Yoga endowed with which you will get rid of the bondage of action
3. This which has been taught to you is wisdom concerning Sankhya Now listen to wisdom concerning Yoga endowed with which O Arjuna you shall cast off the bonds of action
4. Listen how this knowledge imparted to you for your Sankhya is also for the Yoga endowed with this knowledge you shall cast off the bondage of action O son of Prtha
5. I have told you the philosophy of knowledge Now listen and I will explain the philosophy of action by means of which O Arjuna you will break through the bondage of all action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.40 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नहभकरमनशऽसत परतयवय न वदयत सवलपमपयसय धरमसय तरयत महत भयत 240
Translations:
1. Here there is no loss of effort nor any accrual of evil Even a little of this Dharma called Karma Yoga protects one from the great fear
2. Here there is no waste of effort nor is there any harm Even a little of this righteousness saves one from great fear
3. In this there is no loss of effort nor is there any harm produced nor any transgression Even a little of this knowledge protects one from great fear
4. Here there is no loss due to transgression and there is no contrary downward course sin even a little of this righteous thing can save one from great danger
5. On this path endeavour is never wasted nor repressed Even a little practice of it protects one from great danger
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.41 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.41 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वयवसयतमक बदधरकह करननदन बहशख हयननतशच बदधयऽवयवसयनम 241
Translations:
1. In this Karma Yoga O Arjuna the resolute mind is singlepointed the minds of the irresolute are multibranched and endless
2. O scion of the Kuru dynasty in this there is a single onepointed conviction The thoughts of the irresolute ones however have many branches indeed and are innumerable
3. Here O joy of the Kurus there is only one singlepointed determination manybranched and endless are the thoughts of the indecisive
4. O source of joy to the Kurus The knowledge in the form of determination is one however the knowledge thoughts of those who lack determination has many branches and has no end
5. By its means the straying intellect becomes steadied in contemplating one object only whereas the minds of the irresolute stray into innumerable bypaths
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.42 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यमम पषपत वच परवदनतयवपशचत वदवदरत परथ ननयदसतत वदन 242
Translations:

1. O Partha the unwise who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas say There is nothing else They are full only of worldly desires and they hanker for heaven They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work They look upon the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech offering heavenly rewards and are unable to develop the resolute will of a concentrated mind

2. O son of Prtha those undiscerning people who utter this flowery talk which promises birth as a result of rites and duties and is full of various special rites meant for the attainment of enjoyment and affluence remain engrossed in the utterances of the Vedas and declare that nothing else exists their minds are full of desires and they have heaven as their goal
3. The unwise taking pleasure in the eulogizing words of the Vedas utter flowery speech saying There is nothing else O Arjuna

4. O son of Prtha Those whose very nature is desire whose goal is heaven who esteem only the Vedic declaration of fruits who declare that there is nothing else who proclaim this flowery speech about the paths to the lordship of the objects of enjoymentpaths that are full of different actionsand who desire action alone as a fruit of their birththey are men without insight
5. Only the ignorant speak in figurative language it is they who extol the letter of the scriptures saying There is nothing deeper than this
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.43 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कमतमन सवरगपर जनमकरमफलपरदम करयवशषबहल भगशवरयगत परत 243
Translations:

1. O Partha the unwise who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas say There is nothing else They are full only of worldly desires and they yearn for heaven They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work They view the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech offering heavenly rewards and are unable to develop the resolute will of a focused mind

2. O son of Prtha those undiscerning people who utter this flowery talk which promises birth as a result of rites and duties and is full of various special rites meant for the attainment of enjoyment and affluence remain engrossed in the utterances of the Vedas and declare that nothing else exists their minds are full of desires and they have heaven as their goal

3. Full of desires with heaven as their goal they speak words that are directed to ends leading to new births as the result of their works and prescribe various methods abounding in specific actions for the attainment of pleasure and power

4. O son of Prtha Those whose very nature is desire whose goal is heaven who esteem only the Vedic declaration of fruits who declare that there is nothing else who proclaim this flowery speech about the paths to the lordship of the objects of enjoymentpaths that are full of different actionsand who desire action alone as a fruit of their birththey are men without insight
5. Consulting only their own desires they construct their own heaven devising arduous and complex rites to secure their own pleasure and power and the only result is rebirth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.44 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.44 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भगशवरयपरसकतन तयपहतचतसम वयवसयतमक बदध समध न वधयत 244
Translations:

1. O Partha the unwise who rejoice in the letter of the Vedas say There is nothing else They are full only of worldly desires and they hanker for heaven They speak flowery words that offer mirth as the fruit of work They look upon the Vedas as consisting entirely of varied rites for the attainment of pleasure and power Those who cling so to pleasure and power are attracted by that speech offering heavenly rewards and are unable to develop the resolute will of a concentrated mind
2. Onepointed conviction does not become established in the minds of those who delight in enjoyment and affluence and whose intellects are carried away by it

3. For those who are attached to pleasure and power whose minds are drawn away by such teachings their determinate reason is not formed which is steadily bent on meditation and Samadhi superconscious state

4. Those who are very attached to the ownership of enjoyable objects and whose minds have been carried away by that flowery speech their knowledge in the form of determination is not prescribed for concentration
5. While their minds are absorbed with thoughts of power and personal enjoyment they cannot focus their discrimination on one point
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.45 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.45 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तरगणयवषय वद नसतरगणय भवरजन नरदवनदव नतयसततवसथ नरयगकषम आतमवन 245
Translations:

1. The Vedas have the three Gunas as their sphere O Arjuna You must be free from the three Gunas and be free from the pairs of opposites Abide in pure Sattva never care to acquire things and to protect what has been acquired but be established in the Self

2. O Arjuna the Vedas Meaning only the portion dealing with rites and duties karmakanda have the three qualities as their object You become free from worldliness free from the pairs of duality everpoised in the quality of sattva without desire for acquisition and protection and selfcollected

3. The Vedas deal with the three attributes be thou above these three attributes O Arjuna free yourself from the pairs of opposites and ever remain in the quality of Sattva freed from acquisition and preservation and be established in the Self

4. The Vedas bind through the three strands Therefore O Arjuna be free from the three strands free from the pairs of opposites be established in this eternal Being be free from acquisition and preservation and possess the Self

5. The Vedic Scriptures tell of the three constituents of lifethe Qualities Rise above all of them O Arjuna above all the pairs of opposing sensations be steadfast in truth free from worldly anxieties and centered in the Self
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.46 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.46 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यवनरथ उदपन सरवत सपलतदक तवनसरवष वदष बरहमणसय वजनत 246
Translations:
1. What use does a thirsty person have for a water reservoir when all sides of it are floodedthat is the only use of all the Vedas for a Brahmana who knows
2. A Brahmana with realization has that much utility in all the Vedas as a person has in a well when there is a flood all around
3. To the Brahmana who has known the Self all the Vedas are of as much use as a reservoir of water would be in a place where there is a flood
4. What portion of a reservoir flooded with water everywhere is useful for a man in thirst That much portion alone in all the Vedas is useful for an intelligent student of the Vedas
5. As a man can drink water from any side of a full tank so the skilled theologian can extract from any scripture that which will serve their purpose
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.47 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.47 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमणयवधकरसत म फलष कदचन म करमफलहतरभरम त सङगऽसतवकरमण 247
Translations:
1. You have the right to work alone but not to the fruits of it Do not be driven by the results of your work nor have attachment to inaction
2. Your right is only to act not to the results Do not be the cause of the results of action Do not have any inclination towards inaction
3. Your right is only to work but not to its results do not let the results of action be your motive nor let your attachment be to inaction
4. Let your claim rest on action alone and never on the fruits you should never be the cause of the fruits of action let not your attachment be to inaction
5. But you have only the right to work but none to the fruit of it Let not the fruit of your action be your motive nor be you enamored of inaction
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.48 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.48 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यगसथ कर करमण सङग तयकतव धनञजय सदधयसदधय सम भतव समतव यग उचयत 248
Translations:
1. Abandoning attachment and established in Yoga perform works viewing success and failure with an even mind Evenness of mind is said to be Yoga
2. By being established in Yoga O Dhananjaya Arjuna undertake actions casting off attachment and remaining equanimous in success and failure Equanimity is called Yoga
3. Perform action O Arjuna being steadfast in Yoga abandoning attachment and balanced in success and failure evenness of mind is called Yoga
4. O Dhananjaya Establish yourself in the Yoga perform actions while abandoning attachment and remain evenminded in success and failure for evenmindedness is said to be the Yoga
5. Revised Perform all your actions with your mind concentrated on the Divine renouncing attachment and looking upon success and failure with an equal eye Spirituality implies equanimity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.49 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.49 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दरण हयवर करम बदधयगदधनञजय बदध शरणमनवचछ कपण फलहतव 249
Translations:
1. Action done with attachment O Arjuna is far inferior to action done with evenness of mind Seek refuge in evenness of mind those who act with a motive for results are miserable
2. O Dhananjaya indeed action is inferior to the yoga of wisdom Take refuge in wisdom Those who thirst for rewards are pitiable
3. Far lower than the Yoga of wisdom is action O Arjuna Seek thou refuge in wisdom wretched are those whose motive is the fruit

4. O Dhananjaya The inferior action stays away at a distance due to the yoga of ones contact with the determining faculty in the determining faculty you must seek refuge wretched are those who are the cause of the fruits of action
5. Physical action is far inferior to an intellect concentrated on the Divine Therefore have recourse to Pure Intelligence It is only the smallminded who work for reward
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.50 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.50 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बदधयकत जहतह उभ सकतदषकत तसमदयगय यजयसव यग करमस कशलम 250
Translations:
1. A person with an evenness of mind discards both good and evil here and now Therefore strive for Yoga for Yoga is skill in action
2. Possessed of wisdom one rejects here both virtue and vice Therefore devote yourself to Karma yoga for yoga is skilfulness in action
3. Endowed with wisdom and evenness of mind one casts off in this life both good and evil deeds therefore devote yourself to Yoga Yoga is skill in action
4. Whoever is endowed with the faculty of determination casts off both good and bad actions Therefore strive for Yoga which is proficiency in action
5. When a person attains Pure Reason they renounce in this world the results of both good and evil Cling to Right Action Spirituality is the true art of living
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.51 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.51 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमज बदधयकत ह फल तयकतव मनषण जनमबनधवनरमकत पद गचछनतयनमयम 251
Translations:
1. The wise who possess evenness of mind relinquish the fruits born of action and are freed from the bondage of birth going to the region beyond all ills
2. For those devoted to wisdom they become enlightened men by giving up the fruits of their actions and thus they reach a state beyond evil having been freed from the bondage of birth
3. The wise possessing knowledge having abandoned the fruits of their actions and being freed from the bonds of birth go to the place which is beyond all evil
4. By renouncing the fruits of their actions the intelligent ones endowed with the power of determination and freed from the bonds of birth go to a place that is free from illness
5. The sages guided by pure intellect renounce the fruits of their actions and thus freed from the shackles of rebirth they attain the highest bliss
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.52 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.52 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद त महकलल बदधरवयततरषयत तद गनतस नरवद शरतवयसय शरतसय च 252
Translations:
1. When your intellect has passed beyond the tangle of delusion you will yourself feel disgusted with what you have heard and what you will hear
2. When your mind goes beyond the turbidity of delusion then you will acquire dispassion for what has to be heard and what has already been heard
3. When your intellect passes beyond the mire of delusion then you will attain indifference to what has been heard and what has yet to be heard
4. When your determining faculty surpasses the impenetrable thicket of delusion then you will attain an attitude of futility regarding what needs to be heard and what has been heard
5. When your reason has crossed the entanglements of illusion then you will become indifferent to both the philosophies you have heard and those you may yet hear
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.53 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.53 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरतवपरतपनन त यद सथसयत नशचल समधवचल बदधसतद यगमवपसयस 253
Translations:
1. When your intellect having been well enlightened by hearing from Me and firmly placed stands unshaken in a concentrated mind then you will attain the vision of the Self
2. When your mind which has become bewildered by hearing becomes unshakable and steadfast in the Self then you will attain Yoga that arises from discrimination
3. When your intellect which is perplexed by the Vedic texts you have read stands immovable and steady in the Self then you will attain Selfrealization
4. When your determining faculty which had been previously confused by your hearing of scriptural declarations of fruits stands stable in concentration then you shall attain Yoga
5. When the intellect bewildered by the multiplicity of holy scriptures stands unperturbed in blissful contemplation of the infinite then you have attained spirituality
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.54 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 2.54 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सथतपरजञसय क भष समधसथसय कशव सथतध क परभषत कमसत वरजत कम 254
Translations:
1. Arjuna said What is the mode of speech of one who is of firm wisdom established in the control of the mind What does a man of firm wisdom speak O Krsna How does he sit How does he move
2. Arjuna said O Kesava what is the description of a man of steady wisdom who is selfabsorbed How does he speak sit and move about
3. Arjuna said O Krishna what is the description of one who has steady wisdom and is merged in the superconscious state How does one of steady wisdom speak how do they sit and how do they walk

4. Arjuna said O Kesava What is the meaning of sthitaprajna a manofstabilizedintellect when applied to a man fixed in concentration What does sthiradhih the fixedminded imply Where does the fixedminded abide And what is their goal

5. Arjuna asked My Lord How can we recognize the saint who has attained Pure Intellect who has reached this state of Bliss and whose mind is steady How does he talk how does he live and how does he act
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.55 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.55 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परजहत यद कमन सरवन परथ मनगतन आतमनयवतमन तषट सथतपरजञसतदचयत 255
Translations:
1. The Lord said When a man renounces all the desires of the mind and is content with himself then he is said to possess firm wisdom
2. The Blessed One said O Partha when one fully renounces all the desires that have entered the mind and remains satisfied in the Self alone then he is called a man of steady wisdom
3. The Blessed Lord said When a man completely casts off O Arjuna all the desires of the mind and is satisfied in the Self by the Self then he is said to be one of steady wisdom
4. The Bhagavat said O son of Prtha When a man casts off all desires existing in his mind and remains content in the Self by the self mind then he is called a man of stabilized intellect
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied When a person has given up the desires of their heart and is content with the Self alone they have surely reached the highest state
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.56 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.56 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दखषवनदवगनमन सखष वगतसपह वतरगभयकरध सथतधरमनरचयत 256
Translations:
1. He whose mind is not perturbed by pain who has no longing for pleasures who is free from desire fear and angerhe is called a sage of firm wisdom
2. That monk is called a man of steady wisdom when his mind is unperturbed even in sorrow he is free from longing for delights and has gone beyond attachment fear and anger
3. He whose mind is not shaken by adversity who does not long for pleasures and is free from attachment fear and anger is called a sage of steady wisdom
4. He whose mind is undisturbed amidst sorrows who is free from desire amidst pleasures and from whom longing fear and wrath have departedhe is said to be a firmminded sage
5. The sage whose mind is unruffled in suffering whose desire is not aroused by enjoyment who is without attachment anger or feartake him to be one who stands at a lofty level
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.57 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.57 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य सरवतरनभसनहसततततपरपय शभशभम नभननदत न दवषट तसय परजञ परतषठत 257
Translations:
1. He who has no love on any side who upon finding good or evil neither rejoices nor hateshis wisdom is firmly set
2. The wisdom of that person remains established who has no attachment to anything anywhere who neither welcomes nor rejects anything whatever good or bad when they come across it
3. He who is everywhere without attachment upon encountering anything good or bad neither rejoices nor hastens his wisdom is firm
4. He who has no desire for anything and who neither rejoices nor hates upon obtaining this or that good or badhis intellect is properly stabilized
