5. He who wherever he goes is not attached to any person or place by ties of flesh who accepts good and evil alike neither welcoming the one nor shrinking from the othertake him to be one who is merged in the Infinite
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.58 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.58 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद सहरत चय करमऽङगनव सरवश इनदरयणनदरयरथभयसतसय परजञ परतषठत 258
Translations:
1. When one is able to draw their senses away from the objects of sense on all sides like a tortoise draws in its limbs then their wisdom is firmly established
2. And when one fully withdraws their senses from the objects of the senses just as a tortoise withdraws its limbs then their wisdom remains established
3. When like the tortoise which withdraws all its limbs on all sides he withdraws his senses from the senseobjects then his wisdom becomes steady
4. When he withdraws all his senses from senseobjects just as a tortoise withdraws all its limbs then he is declared to be a man of stabilized intellect
5. He who can withdraw his senses from the attraction of their objects as the tortoise draws its limbs within its shelltake it that such a one has attained perfection
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.59 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.59 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वषय वनवरतनत नरहरसय दहन रसवरज रसऽपयसय पर दषटव नवरतत 259
Translations:

1. The objects of the senses except for the pleasure they bring turn away from the abstinent dweller in the body Even the pleasure turns away from him when the Self which is supreme over the senses is seen
2. The objects recede from an abstinent person except for the taste for them Even this taste falls away after realization of the Absolute
3. The objects of the senses turn away from the abstinent man leaving the longing behind but his longing also turns away upon seeing the Supreme
4. Leaving their taste behind the senseobjects retreat from the embodied one who abstains from food his taste too disappears when he beholds the Supreme
5. The objects of sense turn away from him who is abstinent Even the taste for them is lost in him who has seen the Truth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.60 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.60 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतत हयप कनतय परषसय वपशचत इनदरयण परमथन हरनत परसभ मन 260
Translations:
1. The turbulent senses O Arjuna can forcibly carry away the mind of even a wise man though he is ever striving
2. For O son of Kunti the turbulent senses can violently snatch away the mind of an intelligent person even while they are striving diligently
3. The turbulent senses O Arjuna can violently carry away the mind of a wise person even though they are striving to control them
4. For the turbulent senseorgans can forcibly carry away even the mind of this discerning person O son of Kunti
5. O Arjuna The mind of one who is attempting to conquer it is forcibly carried away in spite of their efforts by their tumultuous senses
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.61 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.61 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तन सरवण सयमय यकत आसत मतपर वश ह यसयनदरयण तसय परजञ परतषठत 261
Translations:
1. Having controlled all the senses let him remain in contemplation regarding Me as supreme for his knowledge is firmly established when his senses are under control
2. Controlling all of them one should remain focused on Me as the supreme For the wisdom of one whose senses are under control becomes steadfast
3. Having restrained them all he should sit steadfast intent on Me his wisdom is steady whose senses are under control
4. Restraining the same organs by the mind the master of Yoga would sit making Me their goal for the intellect of that person is stabilized whose senseorgans are under control
5. Restraining them all let him steadfastly meditate on Me for whoever conquers their senses achieves perfection
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.62 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.62 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धययत वषयनपस सङगसतषपजयत सङगत सजयत कम कमतकरधऽभजयत 262
Translations:
1. To one who is thinking about senseobjects attachment to them arises from attachment arises desire and from desire arises anger
2. In the case of a person who dwells on objects there arises attachment to them From attachment grows hankering and from hankering springs anger
3. When one thinks of objects attachment to them arises from attachment desire is born from desire anger arises
4. In a person meditating on senseobjects attachment to them is born in succession from attachment springs passion from passion arises wrath
5. When a person dwells on the objects of sense they create an attraction for them this attraction develops into desire and desire breeds anger
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.63 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.63 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करधदभवत समह समहतसमतवभरम समतभरशद बदधनश बदधनशतपरणशयत 263
Translations:
1. Anger leads to delusion which causes the loss of memory the loss of memory leads to the destruction of discrimination and with the destruction of discrimination one is lost
2. From anger follows delusion from delusion a failure of memory from the failure of memory the loss of understanding and from the loss of understanding one perishes
3. Anger leads to delusion which causes loss of memory this in turn leads to the destruction of discrimination resulting in destruction
4. From wrath comes delusion from delusion the loss of memory from the loss of memory the loss of capacity to decide due to the loss of capacity to decide he perishes outright
5. Anger induces delusion delusion leads to loss of memory loss of memory results in the shattering of reason and the destruction of reason leads to destruction
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.64 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.64 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रगदवषवयकतसत वषयननदरयशचरन आतमवशयरवधयतम परसदमधगचछत 264
Translations:
1. But he who goes through the senseobjects with the senses free from love and hate disciplined and controlled attains serenity
2. But by perceiving objects with the organs that are free from attraction and repulsion and are under his own control the selfcontrolled person attains serenity
3. But the selfcontrolled man moving among objects with the senses restrained and free from attraction and repulsion attains peace

4. On the contrary one who moves about and consumes the senseobjects through their senseorgans freed from desire and hatred and controlled in the Selfsuch a one with a disciplined mind attains serenity of disposition
5. But the selfcontrolled soul who moves among sense objects free from attachment or repulsion wins eternal peace
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.65 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.65 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परसद सरवदखन हनरसयपजयत परसननचतस हयश बदध परयवतषठत 265
Translations:
1. In that serenity all sorrow is lost for in the case of the person with a serene mind the Buddhi is quickly established
2. When there is serenity all his sorrows are eradicated for the wisdom of one with a serene mind soon becomes firmly established
3. In that peace all pains are destroyed for the intellect of the tranquilminded soon becomes steady
4. Upon attaining serenity all miseries are extinguished in succession the ability to make decisions is quickly stabilized in the mind of one who is serene
5. Having attained peace he becomes free from misery for when the mind gains peace right discrimination follows
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.66 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.66 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नसत बदधरयकतसय न चयकतसय भवन न चभवयत शनतरशनतसय कत सखम 266
Translations:
1. There is no Buddhi for the unintegrated nor is there contemplation of the self for him and without contemplation of the self there is no peace and for one who lacks peace where is the happiness
2. For the unsteady there is no wisdom and there is no meditation for the unsteady person And for an unmeditative person there is no peace How can there be happiness for one without peace
3. There is no knowledge of the Self for the unsteady and no meditation is possible for the unsteady and no peace for the unmeditative and how can there be happiness for one who has no peace

4. The ability to make decisions is not for one who is not a master of Yoga and concentration of the mind is not for one who is not a master of Yoga and peace is not for one who does not concentrate whence could happiness come to one who has no peace

5. Right discrimination is not for him who cannot concentrate Without concentration there cannot be meditation he who cannot meditate must not expect peace and without peace how can one expect happiness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.67 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.67 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इनदरयण ह चरत यनमनऽनवधयत तदसय हरत परजञ वयरनवमवमभस 267
Translations:
1. For when the mind follows the senses experiencing their objects his understanding is carried away by them just as the wind carries away a ship on the waters
2. For the mind which follows in the wake of the wandering senses carries away his wisdom like a boat on the waters
3. For the mind which follows in the wake of the wandering senses carries away his discrimination as the wind carries away a boat on the waters
4. That mind which is directed to follow the wandering senseorgansthat mind carries away his knowledge just as the wind carries away a ship on the waters
5. As a ship at sea is tossed by the tempest so the reason is carried away by the mind when preyed upon by straying senses
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.68 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.68 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमदयसय महबह नगहतन सरवश इनदरयणनदरयरथभयसतसय परजञ परतषठत 268
Translations:
1. Therefore O mightyarmed one he whose senses are restrained from going after their objects on all sides his wisdom is firmly established
2. Therefore O mightyarmed one this wisdom becomes established when all its senses are withdrawn from their objects
3. Therefore O mightyarmed Arjuna his knowledge is steady whose senses are completely restrained from sense objects
4. Therefore O mightyarmed one the intellect of that person is stabilized all of whose senseorgans starting from the senseobjects have been well restrained
5. Therefore O MightyArmed One he whose senses are detached from their objectstake it that his reason is purified
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.69 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.69 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य नश सरवभतन तसय जगरत सयम यसय जगरत भतन स नश पशयत मन 269
Translations:
1. What is night for all beings in it the one who is controlled is awake when all beings are awake that is the night for the sage who sees
2. The selfrestrained man stays awake during what is night for all creatures That which creatures stay awake for it is night to the discerning sage
3. That which is night to all beings in that the selfcontrolled man is awake when all beings are awake that is night for the sage who sees
4. What is night for every other being in that a man of selfrestraint is awake wherein every other being is awake that is night for the sage who sees the truth
5. The saint is awake when the world sleeps and he disregards that for which the world lives
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.70 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.70 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आपरयमणमचलपरतषठ समदरमप परवशनत यदवत तदवतकम य परवशनत सरव स शनतमपनत न कमकम 270
Translations:
1. He into whom all desires enter just as the waters enter the full and undisturbed sea attains peace and not he who longs after objects of desire
2. That man attains peace into whom all desires enter just as waters flow into the sea that remains unchanged even when filled from all sides Not so one who is desirous of objects
3. He attains peace into whom all desires enter just as waters enter the ocean which filled from all sides remains unmoved but not the man who is full of desires

4. Just as waters enter into the ocean which is being filled continuously yet firmly established so too he into whom all objects of desire enterhe attains peace not he who longs for the objects of desire
5. He attains peace into whom desires flow as rivers into the ocean which though brimming with water remains ever the same not he whom desires carry away
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.71 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.71 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वहय कमनय सरवनपमशचरत नसपह नरमम नरहकर स शतमधगचछत 271
Translations:
1. The one who abandoning all desires abides without longing possession and the sense of I and mine wins peace
2. That man attains peace who after rejecting all desires moves about free from hankering without the idea of me and mine and devoid of pride
3. That person attains peace who abandoning all desires moves about without longing without the sense of ownership and without egoism
4. That person who by abandoning all desires consumes objects without longing without a sense of ownership and without egotism attains peace
5. He attains peace who giving up desire moves through the world without aspiration possessing nothing that he can call his own and is free from pride
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 2.72 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 2.72 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एष बरहम सथत परथ नन परपय वमहयत सथतवऽसयमनतकलऽप बरहमनरवणमचछत 272
Translations:
1. This is the Brahmic state O Arjuna No one attaining to this is deluded By abiding in this state even at the hour of death one will attain the Self

2. O Partha this is the state of being established in Brahman One does not become deluded after attaining this one attains identification with Brahman by being established in this state even in the closing years of ones life
3. O son of Pritha this is the eternal state the Brahmic seat Attaining this one is not deluded Being established in it one attains oneness with Brahman even at the end of life

4. O son of Prtha This is the Brahmanic state having attained this one never gets deluded again Even by remaining in this state for a while one attains at the time of death the Brahman the Transcendent One

5. O Arjuna This is the state of the Self the Supreme Spirit to which if one attains it shall never be taken away Even at the time of leaving the body one will remain firmly enthroned there and become one with the Eternal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.1 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 3.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जययस चतकरमणसत मत बदधरजनरदन ततक करमण घर म नयजयस कशव 31
Translations:
1. Arjuna said O Krsna if you deem knowledge to be superior to action why then do you urge me to engage in this terrible deed
2. Arjuna said O Janardana Krishna if it is Your opinion that wisdom is superior to action why do You urge me to take such horrible action O Kesava
3. Arjuna said If Thou thinkest that knowledge is superior to action O Krishna why then O Kesava doest Thou ask me to engage in this terrible action
4. Arjuna said O Janardana if you hold knowledge to be superior to action then why do you engage me in such terrible action O Kesava
5. Arjuna questioned My Lord If wisdom is superior to action why do you advise me to engage in this terrible fight
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.2 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 3.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वयमशरणव वकयन बदध महयसव म तदक वद नशचतय यन शरयऽहमपनयम 32
Translations:
1. You confuse my mind with statements that seem to contradict each other tell me for certain the one way by which I could reach the highest good
2. You bewilder my understanding as it were with a seemingly conflicting statement Tell me for certain which one of these I may use to attain the highest Good
3. With this seemingly perplexing speech you seem to be confusing my understanding therefore tell me one certain way by which I may attain bliss
4. You seem to be confounding my intellect with Your seemingly confusing speech Therefore tell me with certainty that one thing by which I may attain the good emancipation
5. Your language confuses me and confounds my reason Therefore please tell me the only way by which I can without doubt secure my spiritual welfare
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: लकऽसमनदववध नषठ पर परकत मयनघ जञनयगन सखयन करमयगन यगनम 33
Translations:
1. The Lord said In this world a twofold way was of yore laid down by Me O sinless one Jnana Yoga for the Sankhyas and Karma Yoga for the Yogins

2. The Blessed Lord said O unblemished one two kinds of steadfastness in this world were spoken of by Me in days gone bythrough the Yoga of Knowledge for the men of realization through the Yoga of Action for the yogis
3. The Blessed Lord said In this world there is a twofold path as I said before O sinless one the path of knowledge of the Sankhyas and the path of action of the Yogins

4. The Bhagavat said The twofold path in this worldthe one with Yoga of knowledge for men of reflection and the other with Yoga of action for men of Yogahas been declared by Me to be one O sinless one
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied In this world as I have said there is a twofold path O sinless one There is the path of wisdom for those who meditate and the path of action for those who work
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न करमणमनरमभननषकरमय परषऽशनत न च सनयसनदव सदध समधगचछत 34
Translations:
1. No one experiences freedom from action Naiskarmya by abstaining from work and no one ever attains success through mere renunciation of work
2. A person does not attain freedom from action by abstaining from action nor does he attain fulfillment merely through renunciation
3. Man does not reach actionlessness by not performing actions nor does he attain perfection by mere renunciation
4. A person attains actionlessness not just by abstaining from actions but also by renunciation he attains success emancipation
5. No one can attain freedom from action by abstaining from action nor can they reach perfection by simply not acting
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न ह कशचतकषणमप जत तषठतयकरमकत करयत हयवश करम सरव परकतजरगण 35
Translations:
1. No one can remain still for even a moment without doing work for everyone is compelled to act despite themselves by the Gunas born of Nature
2. For no one ever remains even for a moment without doing work for all are made to work under compulsion by the gunas born of Nature
3. Verily no one can remain for even a moment without performing action for everyone is made to act helplessly indeed by the qualities born of Nature

4. For no one can ever remain even for a moment as a nonperformer of action because everyone not being master of themselves is forced to perform action by the strands born of the Prakriti Material cause
5. He cannot remain inactive even for a moment for the qualities of Nature will compel him to act whether he wills it or not
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमनदरयण सयमय य आसत मनस समरन इनदरयरथनवमढतम मथयचर स उचयत 36
Translations:
1. He who controlling the organs of action lets his mind dwell on the objects of the senses is a deluded person and a hypocrite
2. One who after withdrawing the organs of action sits mentally recollecting the objects of the senses is called a hypocrite for they have a deluded mind
3. He who restraining the organs of action sits thinking of the senseobjects in his mind he of deluded understanding is called a hypocrite
4. Controlling their organs of action whoever sits with their mind pondering over sense objectsthat person is a man of deluded soul and is called a man of deluded action
5. He who remains motionless refusing to act yet brooding over sensuous objects is that deluded soul simply a hypocrite
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसतवनदरयण मनस नयमयरभतऽरजन करमनदरय करमयगमसकत स वशषयत 37
Translations:
1. But he who subduing his senses by the mind O Arjuna begins to practice Karma Yoga through the organs of action and who is free from attachment excels
2. But O Arjuna one who engages in Karmayoga controlling the organs of action with the mind and becoming unattached excels
3. But whoever controlling the senses by the mind O Arjuna engages himself in Karma Yoga with the organs of action without attachment he excels
4. But controlling the senseorgans with the mind whoever undertakes the Yoga of action with the actionsenses he the detached one is superior O Arjuna
5. But O Arjuna All honor to him whose mind controls his senses for he is thereby beginning to practice KarmaYoga the Path of Right Action keeping himself always unattached
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नयत कर करम तव करम जयय हयकरमण शररयतरप च त न परसदधयदकरमण 38
Translations:
1. You must perform your obligatory action for action is superior to nonaction Jñāna Yoga For a person following nonaction not even the sustenance of the body is possible
2. You should perform your obligatory duties for action is superior to inaction Moreover even the maintenance of your body will not be possible without action
3. Perform your bounden duty for action is superior to inaction and even the maintenance of the body would not be possible for you through inaction
4. You must perform the action that has been enjoined upon you For action is superior to inaction and even the maintenance of your body cannot be properly accomplished through inaction
5. Do your duty as prescribed for action for dutys sake is superior to inaction Even the maintenance of the body would be impossible if one remained inactive
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यजञरथतकरमणऽनयतर लकऽय करमबनधन तदरथ करम कनतय मकतसग समचर 39
Translations:
1. This world is held in the bondage of work only when work is not performed as a sacrifice O Arjuna you must perform work for this purpose free from attachment
2. This man becomes bound by actions other than those intended for God Without being attached O son of Kunti perform actions for Him
3. The world is bound by actions other than those performed for the sake of sacrifice do thou therefore O son of Kunti Arjuna perform actions for that sake alone free from attachment
4. The world is fettered by action which is different from Yajnaaction therefore O son of Kunti free from attachment perform Yajnaaction properly
5. In this world people are fettered by their actions unless they are performed as a sacrifice Therefore O Arjuna let your actions be done without attachment as a sacrifice only
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सहयजञ परज सषटव परवच परजपत अनन परसवषयधवमष वऽसतवषटकमधक 310
Translations:
1. In the beginning the Lord of all beings created man along with the sacrifice and said By this shall you prosper this shall be the cow of plenty granting all your desires
2. In days of yore having created the beings together with the sacrifices Prajapati said By this you shall multiply Let this be your yielder of coveted objects of desire
3. The Creator having in the beginning created mankind together with sacrifice said By this shall you propagate let this be the milch cow of your desiresthe cow that yields all the desired objects

4. Having created creatures formerly at the time of creation together with the necessary action the Lord of creatures declared By means of this you shall propagate yourselves and let this be your wishfulfilling cow
5. In the beginning when God created all beings through the sacrifice of Himself He said to them You can procreate through sacrifice and it shall satisfy all your desires
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दवनभवयतनन त दव भवयनत व परसपर भवयनत शरय परमवपसयथ 311
Translations:
1. By this please the gods and they will support you Thus nourishing each other you may obtain the highest good
2. You nourish the gods with this Let those gods nourish you in return By mutually nourishing each other you shall attain the supreme Good
3. With this nourish the gods and may the gods nourish you thus nourishing each other you shall attain the highest good
4. You should gratify the devas with this and let the devas gratify you thus mutually gratifying each other you will attain the highest good
5. Worship the powers of Nature and let them nourish you in return thus supporting each other you shall attain your highest welfare
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इषटनभगनह व दव दसयनत यजञभवत तरदततनपरदयभय य भङकत सतन एव स 312
Translations:
1. The gods pleased by the sacrifice will bestow upon you the enjoyments you desire He who enjoys the bounty of the gods without giving them anything in return is but a thief
2. Being nourished by sacrifices the gods will indeed give you the coveted enjoyments He is certainly a thief who enjoys what has been given by them without offering it to them
3. The gods nourished by the sacrifice will give you the desired objects So he who enjoys the objects given by the gods without offering anything in return is indeed a thief
4. The devas gratified with necessary action will grant you the things sacrificed Therefore whoever enjoys their gifts without offering them to these devas is surely a thief
5. For fed by sacrifice nature will give you all the enjoyment you desire But he who enjoys what she gives without returning is indeed a thief
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यजञशषटशन सनत मचयनत सरवकलबष भञजत त तवघ पप य पचनतयतमकरणत 313
Translations:
1. Pious people who eat the remnants of sacrifices are freed from all sins whereas those who are sinful and cook only for their own sake will only incur sin
2. By partaking in the remnants of sacrifices they become freed from all sins But those who are unholy and cook for themselves incur sin
3. The righteous who eat the remnants of the sacrifice are freed from all sins but those sinful ones who cook food solely for their own sake indeed consume sin
4. The righteous persons who eat the remnants of the actions to be performed necessarily are freed from all sins But those who cook intending for their own selves are sinners and consume sin
5. The sages who enjoy the food that remains after the sacrifice is made are freed from all sins whereas the selfish who spread their feast only for themselves feed on sin alone
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अननदभवनत भतन परजनयदननसमभव यजञदभवत परजनय यजञ करमसमदभव 314
Translations:
1. All beings arise from food food is produced from rain rain comes from sacrifice and sacrifice is born of action
2. From food are born the creatures the origin of food is from rain rain originates from sacrifice sacrifice has action as its source
3. From food come forth beings from rain food is produced from sacrifice arises rain and sacrifice is born of action
4. From food arise the things that are born from the raincloud the food arises from the sacrifice the raincloud arises the sacrifice arises from action
5. All creatures are the product of food food is the product of rain rain comes from sacrifice and sacrifice is the noblest form of action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करम बरहमदभव वदध बरहमकषरसमदभवम तसमतसरवगत बरहम नतय यजञ परतषठतम 315
Translations:
1. Know that activity springs from Brahman ie the physical body Brahman arises from the imperishable Self therefore the allpervading Brahman is ever established in sacrifice
2. Know that action has the Vedas as its origin the Vedas have the Immutable as their source Hence the allpervading Vedas are forever based on sacrifice
3. Know that action comes from Brahma and Brahma comes from the Imperishable Therefore the allpervasive Brahma ever rests in sacrifice
4. Action arises from the Brahman you should know this the Brahman arises from that which does not stream forth therefore the allpervading Brahman is permanently based on the sacrifice
5. All actions originate from the Supreme Spirit which is Imperishable and in sacrificial actions the allpervasive Spirit is consciously present
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एव परवरतत चकर ननवरतयतह य अघयरनदरयरम मघ परथ स जवत 316
Translations:
1. He who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion here lives in sin satisfying the senses O Arjuna he lives in vain
2. O Partha he who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion lives in vain their life is sinful and they indulge in the senses
3. He who does not follow the wheel thus set in motion who is of sinful life rejoicing in the senses lives in vain O Arjuna
4. Whoever does not keep the wheel of life rolling which has been set in motion in this world is a man of sinful life rejoicing in the senses and he lives in vain O son of Prtha
5. Thus he who does not help the revolving wheel of sacrifice but instead leads a sinful life rejoicing in the gratification of his senses O Arjuna is breathing in vain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसतवतमरतरव सयदतमतपतशच मनव आतमनयव च सनतषटसतसय करय न वदयत 317
Translations:
1. But the man whose delight is only in the Self who is satisfied with the Self who rejoices in the Self for him nothing remains to be accomplished
2. But that man who rejoices only in the Self is satisfied with the Self and is content only in the Selffor him there is no duty to perform
3. But for that man who rejoices only in the Self who is satisfied with the Self and is content in the Self alone indeed there is nothing to do
4. But the man who simply rejoices in the Self is satisfied in the Self and delights in the Self alonethere exists no action for him to be performed
5. On the other hand the soul who meditates on the Self is content to serve the Self and rests satisfied in the Self there is nothing more for them to accomplish
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नव तसय कतनरथ नकतनह कशचन न चसय सरवभतष कशचदरथवयपशरय 318
Translations:
1. He has no purpose to gain from work done or left undone nor does he have to rely on any end
2. For him there is no concern here at all with performing action nor any with nonperformance Moreover he has no dependence on any object to serve any purpose
3. For him there is no interest whatsoever in what is done or not done nor does he depend on any being for any purpose
4. No purpose is served for him by what he has done or by what he has not done For him there is hardly any dependence on any purpose among all beings
5. He has nothing to gain from the performance or nonperformance of action his welfare does not depend on any contribution that an earthly creature can make
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमदसकत सतत करय करम समचर असकत हयचरनकरम परमपनत परष 319
Translations:
1. Therefore do your work without attachment which ought to be done For a person who works without attachment attains the Supreme
2. Therefore remaining unattached always perform the obligatory duty for by performing ones duty without attachment a person attains the highest
3. Therefore without attachment always perform the actions that should be done for by performing actions without attachment one reaches the Supreme
4. Therefore always remaining unattached you should perform the action that is to be performed for the person performing action without attachment attains the Supreme
5. Therefore do your duty perfectly without worrying about the results for he who does his duty without attachment attains the Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमणव ह ससदधमसथत जनकदय लकसगरहमवप सपशयनकरतमरहस 320
Translations:
1. Indeed Janaka and others reached perfection through Karma Yoga alone Even recognizing its necessity for guiding the world you must perform action
2. For Janaka and others strove to attain liberation through action itself You should perform your duties keeping in view the prevention of mankind from going astray
3. Janaka and others attained perfection indeed through action alone even with the intention of protecting the masses you should perform action
4. Janaka and others attained emancipation through action alone Moreover you should act with the intention of upholding the world
5. King Janaka and others attained perfection through action alone Even for the sake of enlightening the world it is your duty to act
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यदयदचरत शरषठसतततदवतर जन स यतपरमण करत लकसतदनवरतत 321
Translations:
1. Whatever a great man does others do too Whatever standard he sets the world follows it
2. Whatever a superior person does another person does the same Whatever they uphold as authority an ordinary person follows that
3. Whatever a great man does others also do whatever he establishes as the standard the world follows
4. Whatever a great man does others of lesser stature follow suit whatever standard he sets the world follows it
5. For whatever a great man does others imitate people conform to the standard he has set
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न म परथसत करतवय तरष लकष कञचन ननवपतमवपतवय वरत एव च करमण 322
Translations:
1. For me Arjuna there is nothing in all the three worlds that ought to be done nor is there anything unaccomplished that ought to be accomplished Yet I continue to work
2. In all the three worlds O Partha there is no duty for Me to fulfill nothing remains unachieved or to be achieved Still I continue in action
3. There is nothing in the three worlds O Arjuna that needs to be done by Me nor is there anything unattained that needs to be attained yet I engage Myself in action
4. O son of Prtha For Me there is nothing to be done in the three worlds nor is there anything yet to be attained and yet I am engaged in action
5. There is nothing in this universe O Arjuna that I am compelled to do nor anything for me to attain yet I persistently remain active
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद हयह न वरतय जत करमणयतनदरत मम वरतमनवरतनत मनषय परथ सरवश 323
Translations:
1. If I did not continue to work unweariedly O Arjuna people would follow my path
2. For O Partha if at any time I do not continue vigilantly in action people will follow My path in every way
3. For should I not ever engage myself in action unwearied people would in every way follow my path O Arjuna
4. For if I were ever not to work unwearied all men would follow My path O son of Prtha
5. Were I not to act without ceasing O prince people would be inclined to do the same
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: उतसदयरम लक न करय करम चदहम सङकरसय च करत सयमपहनयमम परज 324
Translations:
1. If I do not do the work these people will be lost and I will be causing chaos in life and thereby ruining all these people
2. These worlds would be ruined if I did not perform action And I shall become the agent of intermingling of castes and shall be destroying these beings
3. These worlds would perish if I did not perform action I would be the author of confusion of castes and destruction of these beings
4. These worlds would perish if I were not to perform action and I would be the cause of confusion I would destroy these people
5. And if I were to refrain from action the human race would be ruined I would lead the world to chaos and destruction would follow
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सकत करमणयवदवस यथ करवनत भरत करयदवदवसतथसकतशचकरषरलकसगरहम 325
Translations:
1. Just as the ignorant attached to their work act O Arjuna so too the learned should act without any attachment and only for the welfare of the world
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty as the unenlightened people act with attachment to work so should the enlightened person act without attachment desiring to prevent people from going astray
3. As the ignorant act out of attachment to action O Bharata so should the wise act without attachment wishing for the welfare of the world
4. Therefore just as the unwise persons being attached to action do O son of Prtha so the wise should perform but being unattached and desiring to hold the world together
5. As the ignorant act out of their fondness for action so should the wise act without attachment fixing their eyes O Arjuna only on the welfare of the world
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न बदधभद जनयदजञन करमसङगनम जषयतसरवकरमण वदवन यकत समचरन 326
Translations:
1. He should not bewilder the minds of the ignorant who are attached to work rather he should himself perform work with devotion and cause others to do the same
2. The enlightened man should not disturb the beliefs of the ignorant who are attached to work He should remain diligent himself and make them perform all their duties
3. Let no wise man unsettle the minds of ignorant people who are attached to action he should engage them in all actions himself fulfilling them with devotion
4. Let the wise master of Yoga fulfill or destroy all actions by performing them all and let him not create any disturbance in the minds of the ignorant persons attached to action

5. But a wise man should not disturb the minds of the ignorant who are attached to action let him perform his own actions in the right spirit with concentration on Me thus inspiring all others to do the same
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकत करयमणन गण करमण सरवश अहङकरवमढतम करतऽहमत मनयत 327
Translations:
1. Actions are being performed in every way by the Gunas of Prakrti he whose nature is deluded by egoism however thinks I am the doer
2. While actions are being done in every way by the gunas qualities of Nature one who is deluded by egoism thinks I am the doer
3. All actions are wrought in all cases by the qualities of Nature alone He whose mind is deluded by egoism thinks I am the doer
4. The actions are performed part by part by the strands of Prakriti yet the person having his self mind deluded with egoity imagines I am the sole doer
5. Action is the product of the qualities inherent in nature It is only the ignorant man who misled by personal egotism says I am the doer
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तततववतत महबह गणकरमवभगय गण गणष वरतनत इत मतव न सजजत 328
Translations:
1. But he who knows the truth about the division of the Gunas and works O mightyarmed one through his knowledge that the Gunas operate on their own products is not attached
2. But O mightyarmed one the one who is a knower of the facts about the varieties of the gunas and actions does not become attached thinking thus The organs act on the objects of the organs
3. But he who knows the Truth O mightyarmed Arjuna about the divisions of the qualities and their functions knowing that the Gunas as senses move amidst the Gunas as the senseobjects is not attached
4. But O mightyarmed one the one who knows the true nature of the strands and their respective divisions of work realizes The strands are in their respective purposes And thus he is not attached

5. But he O Mighty One who correctly understands the relationship between the Qualities and action is not attached to the act for he perceives that it is merely the action and reaction of the Qualities among themselves
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकतरगणसममढ सजजनत गणकरमस तनकतसनवद मनदनकतसनवनन वचलयत 329
Translations:
1. Those who are deluded by the Gunas of Prakrti are attached to the works of the Gunas But he who knows the whole truth should not disturb the ignorant who do not know the whole truth
2. Those who are completely deluded by the gunas of Nature become attached to the activities of the gunas The one who knows the All should not disturb those of dull intellect who do not know the All
3. Those deluded by the qualities of Nature are attached to the functions of the qualities The man of perfect knowledge should not unsettle the foolish one who is of imperfect knowledge
4. Men completely deluded by the strands of Prakrti are attached to the actions of those strands Those who know fully should not confuse them whereas the dullards who do not know fully
5. Those who do not understand the qualities are interested in the act however the wise man who knows the truth should not disturb the mind of those who do not
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मय सरवण करमण सनयसयधयतमचतस नरशरनरमम भतव यधयसव वगतजवर 330
Translations:
1. Surrender all your actions to Me with a mind focused on the Self free from desire and selfishness and fight with the heat of excitement abated
2. Free from the fever of the soul dedicate all actions to Me with your mind intent on the Self and become free from expectations and egoism and engage in battle
3. Renouncing all actions in Me with the mind centered on the Self free from hope and egoism and from mental fever fight thou

4. Renouncing all actions in Me with a mind that concentrates on the Self being free from the act of resting and from the sense of possession and consequently being free from mental fever you should fight
5. Therefore surrender your actions to Me keep your thoughts focused on the Absolute free from selfishness and without expecting a reward with a mind free from excitement begin to fight
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य म मतमद नतयमनतषठनत मनव शरदधवनतऽनसयनत मचयनत तऽप करमभ 331
Translations:
1. Those men who full of faith ever practice this teaching of Mine and those who receive it without cavileven they are released from karma
2. Those who faithfully follow this teaching of Mine without cavil they too become freed from their actions
3. Those who constantly practice this teaching of Mine with faith and without caviling they too are freed from actions
4. Those who constantly follow this doctrine of Mine with faith and without finding fault are freed from the results of all actions
5. Those who always act in accordance with My precepts firmly in faith and without caviling they too are freed from the bondage of action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य तवतदभयसयनत ननतषठनत म मतम सरवजञनवमढसतनवदध नषटनचतस 332
Translations:
1. But those who slander it and those who do not practice this teaching of Mineknow them to be utterly senseless and devoid of all knowledge and thus lost

2. But those who finding fault where there is none do not follow My teaching know themwho are deluded about all knowledge concerning the qualified and the unqualified Brahman and who are devoid of discriminationto have gone to ruin
3. But those who criticize My teaching and do not practice it deprived of all knowledge and lacking discernment know them to be doomed to destruction
4. But those who finding fault do not follow this doctrine of Mine be sure that these men are highly deluded in all branches of knowledge and are lost and brainless
5. But they who ridicule My word and do not observe it are ignorant devoid of wisdom and blind They seek only their own destruction
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सदश चषटत सवसय परकतरजञनवनप परकत यनत भतन नगरह क करषयत 333
Translations:
1. Even the man of knowledge acts according to his nature all beings follow their own nature What will repression accomplish
2. Even a man of wisdom behaves according to his own nature They follow their nature What can restraint do
3. Even a wise man acts in accordance with his own nature beings will follow their nature what can restraint do
4. Even a man of knowledge acts in accordance with his own Prakriti the elements return to Prakriti what will restraint avail
5. Even the wise man acts in accordance with his nature indeed all creatures act according to their natures What is the use of compulsion then
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इनदरयसयनदरयसयरथ रगदवष वयवसथत तयरन वशमगचछतत हयसय परपनथन 334
Translations:
1. Each sense has a fixed attachment to and aversion for its corresponding object but one should not come under their sway for they are enemies
2. Attraction and repulsion are ordained with respect to the objects of all the organs One should not be swayed by these two as they are adversaries
3. Attachment and aversion for the objects of the senses abide in the senses let no one come under their sway for they are his enemies

4. For a man of worldly life there are clearly fixed likes and dislikes with regard to the objects of each of his sense organs These are the obstacles for him the wise would not come under their control
5. The love and hate that are aroused by the objects of sense arise from Nature do not yield to them as they only obstruct the path
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.35 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.35 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरयनसवधरम वगण परधरमतसवनषठतत सवधरम नधन शरय परधरम भयवह 335
Translations:
1. Better is ones own duty even if done poorly than the duty of another done well It is better to die while performing ones own duty the duty of another is full of fear
2. Ones own duty though defective is superior to anothers duty wellperformed Death is better while engaged in ones own duty anothers duty is full of fear
3. Better is ones own duty though devoid of merit than the duty of another well discharged Better is death in ones own duty the duty of another is fraught with fear
4. Better is ones own duty even if it lacks merit than the wellperformed duty of another it is better to suffer ruin in ones own duty than to gain success in anothers

5. It is better to do ones own duty however lacking in merit than to do that of another even though efficiently It is better to die doing ones own duty for doing the duty of another is fraught with danger
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.36 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 3.36 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथ कन परयकतऽय पप चरत परष अनचछननप वरषणय बलदव नयजत 336
Translations:
1. Arjuna said But O Krsna what impels one to commit sin even against their own will as if constrained by force
2. Arjuna said Now then O scion of the Vrsni dynasty Krsna what compels this man to commit sin even against his will as if he were constrained by force
3. Arjuna said But what compels man to commit sin even against his wishes O Varshneya Krishna as if constrained by force
4. Arjuna said Then what induces this person of the world to commit sin even though they do not desire it as if they are being overpowered by a force
5. Arjuna asked My Lord what is it that drives a person to sin even against their will and as if by compulsion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.37 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.37 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कम एष करध एष रजगणसमदभव महशन महपपम वदधयनमह वरणम 337
Translations:
1. The Lord said It is desire it is wrath born of the guna of rajas it is a great devourer an impeller of sin Know this to be the enemy here
2. The Blessed Lord said This desire this anger born of the quality of rajas is a great devourer a great source of sin Know this to be the enemy here
3. The Blessed Lord said It is desire and it is anger both of the quality of Rajas alldevouring and allsinful know this as the foe here in this world
4. The Bhagavat said This desire this wrath born of the Rajasstrand is a swallower of festivity and a mighty bestower of sins Know this to be the enemy here
5. Lord Shri Krishna It is desire it is aversion born of passion Desire consumes and corrupts all things It is the greatest enemy of man
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.38 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.38 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धमनवरयत वहनरयथऽऽदरश मलन च यथलबनवत गरभसतथ तनदमवतम 338
Translations:
1. As a fire is enveloped by smoke as a mirror is covered by dust and as an embryo is encased in the amniotic sac so is this world enveloped by desire
2. As fire is enveloped by smoke as a mirror is obscured by dirt and as a fetus remains enclosed in the womb so is this shrouded by that
3. As fire is enveloped by smoke as a mirror is covered by dust and as an embryo is surrounded by the amniotic sac so is this enveloped by that
4. As fire is concealed by smoke a mirror by dirt and an embryo by its membranecover so He is concealed by this foe
5. As fire is shrouded by smoke a mirror is covered by dust and a child is enveloped by the womb so is the universe enveloped in desire
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.39 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.39 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आवत जञनमतन जञनन नतयवरण कमरपण कनतय दषपरणनलन च 339
Translations:
1. The knowledge of the intelligent self is enveloped by this constant enemy O Arjuna which is of the nature of desire and is difficult to gratify and insatiable
2. O son of Kunti knowledge is covered by this constant enemy of the wise in the form of desire which is an insatiable fire
3. O Arjuna wisdom is enveloped by this constant enemy of the wise in the form of desire which is insatiable like fire
4. O son of Kunti The knowledge of the wise is concealed by this eternal foe which appears desirable yet is insatiable like fire
5. It is the wise mans constant enemy it tarnishes the face of wisdom being as insatiable as a flame of fire
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.40 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इनदरयण मन बदधरसयधषठनमचयत एतरवमहयतयष जञनमवतय दहनम 340
Translations:
1. The senses the mind and the intellect are said to be its instruments by these it overpowers the embodied self enveloping its knowledge
2. The organs mind and intellect are said to be its abode This one diversely deludes the embodied being veiling Knowledge with the help of these
3. The senses the mind and the intellect are said to be its seat through these it deludes the embodied one veiling their wisdom
4. It is said to be based on the senseorgans the mind and the intellect With these it deludes the embodied by concealing knowledge
5. It works through the senses the mind and the reason and with their help it destroys wisdom and confounds the soul
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.41 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.41 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमततवमनदरयणयद नयमय भरतरषभ पपमन परजह हयन जञनवजञननशनम 341
Translations:
1. Therefore O Arjuna control your senses from the outset and slay this sinful thing that destroys both knowledge and discernment
2. Therefore O scion of the Bharata dynasty after first controlling the organs completely renounce this one which is sinful and a destroyer of learning and wisdom
3. Therefore O best of the Bharatas control your senses first and then kill this sinful thing which destroys knowledge and realization
4. Therefore O best among the Bharatas you must avoid this sinful one destroying knowledge and action by controlling completely the senseorgans in the beginning itself
5. Therefore O Arjuna first control your senses and then slay desire for it is full of sin and is the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.42 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इनदरयण परणयहरनदरयभय पर मन मनससत पर बदधरय बदध परतसत स 342
Translations:
1. They say that the senses are high the mind is higher than the senses the intellect is higher than the mind but what is greater than the intellect is that desire
2. They say that the organs are superior to the gross body the mind is superior to the organs and the intellect is superior to the mind However He is superior to the intellect
3. They say that the senses are superior to the body the mind is superior to the senses the intellect is superior to the mind and He the Self is superior even to the intellect
4. It is said that the senseorgans are different from their objects the mind is different from the senseorgans the intellect is different from the mind and That Self is different from the intellect
5. It is said that the senses are powerful however beyond the senses is the mind beyond the mind is the intellect and greater than the intellect is He
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 3.43 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 3.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एव बदध पर बदधव ससतभयतमनमतमन जह शतर महबह कमरप दरसदम 343
Translations:

1. Thus knowing that which is higher than the intellect and fixing the mind with the help of the intellect in Karma Yoga O Arjuna slay this enemy which takes the form of desire and which is difficult to overcome

2. The Ast introduces this verse with Tatah kim what follows from thatTr Understanding the Self thus as superior to the intellect and completely establishing the Self through spiritual absorption with the help of the mind O mightyarmed one the enemy in the form of desire which is difficult to subdue vanishes
3. Thus knowing Him who is superior to the intellect and restraining the self by the Self slay thou O mightyarmed Arjuna the enemy in the form of desire hard to conquer
4. Thus being conscious that it is different from the intellect and steadying the self with the self kill the foe that is of the form of desire and that is hard to approach
5. Thus O MightyArmed One knowing Him to be beyond the intellect and with His help subdue your personal ego and kill your enemy Desire even though it is extremely difficult
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इम ववसवत यग परकतवनहमवययम ववसवन मनव परह मनरकषवकवऽबरवत 41
Translations:
1. The Lord said I taught this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan Vivasvan taught it to Manu Manu declared it to Iksvaku
2. The Blessed Lord said I imparted this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan who then taught it to Manu who in turn transmitted it to Iksavaku
3. The Blessed Lord said I taught this imperishable Yoga to Vivasvan he then told it to Manu Manu proclaimed it to Ikshvaku
4. The Bhagavad said This changeless Yoga I had properly taught thus to Vivasvat Vivasvat correctly told it to Manu and Manu declared it to Iksvaku
5. Lord Shri Krishna said I taught this imperishable philosophy to Vivaswān the founder of the Sun dynasty Vivaswān gave it to Manu the lawgiver and Manu to King Ikshvāku
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एव परमपरपरपतमम रजरषय वद स कलनह महत यग नषट परनतप 42
Translations:
1. Thus handed down in succession the royal sages knew this Karma Yoga But with a long lapse of time O Arjuna that Yoga was lost to the world
2. The kingsages knew this yoga which was received in regular succession That yoga O destroyer of foes is now lost due to a long lapse of time
3. This handed down in regular succession by the royal sages was known This Yoga however has been lost here over time O Parantapa burner of the foes
4. Thus the regal sages knew this which was received in regular succession Over time however this Yoga has been lost O scorcher of enemies
5. The Divine Kings knew it for it was their tradition then after a long time it was at last forgotten
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: स एवय मय तऽदय यग परकत परतन भकतऽस म सख चत रहसय हयतदततमम 43
Translations:
1. It is the same ancient Yoga that I am now teaching you for you are My devotee and My friend For this is a supreme mystery
2. That ancient Yoga itself which is this has been taught to you by Me today considering you to be My devotee and friend for this Yoga is a profound secret
3. That same ancient yoga has been today taught to you by me for you are my devotee and my friend it is the supreme secret
4. The same ancient Yoga has now been taught to you by Me on the grounds that you are both My devotee and friend This is the highest secret
5. It is the same ancient path that I have now revealed to you for you are my devotee and my friend It is the supreme secret
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.4 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 4.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अपर भवत जनम पर जनम ववसवत कथमतदवजनय तवमद परकतवनत 44
Translations:
1. Arjuna said Your birth was later and Vivasvans birth was earlier How then can I understand that you taught it in the beginning
2. Arjuna said Your birth was later whereas Vivasvans birth was earlier How am I to understand that You instructed him in the beginning
3. Arjuna said Later was Thy birth and prior to it was the birth of Vivasvan the Sun how am I to understand that Thou hast taught this Yoga from the beginning
4. Arjuna said Your birth is later while the birth of Vivasvat is earlier how am I then to understand that You had properly taught him this in the beginning
5. Arjuna asked My Lord Vyasawana was born before You how then can You have revealed it to him
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बहन म वयततन जनमन तव चरजन तनयह वद सरवण न तव वतथ परनतप 45
Translations:
1. The Lord said Many of My births have passed O Arjuna as have yours I know them all but you do not know them
2. The Blessed Lord said O Arjuna many lives of Mine have passed and so have yours I know them all but you do not know O scorcher of enemies
3. The Blessed Lord said Many births of Mine have passed as well as of thine O Arjuna I know them all but thou knowest not O Parantapa scorcher of foes
4. The Bhagavat said O Arjuna many births of Mine as well as yours have passed All of them I know but you do not O scorcher of foes
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied I have been born again and again from time to time thou too O Arjuna My births are known to me but yours are unknown to you
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अजऽप सननवययतम भतनमशवरऽप सन परकत सवमधषठय सभवमयतममयय 46
Translations:
1. Though I am birthless and of immutable nature and the Lord of all beings yet by employing My own Nature Prakrti I am born out of My own free will
2. Though I am birthless and undecaying by nature and the Lord of beings yet by subjugating My Prakriti I take birth through My own Maya
3. Though I am unborn and of imperishable nature and though I am the Lord of all beings yet governing my own nature I am born by my own Maya
4. Though I am unborn and changeless Self though I am the Lord of all beings yet presiding over My own nature I take birth by My own trick of illusion
5. There is no beginning to Me Though I am imperishable and the Lord of all that exists I manifest Myself by My own will and power
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद यद ह धरमसय गलनरभवत भरत अभयतथनमधरमसय तदऽऽतमन सजमयहम 47
Translations:
1. Whenever there is a decline of Dharma O Arjuna and an uprising of Adharma then I incarnate myself
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty whenever there is a decline in virtue and an increase in vice then I manifest Myself
3. Whenever there is a decline of righteousness and an increase of unrighteousness O Arjuna then I manifest Myself
4. For whenever there is a decay of righteousness and the rise of unrighteousness then O descendant of Bharata I send forth that which the Self is unimportant
5. Whenever spirituality decays and materialism is rampant then O Arjuna I reincarnate myself
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परतरणय सधन वनशय च दषकतम धरमससथपनरथय सभवम यग यग 48
Translations:
1. For the protection of the good and the destruction of the wicked for the establishment of Dharma I am born from age to age
2. For the protection of the pious the destruction of the evildoers and the establishment of virtue I manifest myself in every age
3. For the protection of the good for the destruction of the wicked and for the establishment of righteousness I am born in every age
4. For the protection of the good people and for the destruction of evildoers and for the purpose of firmly establishing righteousness I take birth in every age
5. To protect the righteous to destroy the wicked and to establish the kingdom of God I am reborn from age to age
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जनम करम च म दवयमव य वतत तततवत तयकतव दह पनरजनम नत ममत सऽरजन 49
Translations:
1. He who thus knows in truth My divine birth and actions does not get rebirth after leaving the body he will come to Me O Arjuna
2. He who thus knows truly the divine birth and actions of Mine does not get rebirth after casting off the body He attains Me O Arjuna
3. He who thus knows in their true light My divine birth and actions having abandoned the body is not born again he comes to Me O Arjuna
4. Whoever knows correctly the divine birth and action of Mine he upon abandoning the body does not go to rebirth but goes to Me O Arjuna
5. He who realizes the divine truth concerning My birth and life does not take birth again and when he leaves his body he becomes one with Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वतरगभयकरध मनमय ममपशरत बहव जञनतपस पत मदभवमगत 410
Translations:
1. Freed from desire fear and wrath absorbed in Me depending upon Me purified by the austerity of knowledge many have attained My state
2. Many who were devoid of attachment fear and anger who were absorbed in Me who had taken refuge in Me and were purified by the austerity of knowledge have attained My state
3. Freed from attachment fear and anger absorbed in Me taking refuge in Me purified by the fire of knowledge many have attained My Being
4. Many persons who are free from passion fear and anger are full of Me take refuge in Me and have become pure through the austerity of wisdomthey have come to My being
5. Many have merged their existences in Mine being freed from desire fear and anger always filled with Me and purified by the illuminating flame of selfabnegation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य यथ म परपदयनत तसतथव भजमयहम मम वरतमनवरतनत मनषय परथ सरवश 411
Translations:
1. Whoever resorts to Me in any manner I favor them in the same manner men experience Me alone in different ways O Arjuna
2. According to the manner in which they approach Me I favor them in that same manner O son of Partha humans follow My path in every way
3. In whatever way men approach Me even so do I reward them My path do men tread in all ways O Arjuna
4. The way in which people resort to Me in the same way I favor them O son of Prtha all kinds of people follow My path
5. However men try to worship Me I welcome them Whatever path they take it leads to Me in the end
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कङकषनत करमण सदध यजनत इह दवत कषपर ह मनष लक सदधरभवत करमज 412
Translations:
1. Those who desire the fruits of their ritualistic acts sacrifice to the gods here for success quickly accrues in the world of men
2. Longing for the fruition of their actions of their rites and duties they worship the gods here For in the human world success from action comes quickly
3. Those who long for success in action in this world sacrifice to the gods for success is quickly attained by men through action
4. Those who desire success in their actions perform sacrifices intending them for the deities For the success born of ritualistic actions is quick in the world of men
5. Those who seek success worship the Powers and their actions in this world bear immediate fruit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: चतरवरणय मय सषट गणकरमवभगश तसय करतरमप म वदधयकरतरमवययम 413
Translations:
1. The system of four stations was created by Me according to the distinction of Gunas and Karma Though I am their creator know Me to be nonagent and immutable
2. The four castes have been created by Me through a classification of the gunas and duties Even though I am the agent of that act of classification still know Me to be nonagentive and changeless
3. The fourfold caste has been created by Me according to the differentiation of Guna and Karma though I am the author of it know Me as nondoer and immutable
4. I have created the fourfold caste structure according to the division of their respective qualities and actions Though I am the creator of this know Me as the changeless noncreator

5. The four divisions of societythe wise the soldier the merchant and the laborerwere created by Me according to the natural distribution of qualities and instincts I am the author of them though I Myself do not act and am changeless
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न म करमण लमपनत न म करमफल सपह इत म यऽभजनत करमभरन स बधयत 414
Translations:
1. Works cannot contaminate Me There is no desire for the fruits of actions in Me He who understands this is not bound by actions
2. Actions do not taint Me for I have no hankering for the results of actions One who knows Me thus does not become bound by their actions
3. Actions do not taint Me nor do I have a desire for the fruit of actions He who knows Me thus is not bound by actions
4. Actions do not stain me nor do I have a desire for the fruits of them either Whoever understands me in this way is not bound by their actions
5. My actions do not bind Me nor do I desire anything that they can bring He who thus realizes Me is not enslaved by action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एव जञतव कत करम परवरप ममकषभ कर करमव तसमततव परव परवतर कतम 415
Translations:
1. Knowing thus even ancient seekers of liberation did their work Therefore do your work only as the ancients did in olden times
2. Thus having known even the ancient seekers of liberation performed their duties Therefore you should undertake action as was performed earlier by the ancients
3. Having known this the ancient seekers of freedom also performed action therefore do thou also perform action as the ancients did in days of yore
4. Realizing in this fashion ancient seekers of salvation also undertook action Hence you too should by all means perform the action that was performed by the ancients
5. In the light of wisdom our ancestors who sought deliverance performed their acts Act thou also as our fathers of old did
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: क करम कमकरमत कवयऽपयतर महत ततत करम परवकषयम यजजञतव मकषयसऽशभत 416
Translations:
1. What is action What is nonaction Even the wise are puzzled in respect of these I shall declare to you that kind of action knowing which you will be freed from evil
2. Even the intelligent are confounded as to what is action and what is inaction I shall tell you of that action knowing which you will become free from evil

3. What is action What is inaction Even the wise are confused about this Therefore I shall teach you the nature of action and inaction by knowing which you will be liberated from the evil of Samsara the wheel of birth and death
4. Even the wise are perplexed about what is action and what is nonaction I shall properly teach you the action by knowing which you will be freed from evil
5. What is action and what is inaction This is a question that has bewildered the wise But I will declare to you the philosophy of action and knowing it you will be free from evil
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमण हयप बदधवय बदधवय च वकरमण अकरमणशच बदधवय गहन करमण गत 417
Translations:

1. For there is what ought to be known in action likewise there is what ought to be known in multiform action and there is what ought to be understood in nonaction Thus mysterious is the way of action
2. For there is something to be known even about action and something to be known about prohibited action and something needs to be known about inaction The true nature of action is inscrutable
3. For verily the true nature of action enjoined by the scriptures should be known as well as that of forbidden or unlawful action and of inaction the nature of action is hard to understand

4. Something has to be understood about good action something has to be understood about wrong action and something has to be understood about nonaction It is difficult to comprehend the way of action
5. It is necessary to consider what is right action what is wrong action and what is inaction for the law of action is mysterious
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमणयकरम य पशयदकरमण च करम य स बदधमन मनषयष स यकत कतसनकरमकत 418
Translations:
1. He who sees inaction in action and also action in inaction is wise among men He is fit for release and has accomplished all actions
2. He who finds inaction in action and action in inaction is the wise one possessed of the knowledge of Brahman among men they are engaged in yoga and are performers of all actions
3. He who sees inaction in action and action in inaction he is wise among men he is a yogi and performer of all actions
4. He who finds nonaction in action and action in nonaction is an intelligent one among men and is said to be a performer or destroyer of all actions
5. He who can see inaction in action and action in inaction is the wisest among men He is a saint even though he still acts
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसय सरव समरमभ कमसङकलपवरजत जञनगनदगधकरमण तमह पणडत बध 419
Translations:
1. He whose every undertaking is free from desire and delusive identification of the body with the self whose actions are burned up in the fire of knowledgethe wise describe him as a sage
2. The wise call him learned whose actions are all devoid of desires and their thoughts and whose actions have been burned away by the fire of wisdom
3. He whose undertakings are all devoid of desires and selfish purposes and whose actions have been burned by the fire of knowledge the wise call him a sage
4. He whose every exertion is devoid of intention for desirable objects and whose actions are burnt up by the fire of wisdomthe wise call such a person a man of learning
5. The wise call him a sage for whatever he undertakes is free from the motive of desire and his deeds are purified by the fire of wisdom
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तयकतव करमफलसङग नतयतपत नरशरय करमणयभपरवततऽप नव कञचतकरत स 420
Translations:
1. Having renounced attachment to the fruits of their actions ever contented with the eternal self and dependent on none one does not act at all even while being engaged in action
2. Having given up attachment to the results of action he who is evercontent dependent on nothing does not really do anything even though he is engaged in action
3. Having abandoned attachment to the fruits of the action ever content depending on nothing he does not do anything even while being engaged in activity
4. By abandoning attachment to the fruits of actions remaining ever content and depending on nothing that person even though they are engaged in action does not perform anything at all
5. Having surrendered all claim to the results of his actions he is always contented and independent in reality he does nothing even though he appears to be acting
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नरशरयतचतततम तयकतसरवपरगरह शरर कवल करम करवननपनत कलबषम 421
Translations:
1. Free from desire his intellect and mind controlled having given up all possessions and doing bodily work only he is not subject to evil
2. One who is without solicitation who has the mind and senses under control and is totally without possessions incurs no sin by performing actions merely for the maintenance of the body
3. Without hope controlling the mind and the self having abandoned all covetousness and performing only bodily actions one incurs no sin
4. Having rid of cravings controlling the mind and body abandoning all sense of possession and performing actions with the body alone one does not incur any sin
5. Expecting nothing his mind and personality controlled without greed doing only bodily actions though he acts he remains untainted
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यदचछलभसनतषट दवनदवतत वमतसर सम सदधवसदध च कतवप न नबधयत 422
Translations:
1. Content with what chance may bring rising above the pairs of opposites free from illwill evenminded in success and failure he acts yet is not bound
2. Remaining satisfied with what comes unasked for having transcended the dualities being free from spite and balanced under success and failure he is not bound even by performing actions
3. Content with what comes to him without effort free from the pairs of opposites and envy evenminded in success and failure he acts yet is not bound

4. Remaining content with the gain brought by chance transcending the dualities pairs of opposites entertaining no jealousy and remaining equal in success and in failure he does not get bound even when he acts

5. Content with what comes to him without any effort of his own rising above the pairs of opposites free from envy his mind balanced in both success and failure though he acts the consequences do not bind him
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 4.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 4.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: गतसङगसय मकतसय जञनवसथतचतस यजञयचरत करम समगर परवलयत 423
Translations:
1. One whose attachments have been relinquished who is liberated whose mind is established in knowledge and who works only for sacrificestheir Karma is entirely dissolved
2. The liberated person who has rid themselves of attachment and whose mind is fixed in knowledge has their actions undertaken for a sacrifice completely destroyed
