5. Supreme bliss is the lot of the sage whose mind attains peace whose passions subside who is without sin and who becomes one with the Absolute
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यञजननव सदऽऽतमन यग वगतकलमष सखन बरहमससपरशमतयनत सखमशनत 628
Translations:
1. Thus devoting himself to the Yoga of the Self freed from impurities the yogin easily attains the supreme bliss of contact with the Brahman
2. By constantly concentrating his mind in this way the taintless yogi easily attains the absolute bliss of contact with Brahman
3. The yogi always engaging the mind thus in the practice of yoga is freed from sins and easily enjoys the infinite bliss of contact with Brahman the Eternal
4. Thus yoking the self always the man of Yoga with a subdued mind easily attains complete union that is the Brahman
5. Thus free from sin abiding always in the Eternal the saint enjoys without effort the bliss that flows from the realization of the Infinite
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवभतसथमतमन सरवभतन चतमन ईकषत यगयकततम सरवतर समदरशन 629
Translations:
1. He whose mind is fixed in Yoga sees reality everywhere he sees his Self as abiding in all beings and all beings in his Self
2. One who has their mind Selfabsorbed through Yoga and who has the vision of sameness everywhere sees this Self existing in everything and everything in their Self
3. With the mind harmonized by Yoga he sees the Self abiding in all beings and all beings in the Self he sees the same everywhere
4. He who has yoked himself in Yoga and observes everything truly perceives the Self to be abiding in all beings and all beings to be abiding in the Self
5. He who experiences the unity of life sees his own Self in all beings and all beings in his own Self and looks upon everything with an impartial eye
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य म पशयत सरवतर सरव च मय पशयत तसयह न परणशयम स च म न परणशयत 630
Translations:
1. To him who sees Me in every self and every self in MeI am not lost to him nor is he lost to Me
2. One who sees Me in everything and sees all things in MeI do not lose sight of him and he also is not lost to My vision
3. He who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me never becomes separated from Me nor do I from him
4. He who observes Me in all and observes all in Mefor him I am not lost and he too is not lost for me
5. He who sees Me in everything and everything in Me I shall never forsake him nor shall he lose Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवभतसथत य म भजतयकतवमसथत सरवथ वरतमनऽप स यग मय वरतत 631
Translations:
1. The yogi who fixed in oneness worships Me dwelling in all beingshe abides in Me no matter how he may live
2. That yogi who being established in unity adores Me as existing in all things exists in Mein whatever condition he may be
3. He who being established in unity worships Me who dwells in all beings that yogi abides in Me whatever their mode of living may be
4. He who is firmly established in the oneness of Me experiences Me immanent in all beingsthat man of Yoga is never stained no matter what stage he may be in
5. The sage who realizes the unity of life and who worships Me in all beings lives in Me no matter what their lot may be
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आतमपमयन सरवतर सम पशयत यऽरजन सख व यद व दख स यग परम मत 632
Translations:
1. He who due to the similarity of selves everywhere sees pleasure or pain as the same everywherethat yogi O Arjuna is considered the closest
2. O Arjuna that yogi is considered the best who judges what is happiness and sorrow in all beings by the same standard as they would apply to themselves
3. He who through the likeness of the Self O Arjuna sees reality everywhere be it pleasure or pain is regarded as the highest Yogi
4. Whoever finds pleasure or pain equally in all as if it were in themselvesthat person is considered to be a great man of Yoga O Arjuna
5. O Arjuna He is the perfect saint who having been taught by the likeness within himself sees the same Self everywhere regardless of whether the outer form be pleasurable or painful
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.33 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 6.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यऽय यगसतवय परकत समयन मधसदन एतसयह न पशयम चञचलतवत सथत सथरम 633
Translations:
1. Arjuna said O Krsna this Yoga of reality which You have declared I do not see how it can be steady due to the fickleness of the mind
2. Arjuna said O Madhusudana Krsna this Yoga that You have spoken of as sameness I do not see its steady continuance due to the restlessness of the mind
3. Arjuna said O Krishna I do not see how this Yoga of equanimity which you have taught me can be maintained steadily due to the restlessness of the mind
4. Arjuna said O slayer of Mandhu this Yoga of equanimity which You have spoken of I do not find a proper foundation for it due to the unsteadiness of the mind
5. Arjuna said I do not see how I can attain this state of equanimity which You have revealed owing to the restlessness of my mind
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.34 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 6.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: चञचल ह मन कषण परमथ बलवददढम तसयह नगरह मनय वयरव सदषकरम 634
Translations:
1. For the mind is fickle O Krsna impetuous powerful and stubborn I think that restraining it is as difficult as restraining the wind
2. For O Kṛṣṇa the mind is unsteady turbulent strong and obstinate I consider its control to be as difficult as that of the wind
3. The mind is indeed restless turbulent strong and unyielding O Krishna I consider it as difficult to control as controlling the wind
4. O Krsna The mind is indeed unsteady destructive strong and obstinate I believe it is very difficult to control it just as it is to control the wind
5. My Lord Verily the mind is fickle and turbulent obstinate and strong indeed it is extremely difficult to control like the wind
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.35 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.35 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: असशय महबह मन दरनगरह चल अभयसन त कनतय वरगयण च गहयत 635
Translations:
1. The Lord said The mind is indeed hard to subdue and fickle O mightyarmed one but O son of Kunti it can be brought under control through practice and the exercise of dispassion
2. The Blessed Lord said O mightyarmed one undoubtedly the mind is untamable and restless But O son of Kunti it can be brought under control through practice and detachment
3. The Blessed Lord said Undoubtedly O mightyarmed Arjuna the mind is difficult to control and restless but with practice and dispassion it can be restrained
4. The Bhagavat said O mightyarmed one No doubt the mind is unsteady and hard to control But it can be controlled through practice and an attitude of desirelessness O son of Kunti
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied Doubtless O Mighty One the mind is fickle and exceedingly difficult to restrain however O Son of Kunti with practice and renunciation it can be done
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.36 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.36 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: असयततमन यग दषपरप इत म मत वशयतमन त यतत शकयऽवपतमपयत 636
Translations:
1. In my opinion Yoga is hard to attain by a person of an unrestrained mind however it can be attained through the right means by one who strives for it and has a subdued mind
2. My conviction is that Yoga is difficult to attain for one with an uncontrolled mind But it is possible to attain through the aforementioned means for one who strives and has a controlled mind
3. I think Yoga is hard to be attained by one with an uncontrolled self but the selfcontrolled and striving one can attain it by the appropriate means
4. My belief is that attaining Yoga is difficult for a person with an uncontrolled mind but it is possible to attain by proper means with a subdued self
5. It is not possible to attain SelfRealization unless one knows how to control oneself however for one who striving by the proper means learns such control it is possible
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.37 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 6.37 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अयत शरदधयपत यगचचलतमनस अपरपय यगससदध क गत कषण गचछत 637
Translations:
1. Arjuna said If a person who is possessed of faith but has put in only inadequate effort finds his mind wandering away from Yoga and then fails to attain perfectionwhat way does he go O Krishna
2. Arjuna said O Krsna what goal does one attain who despite having faith fails to achieve perfection in Yoga due to lack of diligence and whose mind becomes deflected from Yoga
3. Arjuna said He who is unable to control himself even though he has faith and whose mind wanders away from Yoga what end does he meet having failed to attain perfection in Yoga O Krishna

4. Arjuna said A person who has faith and is desirous of reaching the path of the good but whose mind has been severed from Yogato which goal does he go having failed to attain success in Yoga O Krsna
5. Arjuna asked What of him who fails to control himself whose mind falls from spiritual contemplation yet retains his faith and does not attain perfection
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.38 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 6.38 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कचचननभयवभरषटशछननभरमव नशयत अपरतषठ महबह वमढ बरहमण पथ 638
Translations:
1. Without any support confused in the path leading to the Brahman and thus having fallen from both does he not perish O mightyarmed one like a rent cloud
2. O Mightyarmed one having fallen from both paths without support deluded on the path to Brahman does he not get ruined like a scattered cloud
3. Fallen from both does he not perish like a rent cloud supportless O mightyarmed one deluded on the path of Brahman
4. Does he fallen from both get lost like a broken cloud Or O mightyarmed one Having no support does he meet total destruction
5. Having failed in both my Lord is he without hope like a riven cloud with no support lost on the spiritual path
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.39 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 6.39 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एतनम सशय कषण छततमरहसयशषत तवदनय सशयसयसय छतत न हयपपदयत 639
Translations:
1. O Krsna you should altogether remove this doubt of mine for there is no other remover of this doubt than you
2. O Krsna you should totally eradicate this doubt of mine for none other than yourself can be the dispeller of this doubt
3. O Krishna please completely dispel this doubt of mine for it is not possible for anyone but You to do so
4. Please dispel this doubt of mine completely But for You O Krsna no one else is capable of eradicating this doubt
5. My Lord You are worthy to solve this doubt once and for all there is no one else competent to do so save Yourself
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.40 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परथ नवह नमतर वनशसतसय वदयत नह कलयणकतकशचददरगत तत गचछत 640
Translations:
1. The Lord said Neither here in this world nor there in the next is there destruction for him For no one who does good ever comes to an evil end
2. The Blessed Lord said O Partha there is certainly no ruin for him here or hereafter for no one engaged in good meets with a deplorable end my son
3. The Blessed Lord said O Arjuna neither in this world nor in the next will there be destruction for him none indeed who does good O my son ever comes to grief
4. The Bhagavat said O dear Partha Neither in this world nor in the other is there destruction for him Certainly no one who performs an auspicious act ever comes to a grievous state
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied My beloved child There is no destruction for him either in this world or the next No evil fate awaits him who treads the path of righteousness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.41 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.41 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परपय पणयकत लकनषतव शशवत सम शचन शरमत गह यगभरषटऽभजयत 641
Translations:
1. He who has fallen away from Yoga is born again in the house of the pure and prosperous having attained to the worlds of doers of good deeds and dwelled there for many long years
2. Attaining the worlds of the righteous and residing there for eternal years the man who has fallen from Yoga is born in the house of the pious and prosperous
3. Having attained to the worlds of the righteous and having dwelt there for everlasting years he who fell from Yoga is born in a house of the pure and wealthy
4. Having attained the worlds of those who perform pious acts and having resided there for years of eternity the one who has fallen from Yoga is born again in the house of the pure and wealthy

5. Having reached the worlds where the righteous dwell and having remained there for many years he who has strayed from the path of spirituality will be born again into the family of the pure benevolent and prosperous
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.42 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथव यगनमव कल भवत धमतम एतदध दरलभतर लक जनम यददशम 642
Translations:
1. Or he is born into a family of wise yogis But such a birth in this world is rare
2. Or he is born in the family of wise yogis Such a birth is indeed more difficult to come by in the world
3. Or he is born in a family of even the wisest of yogis verily such a birth is very difficult to obtain in this world
4. Or he is born nowhere else but in the family of the intelligent men of Yoga for this birth is more difficult to obtain in the world
5. Or he may be born in the family of wise sages though such a birth is indeed very difficult to obtain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.43 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ततर त बदधसयग लभत परवदहकम यतत च तत भय ससदध करननदन 643
Translations:
1. There O Arjuna he regains the disposition of mind that he had in his former body and from there he strives even more for success in Yoga
2. There he becomes endowed with the wisdom acquired in the previous body and he strives even more than before for perfection O scion of the Kuru dynasty
3. Then he comes into contact with the knowledge acquired in his former body and strives even more for perfection O Arjuna
4. There in that life he regains the link of mentality transmitted from his former body Consequently once again he strives for full success O rejoicer of the Kurus
5. Then the experience acquired in his former life will revive and with it he will more eagerly strive for perfection than before
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.44 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.44 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परवभयसन तनव हरयत हयवशऽप स जजञसरप यगसय शबदबरहमतवरतत 644
Translations:
1. By the power of his earlier practice he is carried forward even against his will Even though he is an enquirer about Yoga he transcends the SabdaBrahman ie Prakṛti or matter
2. For by that very past practice he is carried forward even in spite of himself Even a seeker of Yoga transcends the results of the performance of Vedic rituals
3. By that same former practice he is borne on in spite of himself Even he who merely wishes to know Yoga goes beyond the Brahmanic word
4. Not being a master of himself he is only dragged by that former practice its mental impression only a seeker of the knowledge of Yoga passes beyond what strengthens the sacred text
5. Unconsciously he will return to the practices of his old life thus one who strives to attain spiritual consciousness is certainly superior to one who merely talks about it
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.45 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.45 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परयतनदयतमनसत यग सशदधकलबष अनकजनमससदधसतत यत पर गतम 645
Translations:
1. But the yogi striving earnestly cleansed of all his stains and perfected through many births reaches the supreme state
2. However the yogi applying himself assiduously becoming purified from sin and attaining perfection through many births they achieve the highest Goal
3. But the Yogi who strives assiduously purified of sins and perfected gradually over many births reaches the highest goal
4. After that the assiduous man of Yoga having his sins completely cleansed and being perfected through many births reaches the Supreme Goal
5. Then after many lives of earnest striving and absolution of sins the student of spirituality attains perfection and reaches the Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.46 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.46 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तपसवभयऽधक यग जञनभयऽप मतऽधक करमभयशचधक यग तसमदयग भवरजन 646
Translations:
1. Greater than the ascetics greater than those who possess knowledge greater than the ritualists is the Yogin Therefore O Arjuna become a Yogin
2. A yogi is higher than those of austerity they are considered higher even than those of knowledge The yogi is also higher than those of action Therefore O Arjuna become a yogi

3. The yogi is thought to be superior to the ascetics even superior to those who have knowledge obtained through the study of scriptures he is also superior to men of action therefore be thou a yogi O Arjuna

4. The person of Yoga is superior to the persons of austerities and is considered superior even to the persons of knowledge and the person of Yoga is superior to the persons of action Therefore O Arjuna you should become a person of Yoga
5. The wise person is superior to the ascetic the scholar and the man of action therefore be thou a wise person O Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 6.47 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 6.47 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यगनमप सरवष मदगतननतरतमन शरदधवनभजत य म स म यकततम मत 647
Translations:
1. He who with faith worships Me and whose innermost self is fixed in Me I consider him to be the greatest of the Yogins
2. Even among all the yogis he who adores Me with his mind fixed on Me and with faith is considered by Me to be the best of the yogis
3. And among all the Yogis he who full of faith and with his inner self merged in Me worships Me is deemed by Me to be the most devoted
4. He who has faith and serves Me with his inner self devoted to Me is considered by Me to be the best of all yogis
5. I look upon him as the best of mystics who full of faith worships Me and abides in Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मययसकतमन परथ यग यञजनमदशरय असशय समगर म यथ जञसयस तचछण 71
Translations:
1. The Lord said With your mind focused on Me taking Me as your support and practicing yogahow can you without doubt know Me fully Hear O Arjuna
2. The Blessed Lord said O Partha hear how with your mind fixed on Me practicing the Yoga of Meditation and taking refuge in Me you will certainly and fully know Me
3. The Blessed Lord said O Arjuna hear how you shall without doubt know Me fully with your mind intent on Me practicing Yoga and taking refuge in Me
4. The Bhagavat said O son of Prtha hear from Me how with your mind attached to Me practicing yoga and taking refuge in Me you shall understand Me fully without any doubt
5. Lord Shri Krishna said Listen O Arjuna I will tell you how you can know Me in my full perfection Practice meditation with your mind devoted to Me and take Me as your refuge
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जञन तऽह सवजञनमद वकषयमयशषत यजजञतव नह भयऽनयजजञतवयमवशषयत 72
Translations:
1. I will declare to you in full this knowledge of God along with the knowledge that makes it distinguished knowing which nothing else remains to be known
2. I shall tell you in detail of this Knowledge which is combined with realization after experiencing which there remains nothing else here to be known
3. I will declare to you in full this knowledge combined with realization after knowing which nothing else remains to be known here
4. I shall teach you this knowledge in full together with action for a person who has known this there remains nothing else to be known in this world
5. I will reveal this knowledge to you and how it can be realized once accomplished there is nothing else worth having in this life
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मनषयण सहसरष कशचदयतत सदधय यततमप सदधन कशचनम वतत तततवत 73
Translations:
1. Among thousands of men some strive for perfection even among those who strive for perfection only some know Me and among those who know Me only some truly know Me
2. Among thousands of men a rare one endeavors for perfection Even among the perfected ones who are diligent one perchance knows Me in truth
3. Among thousands of men one may perchance strive for perfection even among those successful strivers only one may perchance know Me in essence
4. Among thousands of men perhaps one makes an effort for the determined knowledge Among those even though they make an effort perhaps one realizes Me correctly with the determined knowledge
5. Among thousands of men scarcely one strives for perfection and even among those who gain occult powers perhaps only one knows me in truth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भमरपऽनल वय ख मन बदधरव च अहङकर इतय म भनन परकतरषटध 74
Translations:
1. Earth water fire air ether Manas Buddhi and the egosensethus My Prakriti is divided into eight parts
2. This Prakrti of Mine is divided eightfold thus earth water fire air space mind intellect and egoism
3. Earth water fire air ether mind intellect and egoismthus is My Nature divided eightfold
4. My nature is divided into eight parts Earth Water Fire Wind Ether Mind Intellect and Ego
5. Earth water fire air ether mind intellect and personalitythis is the eightfold division of My manifested nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अपरयमतसतवनय परकत वदध म परम जवभत महबह ययद धरयत जगत 75
Translations:
1. This is My lower Prakṛti But O mightyarmed one know that My higher nature is another It is the lifeprinciple Jīvabhūta by which this universe is sustained
2. O mightyarmed one this is the inferior Prakrti Know the other Prakrti of Mine which is however higher than this which has taken the form of individual souls and by which this world is upheld
3. O mightyarmed Arjuna this is the inferior Prakriti know it as distinct from My higher Prakriti the very lifeelement by which this world is upheld
4. This is My lower nature My superior nature which has become the individual Soul and by which this world is maintained is not different from this O mightyarmed Arjuna you must know this
5. This is My inferior nature however O valiant one know that distinct from this is My superior nature which is the very life that sustains the universe
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एतदयनन भतन सरवणतयपधरय अह कतसनसय जगत परभव परलयसतथ 76
Translations:
1. Know that all beings have these two as the source of their birth Therefore I am the origin and the dissolver of the whole universe
2. Understand thus that all thingssentient and insentienthave these as their source I am the origin as well as the end of the entire universe
3. Know that these two are the womb of all beings thus I am the source and dissolution of the whole universe
4. All beings are born from this womb hence keep them close I am the origin and dissolution of the entire world
5. It is the womb of all beings for I am He by Whom the worlds were created and shall be dissolved
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मतत परतर ननयतकञचदसत धनञजय मय सरवमद परत सतर मणगण इव 77
Translations:
1. There is nothing higher than Me O Arjuna All this is strung on Me like rows of gems on a thread
2. O Dhananjaya there is nothing else higher than Myself All this is strung on Me like pearls on a string
3. There is nothing higher than Me O Arjuna All this is strung on Me like clusters of gems on a string
4. There is nothing beyond Me O Dhananjaya all this is strung on Me just as pearls are strung on a string
5. O Arjuna There is nothing higher than Me all is strung on Me like rows of pearls on a thread
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रसऽहमपस कनतय परभसम शशसरयय परणव सरववदष शबद ख परष नष 78
Translations:
1. I am the taste in the waters O Arjuna I am the light in the sun and the moon the sacred syllable Om in all the Vedas sound in the ether and manhood in men I am
2. O son of Kunti I am the taste of water I am the effulgence of the moon and the sun the letter Om in all the Vedas the sound in space and manhood in men
3. I am the flavor in water O Arjuna I am the light in the moon and the sun I am the syllable Om in all the Vedas sound in the ether and virility in men
4. O son of Kunti I am the taste in waters the light in the moon and sun the best hymn OM in the entire Vedas the sound that exists in the ether and the manly vigor in men
5. O Arjuna I am the fluidity in water the light in the sun and moon the mystic syllable Om in the Vedic scriptures the sound in ether and the virility in man
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पणय गनध पथवय च तजशचसम वभवस जवन सरवभतष तपशचसम तपसवष 79
Translations:
1. I am the pure scent in the earth I am the radiance in the fire I am the lifeforce in all beings and austerity in ascetics
2. I am also the sweet fragrance in the earth I am the brilliance in the fire and the life in all beings and I am the austerity of the ascetics
3. I am the sweet fragrance in the earth and the brilliance in the fire the life in all beings and I am the austerity of ascetics
4. I am the pure scent in the earth I am also the radiance in the sun I am the life in all beings and the austerity in the ascetics
5. I am the fragrance of the earth the brilliance of fire the life force in all beings and the austerity of ascetics
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बज म सरवभतन वदध परथ सनतनम बदधरबदधमतमसम तजसतजसवनमहम 710
Translations:
1. Know Me O Arjuna to be the primeval seed of all beings I am the intelligence of the discerning and the brilliance of the brilliant
2. O Partha know Me to be the eternal seed of all beings I am the intellect of the intelligent and I am the courage of the courageous
3. Know Me O Arjuna as the eternal seed of all beings I am the intelligence of the intelligent and the splendour of the splendid objects
4. O son of Prtha Know Me as the eternal seed of all beings I am the intellect of the intellectuals and the brilliance of the brilliant
5. Know O Arjuna that I am the eternal seed of being I am the intelligence of the intelligent the splendour of the resplendent
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बल बलवत चह कमरगववरजतम धरमवरदध भतष कमऽसम भरतरषभ 711
Translations:
1. In the strong I am strength devoid of desire and passion In all beings I am the desire which is not contrary to Dharma O Arjuna
2. And of the strong I am the strength that is devoid of passion and attachment Among creatures I am desire that is not contrary to righteousness O scion of the Bharata dynasty
3. Of the strong I am the strength devoid of desire and attachment and in all beings I am the desire in accordance with Dharma O Arjuna
4. Of the strong I am the strength that is free from desire and attachment O best of the Bharatas in all beings I am the desire that is not contrary to dharma
5. I am the strength of the strong those who are free from attachment and desire and O Arjuna I am the desire for righteousness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य चव सततवक भव रजससतमसशच य मतत एवत तनवदध नतवह तष त मय 712
Translations:
1. Know that all those states of Sattva Rajas and Tamas are from Me alone however I am not in them rather they are in Me

2. Those things that are made of the quality of sattva and those things that are made of the quality of rajas and tamas know them to have sprung from Me alone However I am not in them they are within Me
3. Whatever beings and objects that are pure active and inert know that they proceed from Me They are in Me yet I am not in them
4. Whatever beings are therewhether they are of the Sattva Rajas or Tamas strandsbe sure that they are from Me I am not in them but they are within Me
5. Whatever be the nature of their life whether it be pure passionate or ignorant they are all derived from Me They are in Me but I am not in them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तरभरगणमयरभवरभ सरवमद जगत महत नभजनत ममभय परमवययम 713
Translations:
1. The entire universe is deluded by these three states originating from the Gunas of Prakrti and fails to recognize Me who am beyond them and immutable
2. All this world deluded as it is by these three qualities made of the gunas does not know Me who am transcendental to these and undecaying
3. Deluded by these Natures composed of the three qualities of Nature all this world does not know Me as distinct from them and immutable
4. Being deluded by these three strands of nature this entire world does not recognize Me who am eternal and transcend these strands
5. The inhabitants of the world misled by the natures engendered by the Qualities do not know that I am higher than them all and that I do not change
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दव हयष गणमय मम मय दरतयय ममव य परपदयनत मयमत तरनत त 714
Translations:
1. a For this divine Maya or Mine consisting of the three Gunas assumed for purposes of sport is hard to overcome ৷৷ b ৷৷ But those who take refuge in Me alone shall pass beyond the Maya
2. Since this divine Maya of Mine which is constituted by the gunas is difficult to cross over those who take refuge in Me alone can transcend this Maya
3. Verily this divine illusion of Mine composed of the three qualities is difficult to cross over those who take refuge in Me alone can cross over this illusion
4. This is My divine play composed of the strands of illusion and it is hard to cross over Those who take refuge in Me alone they cross over the illusion
5. Verily this divine illusion of phenomenon manifesting itself in the qualities is difficult to surmount Only they who devote themselves exclusively to Me can accomplish it
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न म दषकतन मढ परपदयनत नरधम मययपहतजञन आसर भवमशरत 715
Translations:
1. The evildoers the foolish the lowest of men those persons deprived of knowledge by delusion Maya and those who are dominated by a demoniac nature they do not seek refuge in Me
2. The foolish evildoers who are the most depraved among men who are deprived of their wisdom by Maya and who resort to demonic ways do not take refuge in Me
3. The evildoers and the deluded who are the lowest of men do not seek Me those whose knowledge is destroyed by illusion follow the ways of demons
4. The deluded evildoers the vilest of men who are robbed of knowledge by the trick of illusion and have taken refuge in the demonic naturethey do not resort to Me
5. The sinner the ignorant the vile deprived of spiritual perception by the glamour of illusion and he who leads a godless lifenone of them shall find me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: चतरवध भजनत म जन सकतनऽरजन आरत जजञसररथरथ जञन च भरतरषभ 716
Translations:
1. Four types of men of good deeds worship Me O Arjuna the distressed the seekers of knowledge the seekers of wealth and the men of knowledge
2. O Arjuna foremost of the Bharata dynasty four classes of people of virtuous deeds adore Me the afflicted the seekers of Knowledge the seekers of wealth and the man of Knowledge
3. Four kinds of virtuous men worship Me O Arjuna and they are the distressed the seekers of knowledge the seekers of wealth and the wise O Lord of the Bharatas
4. Men of good action who always worship Me are of four types the afflicted the seeker of knowledge the seeker of wealth and the man of wisdom O best among the Bharatas
5. O Arjuna The righteous who worship Me are grouped in stages first those who suffer next those who desire knowledge then those who thirst after truth and lastly those who attain wisdom
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तष जञन नतययकत एकभकतरवशषयत परय ह जञननऽतयरथमह स च मम परय 717
Translations:
1. Of these the man of knowledge who is ever with Me in Yoga and devoted to the One only is the foremost for I am very dear to the man of knowledge and he is also dear to Me
2. Of them the man of knowledge endowed with constant steadfastness and onepointed devotion excels For I am very dear to the man of knowledge and he too is dear to me
3. Of them the wise who are ever steadfast and devoted to the One excel for I am exceedingly dear to the wise and they are dear to Me
4. Of them the man of wisdom being always attached to Me with singlepointed devotion excels For I am dear to the man of wisdom above all personal gains and he is dear to Me

5. Of all these he who has gained wisdom who meditates on Me without ceasing devoting himself solely to Me is the best for I am exceedingly beloved by the wise man and the wise man is also beloved by Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: उदर सरव एवत जञन तवतमव म मतम आसथत स ह यकततम ममवनततम गतम 718
Translations:
1. All these are indeed generous but I deem the man of knowledge to be My very self for he integrated is devoted solely to Me as the highest end
2. Indeed all of these are noble but the man of Knowledge is the Self itself This is my opinion For with a steadfast mind he is set on the path leading only to Me who am the supreme Goal
3. Indeed all these are noble however I consider the wise man as My very Self for he is steadfast in mind and established in Me alone as the supreme goal

4. All these are indeed noble persons But the man of wisdom is considered as the very Soul of Mine For with his self mind that has mastered the Yoga he has resorted to Me alone as his most supreme goal
5. Nobleminded are they all but the wise man I hold as my own self for he remaining always at peace with me makes me his ultimate goal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बहन जनमनमनत जञनवनम परपदयत वसदव सरवमत स महतम सदरलभ 719
Translations:
1. At the end of many births the man of knowledge finds refuge in Me realizing that Vasudeva is all It is very difficult to find such a greatsouled person
2. At the end of many births the man of knowledge attains Me realizing that Vasudeva is all Such a highsouled one is very rare
3. At the end of many births the wise man comes to Me realizing that all this is Vaasudeva the innermost Self such a great soul Mahatma is very hard to find
4. At the end of many births one attains Me with the conviction that All is Vasudeva that noble soul is very difficult to attain
5. After many lives at last the wise one realizes Me as I am It is very difficult to find a man so enlightened that he sees God everywhere
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कमसतसतरहतजञन परपदयनतऽनयदवत त त नयममसथय परकतय नयत सवय 720
Translations:
1. Controlled by their inherent nature and deprived of knowledge by various desires worldlyminded people resort to other gods observing various disciplines
2. People deprived of their wisdom by desires for various objects and guided by their own nature resort to other deities following the relevant methods
3. Those whose wisdom has been taken away by this or that desire go to other gods following this or that rite led by their own nature
4. Being robbed of their wisdom by innumerable desires and controlled by their own nature persons take refuge in other deities following one or the other religious regulations
5. They in whom wisdom is obscured by one desire or another worship the lesser powers practicing many rites that vary according to their temperaments
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य य य य तन भकत शरदधयरचतमचछत तसय तसयचल शरदध तमव वदधमयहम 721
Translations:
1. Whichever devotee seeks to worship with faith whatever form I make their faith steadfast
2. Whichever form of a deity any devotee wants to worship with faith I strengthen that very firm faith of theirs
3. Whatever form any devotee desires to worship with faith I make that same faith of his firm and unflinching
4. Whatever form of deity a devotee whomever they may be desires to worship with faith I assume that form which is firm and is according to their faith
5. But whatever form of worship if the devotee has faith then I set My own seal upon their faith in that worship
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: स तय शरदधय यकतसतसयरधनमहत लभत च तत कमनमयव वहतन ह तन 722
Translations:
1. Endowed with that faith he worships that form and thereby gets the objects of his desire granted in reality by Me alone
2. Being imbued with that faith that person engages in worshipping that form and they get those very desired results as dispensed by Me alone
3. Endowed with that faith he engages in the worship of that form and obtains his desired outcome which is ordained by Me alone
4. Endowed with that faith he seeks to worship that deity and thereby receives his desired objects which are ordained by none other than Me
5. If he worships one form alone with real faith then his desires shall be fulfilled through that alone for thus I have ordained
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनतवतत फल तष तदभवतयलपमधसम दवनदवयज यनत मदभकत यनत ममप 723
Translations:
1. But the fruit gained by these men of small understanding is limited The worshippers of the gods will go to the gods but My devotees will come to Me
2. The result of those who are of poor intellect is indeed limited The worshippers of gods go to the gods while My devotees go to Me alone
3. Verily the reward fruit that accrues to those men of small intelligence is finite The worshippers of the gods go to them whereas My devotees come to Me
4. But the fruit of those of poor intellect is finite those who perform sacrifices aiming at the gods go to the gods and My devotees come to Me
5. The fruit that comes to people of limited insight is after all finite Those who worship the Lower Powers attain them but those who worship Me come to Me alone
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवयकत वयकतमपनन मनयनत ममबदधय पर भवमजननत ममवययमनततमम 724
Translations:
1. Not knowing My higher immutable and unsurpassed nature the ignorant think of Me as an entity that has now become manifest though I was previously unmanifest
2. The unintelligent unaware of My supreme state which is immutable and unsurpassable think of Me as having become manifest from the unmanifest
3. The foolish think of Me the Unmanifest as having manifestation not knowing My higher immutable and most excellent nature
4. Those of poor intellect are not aware of My higher unchanging and supreme nature and thus they consider Me the Unmanifest to be manifest
5. The ignorant think of Me who am the Unmanifested Spirit as if I were in human form They do not understand that My Superior Nature is unchanging and most excellent
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नह परकश सरवसय यगमयसमवत मढऽय नभजनत लक ममजमवययम 725
Translations:
1. Veiled by my Maya I am not manifest to all this deluded world does not recognize me as the unborn and immutable
2. Being enveloped by yogamaya I do not become manifest to all This deluded world does not know Me who am birthless and undecaying
3. I am not manifest to all veiled as I am by the YogaMaya This deluded world does not know Me who am unborn and imperishable
4. Being surrounded by the trick of yogaillusion I am not clear to all hence this deluded world of perceivers does not recognize Me the unborn and undying
5. I am not visible to all for I am enveloped by the illusion of the Phenomenon This deluded world does not know Me as the Unborn and Imperishable
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वदह समततन वरतमनन चरजन भवषयण च भतन म त वद न कशचन 726
Translations:
1. I know all beings O Arjuna past present and those to come but no one knows Me
2. O Arjuna I know the past present and future beings but no one knows Me
3. I know O Arjuna the beings of the past the present and the future however no one knows Me
4. O Arjuna I know the beings that have gone that are present and those yet to be born but no one knows Me
5. I know O Arjuna all beings in the past the present and the future but they do not know Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इचछदवषसमतथन दवनदवमहन भरत सरवभतन समह सरग यनत परनतप 727
Translations:
1. O Arjuna all beings are deluded as soon as they are born due to the delusion of the pairs of opposites springing from desire and hate
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty O destroyer of foes due to the delusion of duality arising from likes and dislikes all creatures become bewildered upon their birth
3. O Bharata all beings are subject to delusion at birth due to the delusion of the pairs of opposites arising from desire and aversion O Parantapa
4. O descendant of Bharata O scorcher of foes At the time of creation all beings become deluded due to the illusion of pairs of opposites arising from desire and hatred
5. O brave Arjuna Man lives in a fantastical world deceived by the glamour of opposing sensations infatuated by desire and aversion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यष तवनतगत पप जनन पणयकरमणम त दवनदवमहनरमकत भजनत म दढवरत 728
Translations:
1. But those who have done good deeds whose sins have come to an end are freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites They steadfastly worship Me adhering to their vows
2. On the other hand those persons who have done virtuous deeds whose sin has come to an end they being free from the delusion of duality and firm in their convictions adore Me
3. But those men of virtuous deeds whose sins have come to an end and who are freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites worship Me steadfastly with their vows
4. But those men of virtuous deeds whose sin has come to an endthey being free from the delusion of pairs of opposites worship Me with a firm resolve
5. But those who act righteously in whom sin has been destroyed who are free from the infatuation of conflicting emotions they worship Me with a firm resolve
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जरमरणमकषय ममशरतय यतनत य त बरहम तदवद कतसनमधयतम करम चखलम 729
Translations:
1. Those who take refuge in Me and strive for deliverance from old age and death know Brahman or the Self and all about the nature of that Self and the entire Karma or activities leading to rebirth

2. Those who strive by resorting to Me for becoming free from old age and death they know Brahman the individual Self and all about actions They know Brahman as being all the individual entities and all actions This verse prescribes meditation on the qualified Brahman for aspirants of the middle class Verses beginning with the 14th speak about the realization of the unqualified Brahman by aspirants of the highest class
3. Those who strive for liberation from old age and death taking refuge in Me realize in full that Brahman the whole knowledge of the Self and all action
4. Those who relying on Me strive to achieve freedom from old age and death realize all to be the Brahman and realize all the actions governing the Self
5. Those who make Me their refuge striving for liberation from decay and death realize the Supreme Spirit which is their own real Self and in which all action finds its consummation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 7.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 7.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सधभतधदव म सधयजञ च य वद परयणकलऽप च म त वदरयकतचतस 730
Translations:
1. And those who know Me with the Adhibhuta Adhidaiva and Adhiyajna they too with their minds fixed in meditation know Me even at the hour of death
2. Those who know me as existing in both the physical and divine planes and also in the context of the sacrifice they with concentrated minds know me even at the time of death

3. Those who know Me with the Adhibhuta pertaining to the elements Adhidaiva pertaining to the gods and the Adhiyajna pertaining to the sacrifice know Me even at the time of death remaining steadfast in mind
4. Those who realize Me as one identical with what governs the beings deities and with what governs the sacrificesthey even at the moment of their departure experience Me having mastered the Yoga
5. Those who see Me in the life of the world in the universal sacrifice and as pure Divinity keeping their minds steady they live in Me even in the hour of deaths approach
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.1 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 8.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: क तदबरहम कमधयतम क करम परषततम अधभत च क परकतमधदव कमचयत 81
Translations:
1. Arjuna said O Supreme Person what is Brahman What exists in the individual plane What is action What is said to exist in the physical plane And what is said to exist in the divine plane
2. Arjuna said O Supreme Person what is Brahman What exists in the individual plane What is action What is said to exist in the physical plane And what is said to exist in the divine plane
3. Arjuna said What is Brahman What is Adhyatma What is action O best among men What is Adhibhuta declared to be And what is Adhidaiva said to be

4. Arjuna said What is Brahman What is known as the Lord of the Self Adhyatma What is action O Best of Persons what is said to be the Lord of material things Adhibhuta What is called the Lord of divinities Adhidaiva
5. Arjuna asked O Lord of Lords What is that which people call the Supreme Spirit what is humanitys spiritual nature and what is the law What is matter and what is divinity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.2 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 8.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अधयजञ कथ कऽतर दहऽसमनमधसदन परयणकल च कथ जञयऽस नयततमभ 82
Translations:
1. O Madhusudana how is the entity existing in the sacrifice here in this body and who is it And how are You to be known by people of concentrated minds at the time of death
2. O Madhusudana how is the entity existing in the sacrifice here in this body and who is it And how are You to be known by people of concentrated minds at the time of death
3. Who and how is Adhiyajna here in this body O destroyer of Madhu And how at the time of death are You to be known by the selfcontrolled
4. Who is the Lord of Sacrifices and how Who is in this body O slayer of Madhu how can the selfcontrolled realize You at the time of their journey ie death
5. Who rules the spirit sacrifice in many and how may those who have learned selfcontrol come to the knowledge of Thee at the time of death
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अकषर बरहम परम सवभवऽधयतममचयत भतभवदभवकर वसरग करमसजञत 83
Translations:

1. The Blessed Lord said The Immutable is the supreme Brahman selfhood is said to be the entity present in the individual plane By action is meant the offerings that bring about the origin of existence of things

2. The Blessed Lord said The Immutable is the supreme Brahman selfhood is said to be the entity present in the individual plane By action is meant the offerings that bring about the origin of existence of things

3. The Blessed Lord said Brahman is the Imperishable the Supreme its essential nature is called Selfknowledge the offering to the gods that causes the existence and manifestation of beings and sustains them is called action

4. The Bhagavat said The immutable Absolute is the Brahman Its intrinsic nature is called the Lord of the Self The emitting activity that causes the birth of both the animate and inanimate is named action

5. The Lord Shri Krishna replied The Supreme Spirit is the Highest Imperishable Self and Its nature is spiritual consciousness The worlds have been created and are sustained by an emanation from the Spirit which is called the Law
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अधभत कषर भव परषशचधदवतम अधयजञऽहमवतर दह दहभत वर 84
Translations:

1. That which exists in the physical plane is the mutable entity and what exists in the divine plane is the Person O best among the embodied beings I Myself am the entity that exists in the sacrifice within this body

2. That which exists in the physical plane is the mutable entity and what exists in the divine plane is the Person O best among the embodied beings I Myself am the entity that exists in the sacrifice within this body
3. Adhibhutaknowledge of the elementspertains to My perishable nature and the Purusha or the Soul is the Adhidaiva I alone am the Adhiyajna here in this body O best among the embodied
4. The changing nature is the lord of the material beings the Person alone is the lord of the divinities I am alone the Lord of sacrifices and I the best of the embodied souls dwell in this body
5. Matter consists of forms that perish Divinity is the Supreme Self and He who inspires the spirit of sacrifice in man O noblest of their race is I Myself Who now stand in human form before thee
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनतकल च ममव समरनमकतव कलवरम य परयत स मदभव यत नसतयतर सशय 85
Translations:
1. And at the time of death anyone who departs giving up the body while thinking of Me alone attains My state there is no doubt about this
2. And at the time of death anyone who departs giving up the body while thinking of Me alone attains My state there is no doubt about this
3. And whoever leaving their body goes forth remembering Me alone at the time of death they will attain My Being there is no doubt about this
4. Whosoever at the time of death remembering Me alone sets forth abandoning their body behind they attain My being There is no doubt about it
5. Whosoever at the time of death thinks only of Me and thinking thus leaves the body and goes forth will assuredly know Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य य वप समरनभव तयजतयनत कलवरम त तमवत कनतय सद तदभवभवत 86
Translations:
1. O son of Kunti thinking of any entity whichever it may be one gives up the body at the end he attains that very one having been always engrossed in its thought
2. O son of Kunti thinking of any entity whichever it may be one gives up the body at the end he attains that very one having been always engrossed in its thought
3. Whoever at the end leaves the body thinking of any being to that being only does he go O son of Kunti Arjuna due to his constant thought of that being
4. And also remembering whatever being one has been constantly thinking about a person leaves his body at the end of his life O son of Kunti That being alone he attains
5. Whatever sphere of being the mind of a man is intent on at the time of death to that he will go
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमतसरवष कलष ममनसमर यधय च मययरपतमनबदधरममवषयसयसशयम 87
Translations:
1. Therefore think of Me at all times and fight there is no doubt that by dedicating your mind and intellect to Me you will attain only Me
2. Therefore think of Me at all times and fight there is no doubt that by dedicating your mind and intellect to Me you will attain only Me
3. Therefore at all times remember Me only and fight With your mind and intellect fixed on Me you will undoubtedly come to Me alone
4. Therefore at all times keep Me in your mind and fight then having your mind and intellect dedicated to Me you will undoubtedly attain Me alone
5. Therefore always meditate on Me and fight if your mind and reason are fixed on Me you will surely come to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अभयसयगयकतन चतस ननयगमन परम परष दवय यत परथनचनतयन 88
Translations:
1. O son of Prtha by meditating with a mind engaged in the yoga of practice and not straying away to anything else one reaches the supreme Person existing in the effulgent region
2. O son of Prtha by meditating with a mind engaged in the yoga of practice and not straying away to anything else one reaches the supreme Person existing in the effulgent region
3. With the mind not moving towards any other thing made steadfast through the practice of habitual meditation and constantly meditating one goes to the Supreme Person the Resplendent O Arjuna
4. He who is engaged in reflecting on the Supreme Divine Soul with his mind remaining fixed in the practice of yoga and not focusing on any other object that person attains the Supreme O son of Prtha
5. He whose mind does not wander and who is engaged in constant meditation attains the Supreme Spirit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कव परणमनशसतर मणरणयसमनसमरदय सरवसय धतरमचनतयरप मदतयवरण तमस परसतत 89
Translations:

1. He who meditates on the Omniscient the Ancient the Ruler subtler than the subtle the Ordainer of everything of inconceivable form effulgent like the sun and beyond darknesshe attains the Supreme Person

2. He who meditates on the Omniscient the Ancient the Ruler subtler than the subtle the Ordainer of everything of inconceivable form effulgent like the sun and beyond darknesshe attains the Supreme Person

3. Whosoever meditates on the Omniscient the Ancient the Ruler of the whole world minuter than an atom the supporter of all of inconceivable form effulgent like the sun and beyond the darkness of ignorance
4. He who meditates continuously on the Ancient Seer the Ruler the subtler than the subtle the supporter of all the unimaginably formed the suncolored and that which is beyond darkness
5. Whoso meditates on the Omniscient the Ancient more minute than an atom yet the Ruler and Upholder of all Unimaginable Brilliant like the Sun Beyond the reach of darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परयणकल मनसऽचलन भकतय यकत यगबलन चव भरवरमधय परणमवशय समयक स त पर परषमपत दवयम 810
Translations:

1. At the time of death having fully fixed the Prana vital force between the eyebrows with an unwavering mind and being imbued with devotion as well as the strength of concentration he reaches that resplendent Supreme Person

2. At the time of death having fully fixed the Prana vital force between the eyebrows with an unwavering mind and being imbued with devotion as well as the strength of concentration he reaches that resplendent Supreme Person
3. At the time of death with an unwavering mind endowed with devotion by the power of Yoga fixing the whole lifebreath in the middle of the two eyebrows he reaches that resplendent Supreme Person
4. That person endowed with a steady mind devotion and the power of yoga reaches the Supreme Divine Soul at the time of departure by properly fixing the lifebreath between their eyebrows
5. He who leaves the body with an unmoved mind filled with devotion and by the power of his meditation gathers his whole vital energy between his eyebrows attains the Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यदकषर वदवद वदनत वशनत यदयतय वतरग यदचछनत बरहमचरय चरनत ततत पद सगरहण परवकषय 811
Translations:
1. I will briefly speak to you of that immutable Goal which the knowers of the Vedas declare into which diligent ones free from attachment enter and for which people practice celibacy aspiring
2. I will briefly tell you about that immutable Goal which the knowers of the Vedas declare those who are diligent free from attachment and aspire for it enter into it People practice celibacy for it

3. That which is declared to be Imperishable by those who know the Vedas that which the selfcontrolled ascetics or Sannyasins and passionfree enter that goal desiring which celibacy is practised I will declare to thee in brief

4. That Unchanging One which the Vedic knowers speak of which the passionfree ascetics enter into seeking which they practice celibacy or spiritual life that Goal together with the means to reach it I shall tell you

5. Now I will briefly speak of the imperishable goal proclaimed by those who are versed in the scriptures which the mystic attains when free from passion and for which they are content to undergo the vow of continence
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवदवरण सयमय मन हद नरधय च मरधनयधयतमन परणमसथत यगधरणम 812
Translations:
1. Having controlled all the senses confined the mind in the heart and fixed his own vital force in the head he should remain steadfast in yoga
2. Having controlled all the senses confined the mind in the heart and fixed his own vital force in the head he should remain firm in yoga
3. Having closed all the gates confined the mind in the heart and fixed the lifebreath in the head engage in the practice of concentration
4. Properly controlling all the gates of the body restraining the mind firmly in the heat fixing ones own prana in the head taking refuge in the firmness of Yoga
5. Closing the gates of the body drawing the forces of his mind into the heart and by the power of meditation concentrating his vital energy in the brain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ओमतयककषर बरहम वयहरनममनसमरन य परयत तयजनदह स यत परम गतम 813
Translations:
1. He who departs leaving the body while uttering the single syllable viz Om which is Brahman and thinking of Me attains the supreme Goal
2. He who departs leaving the body while uttering the single syllable Om which is Brahman and thinking of Me attains the supreme Goal
3. Uttering the onesyllabled Om the Brahman and remembering Me he who departs leaving the body attains the Supreme Goal
4. Reciting the singlesyllabled Om the very Brahman meditating on Me whoever travels well casting away their bodysurely they attain My State
5. Repeating Om the symbol of eternity and holding Me always in remembrance he who thus leaves his body and goes forth reaches the Supreme Spirit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अननयचत सतत य म समरत नतयश तसयह सलभ परथ नतययकतसय यगन 814
Translations:
1. O son of Prtha to that yogi of constant concentration and singleminded attention who remembers Me uninterruptedly and for a long time I am easy to attain
2. O son of Prtha to that yogi of constant concentration and singleminded attention who remembers Me uninterruptedly and for a long time I am easily attained
3. I am easily attainable by that eversteadfast yogi who constantly and daily remembers me for a long time not thinking of anything else with a singleminded or onepointed focus O Partha
4. And whoever constantly bears Me in mind never attached to any other objectfor this yogi ever devoted I am easy to attain O son of Prtha
5. To him who constantly thinks of Me and nothing else to such an everfaithful devotee O Arjuna I am ever accessible
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ममपतय पनरजनम दखलयमशशवतम नपनवनत महतमन ससदध परम गत 815
Translations:
1. As a result of reaching Me the exalted ones who have attained the highest perfection do not get birth which is an abode of sorrows and is impermanent
2. As a result of reaching Me the exalted ones who have attained the highest perfection do not get rebirth which is an abode of sorrows and is impermanent
3. Having attained Me these great souls do not take birth again herea place of pain and impermanencebut have reached the highest perfection of liberation
4. Having attained Me the men of great soul who have achieved the supreme perfection do not get the transient birth a storehouse of all troubles
5. Coming to Me in this way these great souls no longer go to the misery and death of earthly life for they have achieved perfection
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आबरहमभवनललक पनरवरतनऽरजन ममपतय त कनतय पनरजनम न वदयत 816
Translations:
1. O Arjuna all the worlds together with the world of Brahma are subject to return But O son of Kunti there is no rebirth after reaching Me
2. O Arjuna all the worlds together with the world of Brahma are subject to return But O son of Kunti there is no return after reaching Me
3. All the worlds including the world of Brahma are subject to return again O Arjuna but he who reaches Me O son of Kunti has no rebirth
4. Till the Brahman is attained people return from each and every world O Arjuna But there is no return for one who has attained Me O son of Kunti
5. The worlds along with the entire realm of creation come and go but O Arjuna whoever comes to Me for them there is no rebirth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सहसरयगपरयनतमहरयदबरहमण वद रतर यगसहसरनत तऽहरतरवद जन 817
Translations:

1. Those people who know what day and night are know the day of Brahma which ends in a thousand yugas The four yugas in the human worlds viz Satya Treta Dwapara and Kali are made up of 4320000 years This period multiplied by a thousand constitutes one day of Brahma His night also extends over a similar period See MS and VSA and His night which also ends in a thousand yugas
2. Those people who know what day and night are know the day of Brahma which ends in a thousand yugas and His night which also ends in a thousand yugas
3. Those who know the day of Brahma which lasts a thousand Yugas and the night which also lasts a thousand Yugas know day and night
4. Those who know the day of Brahma as encompassing one thousand yugas worldages and night also as encompassing one thousand yugasthose people know the day and night of Brahma
5. Those who understand the cosmic day and night know that one day of creation is a thousand cycles and that the night is of equal length
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवयकतदवयकतय सरव परभवनतयहरगम रतरयगम परलयनत ततरववयकतसजञक 818
Translations:
1. With the coming of day all manifested things emerge from the Unmanifest and when night comes they merge into that which is called the Unmanifested
2. With the coming of day all manifested things emerge from the Unmanifest and when night comes they merge into that which is called the Unmanifested
3. From the Unmanifested all the manifested worlds proceed upon the arrival of the day upon the arrival of the night they dissolve indeed into that which is known as the Unmanifested
4. As the day approaches all manifestations emerge from the unmanifest and as the night approaches they dissolve into the same which is known as the unmanifest
5. At the dawn of that day all objects in manifestation stream forth from the Unmanifest and when evening falls they are dissolved back into it again
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भतगरम स एवय भतव भतव परलयत रतरयगमऽवश परथ परभवतयहरगम 819
Translations:
1. O son of Prtha after being born again and again that very multitude of beings disappears of its own accord at the approach of night It comes to life again at the approach of day
2. O son of Prtha after being born again and again that very multitude of beings disappears of its own accord at the approach of night It comes to life again at the approach of day
3. This same multitude of beings being born again and again helplessly dissolves O Arjuna into the Unmanifested at the coming of the night and comes forth at the coming of the day
4. Being born and reborn the same multitude of beings is dissolved when night approaches and is born again willynilly when day approaches O son of Prtha
5. The same multitude of beings which have lived on earth so often all are dissolved as the night of the universe approaches to issue forth anew when morning breaks Thus it is ordained
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परसतसमतत भवऽनयऽवयकतऽवयकततसनतन य स सरवष भतष नशयतस न वनशयत 820
Translations:
1. But distinct from that Unmanifested is the other eternal unmanifest Reality who does not get destroyed when all beings are destroyed
2. But distinct from that Unmanifested is the other eternal unmanifest Reality who does not get destroyed when all beings are destroyed
3. But verily there exists higher than this Unmanifested another Unmanifested Eternal which is not destroyed even when all beings are destroyed
4. But there exists another Being which is beyond this and It is both manifest and unmanifest and is eternal It is this Being that does not perish while all other beings perish
5. In truth therefore there is the eternal unmanifest which is beyond and above the unmanifest spirit of creation and which is never destroyed when all these beings perish
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवयकतऽकषर इतयकतसतमह परम गतम य परपय न नवरतनत तदधम परम मम 821
Translations:
1. They call Him who has been mentioned as the Unmanifested the Immutable the supreme Goal That is the supreme abode of Mine from which they do not return once they reach it
2. They call Him who has been mentioned as the Unmanifested the Immutable the supreme Goal That is the supreme abode of Mine from which they do not return once they reach it
3. What is known as the Unmanifested and the Imperishable That is said to be the highest goal Those who reach It do not return to this Samsara That is My supreme abode place or state
4. The scriptures speak of This as Unmanifest and Changeless and declare This to be the highest Goal Having attained it people do not return this is My highest abode
5. The wise say that the Unmanifest and Indestructible is the highest goal of all when once that is reached there is no returning That is my blessed home
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परष स पर परथ भकतय लभयसतवननयय यसयनतसथन भतन यन सरवमद ततम 822
Translations:
1. O son of Prtha that supreme Personin whom all beings are included and by whom all this is pervadedis indeed reached through onepointed devotion
2. O son of Prtha that supreme Personin whom all beings are included and by whom all this is pervadedis indeed reached through onepointed devotion
3. That highest Purusha O Arjuna is attainable by unswerving devotion to Him alone within Whom all beings dwell and by Whom all this is pervaded

4. O son of Prtha the Supreme Soul is attainable through devotion that admits no other things having attained it the men of Yoga do not get birth again within it exist the beings and everything is well established in it O Arjuna

5. O Arjuna That Highest God in Whom all beings abide and Who pervades the entire universe is reached only by wholehearted devotion He is the one who determines the course of worldly events and the destiny of all living beings
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतर कल तवनवततमवतत चव यगन परयत यनत त कल वकषयम भरतरषभ 823
Translations:
1. O best of the Bharata dynasty I shall now speak of that time by which the yogis depart and attain the State of Nonreturn and also the State of Return
2. O best of the Bharata dynasty I shall now speak of that time by which the yogis depart and attain the State of Nonreturn and also the State of Return
3. Now I will tell you O chief of the Bharatas the times of departure at which the Yogis will return or not return
4. Departing at what times the Yogis attain the nonreturn or the return onlythose times I shall declare to you O chief of the Bharatas
5. Now I will tell you O Arjuna the times at which if the mystics go forth they do not return and at which they go forth only to return
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अगनरजयतरह शकल षणमस उततरयणम ततर परयत गचछनत बरहम बरहमवद जन 824
Translations:
1. Fire light daytime the bright fortnight the six months of the northern solsticeby following this path persons who are knowers of Brahman attain Brahman when they die
2. Fire light daytime the bright fortnight the six months of the northern solsticeby following this path persons who are knowers of Brahman attain Brahman when they die
3. Fire light daytime the bright fortnight the six months of the northern path of the sun the northern solstice departing then men who know Brahman go to Brahman
4. The northern course consisting of six months is fire light day and bright Departing in it the Brahmanknowing men attain Brahman

5. If the sage knowing the Supreme Spirit goes forth with fire and light during the day in the fortnight of the waxing moon and in the six months before the Northern summer solstice he will attain the Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धम रतरसतथ कषण षणमस दकषणयनम ततर चनदरमस जयतरयग परपय नवरतत 825
Translations:
1. Following this Path the yogi having reached the lunar light returns smoke night as also the dark fortnight and the six months of the Southern solstice
2. Following this Path the yogi having reached the lunar light returns smoke night as also the dark fortnight and the six months of the Southern solstice
3. Attaining the lunar light through smoke night time the dark fortnight and the six months of the southern path of the sun the southern solstice the yogi returns
4. The southern course of the sun consisting of six months is smoke night and darkness Departing in it the yogi attains the light of the moon and returns
5. But if he departs in gloom at night during the fortnight of the waning moon and in the six months before the winter solstice then he reaches only lunar light and he will be reborn
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शकलकषण गत हयत जगत शशवत मत एकय यतयनवततमनययऽऽवरतत पन 826
Translations:
1. These two paths of the world which are white and black are indeed considered eternal Through one a person goes to the state of nonreturn through the other they return again
2. These two paths of the world which are white and black are indeed considered eternal Through one a person goes to the state of nonreturn through the other they return again
3. The bright and dark paths of the world are thought to be eternal one leads to no return and the other leads to return
4. For these two bright and dark paths are considered to be perpetual for the world One attains nonreturn by the first of these and one returns back by the other
5. These bright and dark paths out of the world have always existed Whoever takes the former does not return he who chooses the latter returns
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नत सत परथ जननयग महयत कशचन तसमतसरवष कलष यगयकत भवरजन 827
Translations:
1. O son of Prtha no yogione steadfast in meditationwhosoever has known these two courses becomes deluded Therefore O Arjuna be steadfast in yoga at all times
2. O son of Prtha no yogione steadfast in meditationwhosoever has known these two courses becomes deluded Therefore O Arjuna be steadfast in yoga at all times
3. Knowing these paths O Arjuna no yogi is deluded therefore at all times be steadfast in yoga
4. O son of Prtha no Yogin knowing these two courses gets deluded Therefore O Arjuna practice Yoga connected with all times
5. O Arjuna The saint knowing these paths is not confused therefore meditate perpetually
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 8.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 8.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वदष यजञष तपस चव दनष यतपणयफल परदषटम अतयत ततसरवमद वदतव यग पर सथनमपत चदयम 828
Translations:
1. Having known this the yogi transcends all the results of righteous deeds declared in the Vedas sacrifices austerities and charities and reaches the primordial supreme state
2. Having known this the yogi transcends all the results of righteous deeds declared in the Vedas sacrifices austerities and charities and reaches the primordial supreme state

3. Whatever fruit of merit is declared in the scriptures to accrue from the study of the Vedas the performance of sacrifices the practice of austerities and gifts beyond all this goes the Yogi having known this and he attains to the Supreme Primeval first or ancient Abode

4. Having understood all this the Yogin goes beyond whatever fruit of merit is ordained in the scriptures in case of reciting the Vedas performing sacrifices observing austerities and donating gifts and he goes to the Supreme Primeval Abode
5. The sage who knows this passes beyond all merit that comes from the study of the scriptures from sacrifice from austerities and from charity and reaches the Supreme Primeval Abode
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इद त त गहयतम परवकषयमयनसयव जञन वजञनसहत यजजञतव मकषयसऽशभत 91
Translations:
1. The Lord said I shall declare to you who does not cavil this most mysterious knowledge together with its special knowledge knowing which you will be freed from evil

2. The Blessed Lord said However to you who are not given to cavilling I shall speak of this highest secret itself which is knowledge combined with experience by realizing which you shall be free from evil
3. The Blessed Lord said I shall now declare to thee who does not cavil the greatest secretthe knowledge combined with experience Selfrealisation Having known this thou shalt be free from evil
4. The Bhagavat said To you who are entertaining no displeasure I shall clearly declare this most secret knowledge together with action by knowing which you shall be free from evil
