5. Lord Shri Krishna said I will now reveal to you since you do not doubt that profound mysticism which when followed with experience will liberate you from sin
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रजवदय रजगहय पवतरमदमततमम परतयकषवगम धरमय ससख करतमवययम 92
Translations:
1. This is the royal science the royal mystery the supreme purifier It is realized through direct experience is in accord with Dharma is pleasant to practice and is abiding
2. This is the sovereign knowledge the sovereign profundity the best sanctifier directly realizable righteous very easy to practice and imperishable
3. This is the royal science the royal secret the supreme purifier realizable by direct intuitive knowledge according to righteousness very easy to perform and imperishable
4. This shines among the sciences it is the secret of monarchs it is a supreme purifier comprehensible by immediate perception righteous easy to do and imperishable
5. This is the supreme science the supreme secret the purest and best it is intuitive righteous and to those who practice it it is exceedingly pleasant
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अशरददधन परष धरमसयसय परनतप अपरपय म नवरतनत मतयससरवरतमन 93
Translations:
1. Men devoid of faith in this Dharma O scorcher of foes ever remain without attaining Me on the mortal path of Samsara
2. O destroyer of foes those who are disregarding this Dharma knowledge of the Self certainly go round and round without reaching Me along the path of transmigration which is fraught with death
3. Those who have no faith in this Dharma O Parantapa return to the path of this world without attaining Me
4. O scorcher of foes Those who have no faith in this Dharma do not attain Me and remain eternally in the cycle of mundane existence fraught with death
5. They who have no faith in this teaching cannot find Me they remain lost in the purlieus of this perishable world
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मय ततमद सरव जगदवयकतमरतन मतसथन सरवभतन न चह तषववसथत 94
Translations:
1. This entire universe is pervaded by Me in an unmanifest form All beings abide in Me but I do not abide in them
2. This whole world is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest form All beings exist in Me but I am not contained within them
3. All of this world is pervaded by Me in My unmanifest aspect all beings exist within Me but I do not dwell within them
4. This entire universe is pervaded by Me having an unmanifest form all beings exist in Me and I do not exist in them
5. The whole world is pervaded by Me yet My form is not visible All living beings have their being in Me yet I am not confined by them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न च मतसथन भतन पशय म यगमशवरम भतभनन च भतसथ ममतम भतभवन 95
Translations:
1. Yet beings do not remain in Me Behold My divine Yoga I am the sustainer of all beings yet I am not in them It is My will alone that causes their existence
2. Neither do the beings dwell in Me Behold My divine Yoga I am the sustainer and originator of beings but My Self is not contained within the beings
3. Nor do beings exist in Me in reality behold My divine Yoga which supports all beings but does not dwell in them is My Self the efficient cause of beings
4. Yet the beings do not exist in Me Look at My Sovereign Yoga My Self is the sustainer of the beings it does not exist in them and causes them to be born
5. Nevertheless they do not consciously abide in Me Such is My Divine Sovereignty that though I the Supreme Self am the cause and upholder of all yet I remain outside
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यथऽऽकशसथत नतय वय सरवतरग महन तथ सरवण भतन मतसथनतयपधरय 96
Translations:
1. As the powerful element air moving everywhere ever remains in the ether know that so too all beings abide in Me
2. Understand thus that just as the voluminous wind moving everywhere is ever present in space similarly all beings abide in Me
3. As the mighty wind moving everywhere always rests in the ether so too know that all beings rest in Me
4. Just as the mighty wind exists in the ether always moving therein everywhere in the same manner all beings exist in Me Be sure of it
5. As the mighty wind though moving everywhere has no resting place except in space so all these beings have no home except in Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवभतन कनतय परकत यनत ममकम कलपकषय पनसतन कलपद वसजमयहम 97
Translations:
1. All beings O Arjuna enter into My Prakriti at the end of a cycle of time again I send them forth at the beginning of a cycle of time
2. O son of Kunti all the beings at the end of a cycle go back to My Prakrti At the beginning of a cycle I project them forth again
3. All beings O Arjuna go into My Nature at the end of a Kalpa I send them forth again at the beginning of the next Kalpa
4. O son of Kunti all beings pass into My nature at the end of the Kalpa the age of universe I send them forth again at the beginning of the next Kalpa
5. All beings O Arjuna return at the end of every cosmic cycle into the realm of Nature which is a part of Me and at the start of the next I send them forth again
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकत सवमवषटभय वसजम पन पन भतगरममम कतसनमवश परकतरवशत 98
Translations:
1. Controlling the Prakṛti which is My own I send forth again and again this multitude of beings helpless under the sway of Prakṛti
2. Keeping My own prakṛti under control I project forth again and again this multitude of beings which are powerless due to the influence of their own nature
3. Animating My Nature I again and again send forth all this multitude of beings helpless under the force of Nature
4. Taking hold of My own nature I again and again send forth this entire host of beings which is powerless under the control of My nature
5. With the help of Nature I again and again pour forth the whole multitude of beings whether they will or not for they are ruled by My will
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न च म तन करमण नबधननत धनञजय उदसनवदसनमसकत तष करमस 99
Translations:
1. But these actions do not bind Me O Arjuna for I remain detached from them as if I were unconcerned
2. O Dhananjaya those actions do not bind Me for I remain unconcerned and unattached to them
3. These acts do not bind Me O Arjuna sitting as one indifferent unattached to those acts
4. O Dhananjaya These acts do not bind me remaining as if unconcerned and unattached in these actions
5. But these acts of mine do not bind me I remain outside and unattached
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मयऽधयकषण परकत सयत सचरचरम हतनऽनन कनतय जगदवपरवरतत 910
Translations:
1. Under My supervision Prakrti gives birth to all mobile and immobile entities and thus O Arjuna the world revolves
2. Under Me as the supervisor the Prakrti produces the world of the moving and the nonmoving things Owing to this O son of Kunti the world revolves
3. Under Me as supervisor Nature produces the moving and the unmoving therefore O Arjuna the world revolves
4. O son of Kunti On account of Me who remain only as an observer and the prime cause My nature gives birth to both the moving and the unmoving hence this world moves in a circle
5. Under my guidance Nature produces all movable and immovable things Thus O Arjuna this universe revolves
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अवजननत म मढ मनष तनमशरतम पर भवमजननत मम भतमहशवरम 911
Translations:
1. Fools disregard Me dwelling in a human form not knowing My higher nature as the Supreme Lord of all beings
2. Not knowing My supreme nature as the Lord of all beings foolish people disregard Me who have taken a human form
3. Fools disregard Me clad in human form not knowing My higher Being as the great Lord of all beings
4. Unaware of My immutable highest Absolute Supreme nature the deluded ones disregard Me dwelling in the human body
5. Fools disregard Me seeing Me clad in human form They do not know that in My higher nature I am the LordGod of all
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मघश मघकरमण मघजञन वचतस रकषसमसर चव परकत महन शरत 912
Translations:
1. Senseless people have a nature that is deluding and similar to that of rakshasas and asuras Their hopes are futile their actions are futile and their knowledge is futile
2. Possessed of vain hopes vain actions vain knowledge and senselessness they become verily possessed of the deceptive disposition of fiends and demons
3. They are possessed of the deceitful nature of demons and undivine beings filled with vain hopes vain actions and vain knowledge that is senseless
4. They are of futile aspirations futile actions futile knowledge and wrong intellect and they take recourse only to the delusive nature that is both demoniac and devilish
5. Their hopes are futile their actions worthless their knowledge ineffective they are without sense deceitful barbaric and godless
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: महतमनसत म परथ दव परकतमशरत भजनतयननयमनस जञतव भतदमवययम 913
Translations:
1. But the greatsouled ones O Arjuna who are associated with My divine nature worship Me with an unwavering mind knowing Me to be the immutable source of all beings
2. O son of Prtha the noble ones being possessed of divine nature surely adore Me with singlemindedness knowing Me to be the immutable source of all objects
3. But the great souls O Arjuna partaking of My divine nature worship Me with a singleminded devotion knowing Me as the imperishable source of all beings
4. O son of Prtha The greatsouled men however taking hold of the divine nature and having nothing else in their minds adore Me viewing Me as the imperishable prime cause of beings
5. But the great souls O Arjuna Filled with my divine spirit they worship me fix their minds on me alone for they know that I am the imperishable source of being
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सतत करतयनत म यतनतशच दढवरत नमसयनतशच म भकतय नतययकत उपसत 914
Translations:
1. Aspiring for eternal communion with Me they worship Me always singing My praises striving with steadfast resolution and bowing down to Me in devotion
2. Always glorifying Me and striving the men of firm vows worship Me by paying obeisance to Me and being ever endowed with devotion
3. Always glorifying Me striving firm in their vows prostrating themselves before Me they worship Me with steadfast devotion
4. Ever speaking of My glory striving with firm resolve paying homage to Me and being permanently endowed with devotion they worship Me
5. Always extolling Me they are strenuous and firm in their vows prostrating themselves before Me and worshiping Me continually with concentrated devotion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जञनयजञन चपयनय यजनत ममपसत एकतवन पथकतवन बहध वशवतमखम 915
Translations:
1. Others besides offering the sacrifice of knowledge also worship Me as One who is characterized by diversity in countless ways and is multiform in My Cosmic aspect
2. Others indeed worship Me by exclusively adoring through the sacrifice of the knowledge of oneness others worship Me in many ways and others as the multiform existing in various forms
3. Others also sacrificing with the wisdomsacrifice worship Me the AllFaced as one distinct and manifold
4. Some worship Me by knowledgesacrifice and others by offering sacrifices thus they worship Me the UniversallyFaced either as One or as Many
5. Others worship Me with full consciousness as the One the Manifold the Omnipresent and the Universal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अह करतरह यजञ सवधऽहमहमषधम मतरऽहमहमवजयमहमगनरह हतम 916
Translations:
1. I am Kratu I am the sacrifice I am the offering to the manes I am the herb I am the Mantra I am the clarified butter I am the fire I am the oblation
2. I am the ritual I am the sacrifice I am the oblation I am the medicinal herb I am the mantra I am the clarified butter I am the fire and I am the act of offering
3. I am Kratu I am Yajna I am the offering to the manes I am the medicinal herbs and all plants I am the Mantra I am also the ghee or melted butter I am the fire I am the oblation
4. I am determination I am sacrifice I am Svadha I am the juice of the herb I am the Vedic hymn I am also the clarified butter I am the sacrificial fire and I am the act of offering
5. I am the oblation the sacrifice and the worship I am the fuel and the chant I am the butter offered to the fire I am the fire itself and I am the act of offering
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पतऽहमसय जगत मत धत पतमह वदय पवतरमकर ऋक सम यजरव च 917
Translations:
1. I am the father mother creator and grandfather of the universe I am the purifier I am the syllable Om as well as Rk Saman and Yajus
2. Of this world I am the father mother ordainer and grandfather I am the knowable the sanctifier the syllable Om as well as the Rk Sama and Yajus

3. I am the father of this world the mother the dispenser of the fruits of actions and the grandfather the one thing to be known the purifier the sacred monosyllable Om and also the Rik Sama and Yajur Vedas
4. I am the father the mother the sustainer and the paternal grandparent of this world I am the sacred object of knowledge the syllable Om the Rk the Saman and the Yajus as well
5. I am the Father of the universe its Mother Nourisher and Grandfather I am the Knowable and the Pure I am Om and I am the Sacred Scriptures
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: गतरभरत परभ सकष नवस शरण सहत परभव परलय सथन नधन बजमवययम 918
Translations:
1. I am the goal the supporter the Lord the witness the abode the refuge and the friend I am the origin the dissolution the basis for preservation and the imperishable seed
2. I am the fruit of actions the nourisher the Lord witness abode refuge friend origin end foundation store and the imperishable seed
3. I am the goal the supporter the Lord the witness the abode the shelter the friend the origin the dissolution the foundation the treasurehouse and the imperishable seed
4. I am the method the nourisher the lord the witness the abode the refuge the goodhearted friend the origin the dissolution the sustenance the repository and the imperishable seed of the world
5. I am the goal the sustainer the Lord the witness the home the shelter the lover and the origin I am life and death I am the fountain and the imperishable seed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तपमयहमह वरष नगहणमयतसजम च अमत चव मतयशच सदसचचहमरजन 919
Translations:
1. I give heat I withhold and send forth the rain I am immortality and also death O Arjuna I am the being and the nonbeing
2. O Arjuna I give heat I withhold and pour down rain I am verily the nectar and also death existence and nonexistence
3. As the sun I give heat I withhold and send forth the rain I am immortality and also death existence and nonexistence O Arjuna
4. I give heat I withhold and also send forth rains I am immortality and also death the real and the unreal O Arjuna
5. I am the heat of the sun I release and withhold the rain I am death and immortality I am being and nonbeing
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तरवदय म समप पतपप यजञरषटव सवरगत पररथयनत त पणयमसदय सरनदरलक मशननत दवयनदव दवभगन 920
Translations:

1. Those who are versed in the three Vedas having been purified from sin by drinking the Soma juice pray for passage to heaven and worship Me through sacrifices Reaching the holy realm of the chief of the gods they enjoy celestial pleasures in heaven

2. Those who are versed in the Vedas who drink Soma and are purified of sin pray for the heavenly goal by worshipping Me through sacrifices Having reached the place world of the king of gods which is the result of righteousness they enjoy the divine pleasure of the gods in heaven

3. The knowers of the three Vedas the drinkers of Soma purified of all sins worshipping Me through sacrifices pray for the way to heaven they reach the holy world of the Lord of the gods and enjoy the divine pleasures of the gods in heaven

4. The masters of the three Vedas the somadrinkers purified of their sins aspire for the heavenly goal by offering sacrifices to Me They attain the meritorious world of the Lord of Gods and taste in the heavens the heavenly pleasures of the gods

5. Those who are versed in the scriptures who drink the mystic Somajuice and are purified from sin and who while worshipping Me with sacrifices pray that I will lead them to heaven they reach the holy world where the Controller of the Powers of Nature resides and they enjoy the feasts of Paradise
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: त त भकतव सवरगलक वशल कषण पणय मरतयलक वशनत एव तरयधरममनपरपनन गतगत कमकम लभनत 921
Translations:
1. Having enjoyed the spacious world of heaven they return to the world of mortals their merit having been exhausted Thus those who follow the Vedic rituals and are drawn by desires come and go

2. After having enjoyed that vast heavenly world they enter into the human world upon the exhaustion of their merit Thus those who follow the rites and duties prescribed in the three Vedas and are desirous of pleasures attain the state of coming and going

3. They having enjoyed the vast heaven enter the world of mortals when their merit is exhausted thus abiding by the injunctions of the three Vedas and desiring objects of desires they attain to the state of coming and going

4. Having enjoyed that vast world of heaven they when their merit is exhausted enter the world of mortals Thus those who long for pleasure and continuously take refuge in the code of conduct prescribed by the Three Vedas attain the state of going and coming

5. Yet although they enjoy the spacious glories of Paradise when their merit is exhausted they are born again into this world of mortals They have followed the letter of the scriptures yet because they have sought to fulfill their own desires they must depart and return again and again
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अननयशचनतयनत म य जन परयपसत तष नतयभयकतन यगकषम वहमयहम 922
Translations:
1. Those who excluding all else think of Me and worship Me aspiring after eternal union with Me I look after their prosperity and welfare Yoga and Ksema
2. Those persons who becoming nondifferent from Me and meditating worship Me everywhere for them who are ever attached to Me I arrange for securing what they lack and preserving what they have
3. For those men who worship Me alone thinking of no one else for those everunited I secure what they have not already possessed and preserve what they already possess

4. Those men who having nothing else as their goal worship Me everywhere and are constantly thinking of Me alone to them who are fully and constantly attached to Me I bear acquisition and the security of acquisition
5. But if a person meditates on Me and Me alone worships Me always and everywhere I will take upon Myself the fulfillment of their aspiration and I will safeguard whatever they attain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यऽपयनयदवत भकत यजनत शरदधयऽनवत तऽप ममव कनतय यजनतयवधपरवकम 923
Translations:
1. Even those who are devoted to other divinities with faith in their hearts worship Me alone O Arjuna though not in accordance with the Sastras
2. Even those who being devoted to other deities and endowed with faith worship them they also O son of Kunti worship Me alone though following the wrong method
3. Even those devotees who endowed with faith worship other gods worship Me alone O Arjuna but by the wrong method
4. O son of Kunti Even those who are devotees of other gods and worship them with faith worship Me alone but following noninjunctive practices
5. Even those who worship the lesser powers if they do so with faith they are thereby worshipping Me though not in the correct manner
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अह ह सरवयजञन भकत च परभरव च न त ममभजननत तततवनतशचयवनत त 924
Translations:
1. For I am the only enjoyer and the only Lord of all sacrifices They do not recognize Me in My true nature hence they fall
2. Indeed I am the enjoyer and the Lord of all sacrifices but they do not know Me truly Therefore they fall
3. For I alone am the enjoyer and Lord of all sacrifices but they do not know Me in reality and thus they return to this mortal world
4. For I am the enjoyer and the Lord of all sacrifices yet they do not recognize Me correctly and thus they turn away
5. I am the willing recipient of sacrifice and I am its true Lord But they do not know me truly so they fall back
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यनत दववरत दवन पतनयनत पतवरत भतन यनत भतजय यनत मदयजनऽप मम 925
Translations:
1. Devotees of gods go to the gods manesworshippers go to the manes worshippers of Bhutas go to the Bhutas and those who worship Me come to Me
2. Those who worship the gods reach the gods the worshippers of the manes go to the manes the devotees of the Beings reach the Beings and those who worship Me come to Me
3. The worshippers of the gods go to them the ancestorworshippers go to the manes the worshippers of the deities who preside over the elements go to them but My devotees come to Me

4. The devotees of the gods attain the gods the devotees of the ancestors attain the ancestors those who perform sacrifices for the spirits attain the spirits and those who perform sacrifices for Me attain Me

5. The votaries of the lesser powers go to them the devotees of spirits go to them they who worship the powers of darkness to such powers shall they go and so too those who worship me shall come to me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पतर पषप फल तय य म भकतय परयचछत तदह भकतयपहतमशनम परयततमन 926
Translations:
1. Whoever offers Me with true devotion a leaf a flower a fruit or some water I accept this offering made with devotion by them who are pure of heart
2. Whoever offers Me with devotiona leaf a flower a fruit or waterI accept that gift of the purehearted man which has been presented devotionally
3. Whoever offers Me with devotion a leaf a flower a fruit or a little water that so offered devotedly by the pureminded I accept
4. Whoever offers Me a leaf a flower a fruit or even a little water with devotion I accept that offering of devotion from one who has a wellcontrolled mind
5. Whatever a person offers to Me be it a leaf flower fruit or water I accept it for it is offered with devotion and a pure mind
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतकरष यदशनस यजजहष ददस यत यततपसयस कनतय ततकरषव मदरपणम 927
Translations:
1. Whatever you do whatever you eat whatever you offer whatever you give away whatever austerity you practice O Arjuna do that as an offering to Me
2. O son of Kunti whatever you do whatever you eat whatever you offer as a sacrifice whatever you give and whatever austerities you undertakeall of that you offer to Me
3. Whatever you do whatever you eat whatever you offer in sacrifice whatever you give whatever austerity you practice O Arjuna do it as an offering to Me
4. Whatever you do whatever you eat whatever oblation you offer whatever gift you make and whatever austerity you perform O son of Kunti do that as an offering to Me
5. Whatever you do whatever you eat whatever you sacrifice and give whatever austerities you practice do all as an offering to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शभशभफलरव मकषयस करमबनधन सनयसयगयकततम वमकत ममपषयस 928
Translations:
1. Thus equipped in mind with the Yoga of renunciation you will free yourself from the bonds of Karma productive of both auspicious and inauspicious fruits Thus liberated you will come to Me

2. Thus you will become free from bondage in the form of actions which are productive of good and bad results Having your mind imbued with the yoga of renunciation and becoming free you will attain Me
3. Thus you shall be freed from the bonds of actions yielding good and evil fruits with the mind steadfast in the Yoga of renunciation and liberated you shall come to Me
4. Thus you shall be freed from the good and evil results which are the actionbonds Having your innate nature immersed in the Yoga of renunciation and thus being fully liberated you shall attain Me
5. So your action will have no result either good or bad but through the spirit of renunciation you will come to Me and be free
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: समऽह सरवभतष न म दवषयऽसत न परय य भजनत त म भकतय मय त तष चपयहम 929
Translations:
1. I am the same to all creation there is none hateful or dear to me But those who worship me with devotion abide in me and I in them
2. I am impartial towards all beings to Me there is none detestable or none dear But those who worship Me with devotion they exist in Me and I too exist in them
3. I am the same to all beings there is none hateful or dear to Me but those who worship Me with devotion are in Me and I am also in them
4. I am the same in all beings none is hateful to Me and none is dear but whoever worships Me with devotion they are in Me and I am in them
5. I am the same to all beings I do not favour any nor do I hate any However those who worship Me devotedly they live in Me and I in them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अप चतसदरचर भजत ममननयभक सधरव स मनतवय समयगवयवसत ह स 930
Translations:
1. If even the most sinful man worships Me with undivided devotion he should be regarded as holy for he has rightly resolved
2. Even if a man of very bad conduct worships Me with singleminded devotion he is to be considered truly good for he has rightly resolved
3. Even if the most sinful worships Me with devotion to no one else he should indeed be regarded as righteous for he has rightly resolved
4. Even if an incorrigible evildoer worships Me not resorting to any other goal he should be deemed to be righteous for he has properly undertaken his task
5. Even the most sinful if he worships Me with his whole heart shall be considered righteous for he is treading the right path
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कषपर भवत धरमतम शशवचछनत नगचछत कनतय परतजनह न म भकत परणशयत 931
Translations:
1. Quickly he becomes righteous and obtains everlasting peace Affirm on My behalf O Arjuna that My devotee never perishes
2. He soon becomes possessed of a virtuous mind he attains everlasting peace Do you proclaim boldly O son of Kunti that My devotee will not be ruined
3. Soon he becomes righteous and attains eternal peace O Arjuna proclaim thou for certain that My devotee never perishes
4. Quickly he becomes righteousminded and attains permanent peace O son of Kunti I swear that my devotee never gets lost
5. He will soon attain spirituality and eternal peace O Arjuna Believe me my devotee is never lost
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: म ह परथ वयपशरतय यऽप सय पपयनय सतरय वशयसतथ शदरसतऽप यनत पर गतम 932

1. By taking refuge in Me even those of evil birth women Vaisyas and Sudras attain the supreme state

2. For O son of Prtha even those who are born of sinwomen Vaisyas as well as SudrasSs construction of this portion is women Vaisyas as well as Sudras and even others who are born of sin ie those who are born low and are of vile deeds viz Mlecchas Pukkasas and others MS also takes papayonayah born of sin as a separate phrase and classifies women and others only as those darred from Vedic study etcTreven they reach the highest Goal by taking shelter under Me

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: क पनरबरहमण पणय भकत रजरषयसतथ अनतयमसख लकमम परपय भजसव मम 933
Translations:
1. How much more then for the pure Brahmanas and royal sages who are My devotees Having obtained this transient and joyless world they should worship Me
2. What of the holy Brahmanas and devoted wise sages Having come to this fleeting miserable world worship Me
3. How much more easily then do Brahmins and devoted royal saints attain the goal Having come to this impermanent and unhappy world do thou worship Me
4. Certainly it should be so in the case of the pious men of the priestly class and devoted royal seers Having come to this transient and joyless world you should be devoted to Me
5. What need is there then to mention the holy ministers of God the devotees and the saintly rulers Therefore do you born in this changing and miserable world also worship Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 9.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 9.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

Translations:
1. Focus your mind on Me be My devotee worship Me Bow down to Me Engaging your mind in this manner and regarding Me as the supreme goal you will come to Me
2. Having your mind fixed on Me be devoted to Me sacrifice to Me and bow down to Me By concentrating your mind and accepting Me as the supreme goal you shall surely attain Me who am thus the Self
3. Fix your mind on Me be devoted to Me sacrifice to Me bow down to Me having thus united your whole self to Me taking Me as the supreme goal you will come to Me
4. Fix your mind on Me be devoted to Me offer sacrifice to Me pay homage to Me Thus having your self fixed and Me as your supreme goal you will certainly attain Me
5. Fix your mind on Me devote yourself to Me sacrifice for Me surrender to Me make Me the object of your aspirations and you shall assuredly become one with Me Who am your own Self
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

4. The Bhagavat said O mightyarmed Arjuna Listen to My best message once more which I shall declare to you with good intention for you are dear to Me

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

3. He who truly knows these manifold manifestations of My Being and this Yogapower of Mine becomes established in unshakable Yoga there is no doubt about it
4. He who knows correctly this extensively manifesting power and My Yogic powerhe is endowed with unwavering Yoga there is no doubt about it
5. He who rightly understands My manifested glory and My creative power attains perfect peace without a doubt
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अह सरवसय परभव मतत सरव परवरतत इत मतव भजनत म बध भवसमनवत 108
Translations:
1. I am the origin of all from me everything proceeds Thinking thus the wise worship me with all devotion Bhava
2. I am the origin of all everything moves on due to Me Realizing this the wise ones filled with fervor adore Me
3. I am the source of all from me everything evolves Understanding this the wise endowed with meditation worship me
4. He is the source of all and from Him all comes forth Thus viewing the wise revere Me with devotion
5. I am the source of all from me everything flows Therefore the wise worship me with an unchanging devotion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मचचतत मदगतपरण बधयनत परसपरम कथयनतशच म नतय तषयनत च रमनत च 109
Translations:
1. With their minds focused on Me their pranas centered in Me inspiring one another and always speaking of Me they live in contentment and bliss at all times
2. With their minds fixed on Me dedicating their lives to Me enlightening one another and always speaking of Me they derive satisfaction and rejoice
3. With their minds and lives wholly absorbed in Me they enlighten each other and ever speak of Me being satisfied and delighted
4. Having their minds fixed on Me having their lives gone into Me enlightening one another and constantly talking about Me they are pleased and delighted
5. With minds concentrated on Me with lives absorbed in Me and enlightening one another they ever feel content and happy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तष सततयकतन भजत परतपरवकम ददम बदधयग त यन ममपयनत त 1010
Translations:
1. To those who are ceaselessly united with Me and who worship Me with immense love I lovingly grant that mental disposition Buddhiyoga which leads them to Me
2. To those who are ever devoted and worship Me with love I grant the possession of wisdom that leads them to Me
3. To those who are ever steadfast worshipping me with love I give the yoga of discrimination by which they come to me
4. To these persons who thus mingle with Me and revere Me with love I grant the knowledgeYoga by which they can reach Me
5. To those who are always devout and who worship Me with love I give the power of discrimination that leads them to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 10.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

Translations:
1. Out of compassion for them alone I abiding in their mental activity as its object dispel the darkness born of ignorance with the brilliant lamp of knowledge
2. Out of compassion for them alone I residing in their hearts destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge
3. Out of mere compassion for them I dwelling within their selves destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge
4. Out of compassion only towards these men I who remain as their very Self destroy with the shining light of wisdom their darkness born of ignorance
5. By My grace I live in their hearts and I dispel the darkness of ignorance with the shining light of wisdom
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.12 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.14 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.15 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

Translations:

2. O Supreme Person Creator of beings Lord of beings God of gods Lord of the worlds You alone know Yourself by Yourself
3. Verily Thou Thyself knowest Thyself by Thyself O Supreme Person O source and Lord of all beings O God of gods O ruler of the world
4. Only yourself know yourself by yourself O Supreme Purusha Creator of all beings Lord of beings God of gods Lord of the universe
5. You alone know Yourself by the power of Your Self You are the Supreme Spirit the Source and Master of all being the Lord of Lords the Ruler of the Universe
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.16 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वकतमरहसयशषण दवय हयतमवभतय यभरवभतभरलकनमसतव वयपय तषठस 1016

2. Be pleased to speak in full of Your own divine manifestations through which You exist pervading these worlds
3. You should indeed tell without reserve of your divine glories by which you exist pervading all these worlds No one else can do so
4. You alone are capable of fully declaring the auspicious manifesting powers of Yours through which manifesting power You remain pervading these worlds
5. Please tell me about all of Your glorious manifestations through which You pervade the world
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.17 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

3. How shall I ever meditating know you O Yogin In what aspects or things O blessed Lord should I think of you
4. O Mighty Yogin How should I know you meditating on you In what various forms O Bhagavat should I contemplate you

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 10.18 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 10.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

Sanskrit: मदनगरहय परम गहयमधयतमसजञतम यततवयकत वचसतन महऽय वगत मम 111
Translations:
1. Arjuna said To show favor to me You have told me that most profound mystery concerning the Self by that this delusion of mine has been dispelled
2. Arjuna said This delusion of mine has departed as a result of that speech which is most secret and pertains to the Self uttered by You for my benefit
3. Arjuna said By this explanation of the highest secret concerning the Self which Thou hast spoken for the sake of blessing me my delusion has been dispelled
4. Arjuna said My delusion has completely gone thanks to the great and mysterious discourse which is termed as a science governing the soul and which You have delivered to me as a favor
5. Arjuna said My Lord Your words concerning the Supreme Secret of Self given for my blessing have dispelled the illusions that surrounded me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.2 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

3. The origin and destruction of beings have been heard in detail from You O lotuseyed Lord and also Your inexhaustible greatness

5. O Lord whose eyes are like the lotus petal You have described in detail the origin and dissolution of beings and Your own Eternal Majesty
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.3 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

4. As You describe Yourself to be the Supreme Lord it must be so O Supreme Self I desire to behold Your divine form

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.4 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मनयस यद तचछकय मय दरषटमत परभ यगशवर तत म तव दरशयऽतमनमवययम 114

4. O Master If you think it is possible for me to behold that form then O Lord of the Yogis please show me Your Immortal Self
5. If you think it is possible for me to see it show me O Lord of Lords your eternal self
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

4. The Bhagavat said Behold O son of Prtha My divine forms in hundreds and thousands of varied nature colors and shapes

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इहकसथ जगतकतसन पशयदय सचरचरम मम दह गडकश यचचनयददरषटमचछस 117

1. Behold here O Arjuna the entire universe with its mobile and immobile things all centered in My body and whatever else you desire to see
2. See now O Gudakesa the entire Universe together with the moving and the nonmoving concentrated in the same place here in My body as also whatever else you would like to see
3. Now behold O Arjuna in this My body the entire universe centered in one including the moving and the unmoving and whatever else you desire to see
4. Now behold the entire universe including the moving and the unmoving and whatsoever else you desire to seeall established in Me O Gudakesa Arjuna
5. Here living in Me O Arjuna behold the entire universe both movable and immovable and anything else you would like to see
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न त म शकयस दरषटमननव सवचकषष दवय ददम त चकष पशय म यगमशवरम 118

1. But you will not be able to see Me with your own eyes I give you a divine eye so behold My Lordly Yoga
2. But you are not able to see Me with just this eye of yours I grant you the divine eye behold My divine Yoga
3. But you are not able to behold Me with these your own eyes I give you the divine eye behold My lordly Yoga
4. But you cannot see Me with just this eye of yours therefore I give you the divine eye Now behold My divine form
5. Yet since you cannot see Me with mortal eyes behold I give you divine sight Now see the glory of My sovereignty
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.9 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.10 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.11 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.12 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दव सरयसहसरसय भवदयगपदतथत यद भ सदश स सयदभससतसय महतमन 1112

2. Should the effulgence of a thousand suns blaze forth simultaneously in the sky that might be akin to the radiance of that exalted One

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.13 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

2. At that time the Pandava saw in the body of the God of gods the whole diversely differentiated universe united in one
3. There in the body of the God of gods Arjuna then saw the entire universe resting in one with its myriad of divisions

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.14 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

4. Then possessed by amazement and with his body hair standing on end Dhananjaya with his head bowed to the God and with folded hands spoke to Him

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.15 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

4. Arjuna said O God In Your body I behold all gods and hosts of different kinds of beingsthe Lord Brahma seated on the lotusseat and all the sages and glowing serpents

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.16 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

2. I see You with numerous arms bellies mouths and eyes with infinite forms all around O Lord of the Universe O Cosmic Person I see no limit no middle nor beginning
3. I see You with boundless form on every side with many arms stomachs mouths and eyes neither the end nor the middle nor the beginning do I see O Lord of the Universe O Cosmic Form
4. I behold You with many arms bellies mouths and eyes and with infinite forms on all sides I find neither the end nor the center nor the beginning of You O Lord of the Universe O UniversalFormed One
5. I see You infinite in form with faces eyes and limbs everywhere with no beginning middle or end O You Lord of the Universe whose form is universal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.17 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

1. I behold You as a mass of light shining everywhere with a diadem mace and discus hard to behold blazing like a burning fire and the sun and immeasurable

2. I see You wearing a diadem wielding a mace and holding a disc a mass of brilliance glowing all around difficult to look at from all sides possessing the radiance of the blazing fire and sun and immeasurable
3. I see You with the diadem club and discus a mass of radiance shining everywhere very hard to look at blazing all around like a burning fire and the sun and immeasurable
4. I behold You as having crowns clubs and discs as a mass of radiance shining on all sides hard to look at and blazing like burning fire and the sun on each side and as an immeasurable one
5. I see you with the crown the scepter and the discus a blaze of splendor Scarcely can I gaze upon you so radiant you are glowing like a blazing fire brilliant as the sun immeasurable
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.18 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

Translations:

1. You are the Imperishable Supreme One to be realized You are the Supreme Substratum of this universe You are immutable the guardian of the eternal law I know You are the Supreme Person who is everlasting

2. You are the Unchanging the supreme One to be known You are the most perfect repository of this Universe You are the Imperishable the Protector of the everexisting religion You are the eternal Person This is my belief

3. You are the Imperishable the Supreme Being worthy of being known You are the great treasurehouse of this universe You are the imperishable protector of the eternal Dharma You are the Primal Person I believe

4. You are the imperishable the Supreme Being to be known You are the ultimate place of rest for this universe You are changeless and the guardian of the pious acts of the Satvatas You are the everlasting Soul I believe
5. You are imperishable the only one worthy of being known the priceless treasurehouse of the universe the immortal guardian of eternal life and the everlasting spirit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.19 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनदमधयनतमननतवरय मननतबह शशसरयनतरम पशयम तव दपतहतशवकतरम सवतजस वशवमद तपनतम 1119
Translations:

1. I behold You as having no beginning middle or end Your might is infinite You are endowed with an innumerable number of arms The sun and moon are Your eyes and Your mouth emits burning fire With Your own radiance You are warning the entire universe

2. I see You as having no beginning middle or end possessing infinite valor with innumerable arms the sun and moon as eyes a mouth like a blazing fire and heating up this universe with Your own brilliance
3. I see You without beginning middle or end infinite in power with endless arms the sun and moon as Your eyes the burning fire Your mouth heating the entire universe with Your radiance

4. I observe You with no beginning no middle and no end having infinite creative power and infinite arms with the moon and the sun as Your eyes and the blazing fire as Your mouth and scorching this universe with Your radiance

5. Without beginning without middle and without end infinite in power with arms allembracing the sun and moon as Your eyes Your face beaming with the fire of sacrifice flooding the whole universe with light
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.20 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दयवपथवयरदमनतर ह वयपत तवयकन दशशच सरव दषटवऽदभत रपमगर तवद लकतरय परवयथत महतमन 1120
Translations:
1. You alone have pervaded the interspace between heaven and earth and all the quarters Beholding your marvelous and terrible form O Mahatman the three worlds are greatly overwhelmed with fear

2. Indeed this intermediate space between heaven and earth as well as all the directions are pervaded by You alone O exalted One the three worlds are struck with fear upon seeing this strange and fearful form of Yours

3. This space between the earth and the heavens and all the quarters is filled by You alone having seen this Your wonderful and terrible form the three worlds are trembling with fear O greatsouled Being
4. This space between heaven and earth as well as all the directions is pervaded singly by You seeing this wondrous form of Yours O Exalted Soul the triple world is greatly frightened
5. Alone you fill all the quarters of the sky earth and heaven and the regions between O Almighty Lord Seeing your marvelous and aweinspiring form the spheres tremble with fear
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.21 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अम ह तव सरसङघ वशनत कचदभत परञजलय गणनत सवसततयकतव महरषसदधसङघ सतवनत तव सततभ पषकलभ 1121
Translations:
1. Verily the hosts of Devas enter into You Some in fear extol You with clasped hands crying Hail The bands of great seers and Siddhas praise You with meaningful hymns
2. Those very groups of gods enter into You struck with fear some extol You with joined palms Groups of great sages and perfected beings praise You with elaborate hymns saying May it be well
3. Verily these hosts of gods enter into Thee some extol Thee with joined palms in fear saying May it be well Bands of great sages and perfected ones praise Thee with complete hymns
4. These hosts of gods enter into You some frightened recite hymns with folded palms simply crying Hail the hosts of great seers praise You with excellent praising hymns
5. The troops of celestial beings enter into You some invoking You in fear with folded palms the great seers and adepts sing hymns to Your glory saying All hail
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.22 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रदरदतय वसव य च सधय वशवऽशवन मरतशचषमपशच गनधरवयकषसरसदधसङघ वकषनत तव वसमतशचव सरव 1122
Translations:
1. The Rudras the Adityas the Vasus the Sadhyas the Visvas the Asvins the Maruts and the Manes as well as the hosts of Gandharvas Yaksas Asuras and Siddhasall gaze upon You in amazement

2. Those who are the Rudras the Adityas the Vasus and the Sadhyas the Visvedevas the two Asvins the Maruts and the Usmapas and hosts of Gandharvas Yaksas demons and Siddhasall of those very ones gaze at You being indeed struck with wonder
3. The Rudras Adityas Vasus Sadhyas Visvedevas the two Asvins Maruts the Manus and the hosts of celestial singers Yakshas demons and the perfected ones all look upon Thee with great amazement

4. The Rudras the Adityas the Vasus the Sadhyas the Visvadevas the twin Asvins and the Maruts the Manes the hosts of the Gandharvas the Yaksas the demons and the perfected onesall gaze upon You in amazement

5. The vital forces major stars fire earth air sky sun heaven moon and planets the angels guardians of the universe divine healers winds fathers heavenly singers and hosts of mammonworshippers demons and saints are all amazed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.23 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रप महतत बहवकतरनतर महबह बहबहरपदम बहदर बहदषटरकरल दषटव लक परवयथतसतथऽहम 1123
Translations:
1. Beholding Your great form with many mouths and eyes many arms thighs and feet many stomachs and terrible with many teeth the worlds tremble and I too quake O mightyarmed one
2. O MightyArmed One seeing Your immense form with many mouths eyes arms thighs feet bellies and teeth the creatures are struck with terror and so am I
3. Having seen Your immeasurable form with many mouths and eyes O mightyarmed one with many arms thighs and feet with many stomachs and fearsome with many teeth the worlds are terrified and so am I
4. O Mightyarmed One Having seen Your mighty form with its many faces and eyes many arms thighs feet and bellies and its terrible array of tusks the worlds are frightened as am I
5. Seeing Thy stupendous Form O Most Mighty One with its myriad faces its innumerable eyes limbs and terrible jaws I myself and all the worlds are overwhelmed with awe
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.24 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नभसपश दपतमनकवरण वयततनन दपतवशलनतरम दषटव ह तव परवयथतनतरतम धत न वनदम शम च वषण 1124
Translations:
1. When I behold You touching the Supreme Heaven shining multicolored with yawning mouths and large resplendent eyes my inner being trembles in fear I am unable to find support or peace O Vishnu
2. O Vishnu indeed upon seeing Your form touching the heavens blazing with many colors openmouthed with fiery large eyes I become terrified in my mind and do not find steadiness and peace
3. On seeing Thee touching the sky shining in many colors with mouths wide open with large fiery eyes I am terrified at heart and find neither courage nor peace O Vishnu
4. As I observe You touching the sky blazing with many colors mouths wide open eyes blazing and large I am terrified in my inner soul and I do not find courage and peace O Visnu
5. When I see You touching the heavens glowing with color with open mouth and wide fiery eyes I am terrified O my Lord My courage and peace of mind abandon me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.25 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दषटरकरलन च त मखन दषटवव कलनलसननभन दश न जन न लभ च शरम परसद दवश जगननवस 1125
Translations:

1. Viewing Your mouths presenting awegenerating fangs and looking like the consuming fire of final destruction I know not the quarters of the globe nor do I find repose Be gracious O Lord of the Devas O Abode of the universe

2. Having merely seen Your mouths made terrible with their teeth and resembling the fire of Dissolution I have lost my sense of direction and find no comfort Be gracious O Lord of gods O Abode of the Universe
3. Having seen Thy mouths fearful with teeth blazing like the fires of cosmic dissolution I know not the four quarters nor do I find peace Have mercy O Lord of the gods O abode of the universe
4. By merely seeing Your faces which are frightening with tusks and look like the fire of destruction I do not know the arters and get no peace Have mercy O Lord of gods O Abode of the Universe
5. When I see Thy mouths with their fearful jaws like glowing fires at the dissolution of creation I lose all sense of place and find no rest Be merciful O Lord in whom this universe abides
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.26 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.27 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.28 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

3. Verily just as many torrents of rivers flow towards the ocean so too these heroes in the world of men enter Thy flaming mouths

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.29 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यथ परदपत जवलन पतङग वशनत नशय समदधवग तथव नशय वशनत लक सतवप वकतरण समदधवग 1129
Translations:
1. As moths rush swiftly into a blazing fire leading to their destruction so do these men swiftly rush into Your mouths leading to their destruction
2. As moths hasten with increased speed into a blazing fire for destruction so too do creatures hasten into Your mouths with increased speed for destruction
3. As moths hurriedly rush into a blazing fire leading to their own destruction so too these creatures hurry into Your mouths leading to their own destruction
4. Just as moths with full speed enter into the flaming fire for their own destruction in the same manner the worlds also enter with full speed into the mouths of Yours for their own destruction
5. As moths impetuously fly towards the flame only to be killed so these men rush into Your mouths to court their own destruction
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.30 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.31 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

2. Tell me who You are fierce in form Salutations to You O Supreme God be gracious I desire to fully know You who are the Prime One For I do not understand Your actions
3. Tell me who you are so fierce of form I offer my salutations to you O God Supreme have mercy on me I desire to know you the original Being I do not indeed know your workings

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कलऽसम लककषयकतपरवदध लकनसमहरतमह परवतत ऋतऽप तव न भवषयनत सरव यऽवसथत परतयनकष यध 1132
Translations:
1. The Lord said I am the worlddestroying Time Manifesting myself fully I have begun to destroy the worlds here Even without you none of the warriors arrayed in the hostile ranks will survive

2. The Blessed Lord said I am the worlddestroying Time grown in stature and now engaged in annihilating the creatures Even without you all the warriors arrayed in the confronting armies will cease to exist
3. The Blessed Lord said I am the fullgrown worlddestroying Time now engaged in destroying the worlds Even without you none of the warriors arrayed in the hostile armies will live

4. The Bhagavat said I am Time the worlddestroyer engaged here in withdrawing the worlds that are overgrown Even without you your fighting all the warriors standing in the rival armies would cease to be

5. Lord Shri Krishna replied I have shown myself to you as the Destroyer who lays waste the world and whose purpose is destruction In spite of your efforts all these warriors gathered for battle shall not escape death
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

Translations:
1. Therefore arise win glory Conquering your foes enjoy a prosperous kingdom By Me they have already been slain You be merely an instrument O Arjuna you great archer
2. Therefore rise up and gain fame and defeating the enemies enjoy a prosperous kingdom These have been killed by Me even earlier be you merely an instrument O Savyasacin Arjuna

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.35 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.35 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

Translations:
1. Sanjaya said Having heard this speech of Krsna Arjuna did Him obeisance and trembling with awe he bowed down again and with folded palms his voice choked with emotion he spoke to Krsna

2. Sanjaya said Hearing this utterance of Kesava Kiriti Arjuna with joined palms and trembling prostrated himself and then with a faltering voice bowed down and overcome by fits of fear said again to Krsna
3. Sanjaya said Having heard that speech of Lord Krishna Arjuna with joined palms trembling prostrated himself again addressing Krishna in a choked voice bowing down overwhelmed with fear

4. Sanjaya said On hearing this speech of Kesava the crownedprince Arjuna had his palms folded trembling he prostrated himself to Krsna stammering and very much afraid he bowed down and spoke to Him again

5. Sanjaya continued Having heard these words from the Lord Shri Krishna the Prince Arjuna with folded hands trembling prostrated himself and with a choked voice bowing down again and again and overwhelmed with awe once more addressed the Lord
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.36 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.36 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.40 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

Translations:
1. Unaware of Your majesty and either out of negligence or love or considering You to be a friend whatever I have said rudely such as O Krsna O Yadava O Comrade
2. Without knowing Your greatness whatever I said rashly inadvertently or even out of familiarity thinking You to be a friend addressing You as O Krsna O Yadava O friend etc
3. Whatever I have presumptuously said from carelessness or love addressing You as O Krishna O Yadava O Friend regarding You merely as a friend unknowing of Your greatness

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.42 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

Translations:

1. And whatever disrespect has been shown to You in jest while playing resting sitting or eating whether alone or in the presence of others O AcyutaI implore You for forgiveness You who are incomprehensible

2. And that You have been discourteously treated out of funwhile walking while on a bed while on a seat while eating in privacy or O Acyuta even in publicfor that I beg pardon of You the incomprehensible One
3. In whatever way I may have insulted You for the sake of fun while at play reposing sitting or at meals when alone with You O Krishna or in company that I implore You immeasurable one to forgive

4. Whatever disrespect I have shown to You making fun of You in the course of play or while on the bed or on the seat or at meals either alone or in the presence of respectable personsfor that I beg pardon of You the Unconceivable One O Acyuta
5. Whatever insults I have offered to You in jest in sport in repose in conversation or at a banquet alone or in a crowd I ask for Your forgiveness for them all O You Who are without equal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.43 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पतस लकसय चरचरसय तवमसय पजयशच गररगरयन न तवतसमऽसतयभयधक कतऽनय लकतरयऽपयपरतमपरभव 1143
Translations:

1. You are the father of this world of all that moves and does not move You are its teacher and the one most worthy of reverence There is none equal to You How then could there be in the three worlds another greater than You O Being of matchless greatness

2. You are the Father of all beings both moving and nonmoving to You the Teacher are they worthy of worship and You are greater than a teacher There is none equal to You how can there be anyone greater even in all the three worlds O You of unrivaled power

3. Thou art the Father of this world both moving and unmoving Thou art to be adored by this world Thou the greatest Guru for none exists who is equal to Thee how then could there be another superior to Thee in the three worlds O Being of unrivaled power

4. You are the father of the world of the moving and the unmoving you are the great preceptor of this universe in the triad of worlds there is no one equal to youhow can there be anyone else superior having greatness not comprehended

5. For you are the Father of all things movable and immovable the Worshipful the Master of Masters In all the worlds there is none equal to you how then could there be one superior O you who stand alone Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.44 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.44 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमतपरणमय परणधय कय परसदय तवमहमशमडयम पतव पतरसय सखव सखय परय परययरहस दव सढम 1144
Translations:

1. Therefore bowing down and prostrating my body I implore Your mercy O adorable Lord As a father listens to his son or a friend to his friend it is fitting O Lord that You who are dear to me should bear with me who am dear to You

2. Therefore by bowing down and prostrating my body I seek to propitiate You who are God and are adorable O Lord You should forgive my faults as a father would forgive the faults of a son as a friend would forgive the faults of a friend and as a lover would forgive the faults of a beloved
3. Therefore bowing down and prostrating my body I crave Thy forgiveness O adorable Lord As a father forgives his son a friend his dear friend a lover his beloved even so may Thou forgive me O God

4. Hence paying homage and prostrating my body I solicit grace from You the praiseworthy Lord O God Be pleased to bear with me just as a beloved father would with his beloved son and just as a dear friend would with his dear friend

5. Therefore I prostrate myself before You O Lord Most Adorable I salute You and I ask for Your blessing Only You can be trusted to bear with me like a father to a son like a friend to a friend and like a lover to his beloved
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.45 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.45 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

5. I rejoice that I have seen what no man has ever seen before yet O Lord I am overwhelmed with fear Please take on the form I know Be merciful O Lord You who are the home of the entire universe
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.46 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.46 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
