5. I long to see Thee as Thou wert before with the crown the scepter and the discus in Thy hands in Thy other form with Thy four hands O Thou whose arms are countless and whose forms are infinite
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.47 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.47 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मय परसननन तवरजनद रप पर दरशतमतमयगत तजमय वशवमननतमदय यनम तवदनयन न दषटपरवम 1147
Translations:
1. The Lord said By My grace O Arjuna this Supreme Form luminous universal infinite and primalnever seen before by anyone but youhas been revealed to you through My own free will

2. The Blessed Lord said Out of grace O Arjuna this supreme radiant cosmic infinite primeval formwhich no one other than you has seen beforehas been shown to you by Me through the power of My own Yoga

3. The Blessed Lord said O Arjuna this Cosmic Form has graciously been shown to you by Me through My own Yogic power It is full of splendour primeval and infinite this Cosmic Form of Mine has never been seen before by anyone other than you

4. The Bhagavat said Being gracious towards you I have shown you O Arjuna this supreme form as a result of your concentration on the Self This form of Mine full of universal splendour unending and primal has never been seen before by anyone other than yourself

5. Lord Shri Krishna replied My beloved friend It is only through My grace and power that you have been able to see this vision of splendour the Universal the Infinite the Original Never has it been seen by anyone else
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.48 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.48 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न वदयजञधययनरन दन रन च करयभरन तपभरगर एवरप शकय अह नलक दरषट तवदनयन करपरवर 1148
Translations:

1. Neither through the study of the Vedas nor by sacrifices nor by recitations of the scriptures nor by gifts nor by rituals nor by strict austerities can I be realized in a form like this in the world of men by anyone else but you O Arjuna

2. Not by the study of the Vedas and sacrifices not by gifts not even by rituals nor by severe austerities can I in this form be perceived in the human world by anyone other than you O most valiant among the Kurus who has not received My grace

3. Neither by the study of the Vedas nor by gifts nor by sacrifices nor by severe austerities can I be seen in this form in the world of men by any other than yourself O great hero of the Kurus Arjuna

4. Not by the knowledge of the Vedas and sacrifices nor by making gifts nor by the rituals nor by severe austerities can I be seen in this form in the world of men by anyone other than yourself O great hero of the Kurus
5. It is not possible for man on earth to see what thou hast seen O thou foremost hero of the Kuruclan through study of the scriptures sacrifice or gift ritual or rigorous austerity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.49 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.49 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: म त वयथ म च वमढभव दषटव रप घरमदङममदम वयपतभ परतमन पनसतव तदव म रपमद परपशय 1149
Translations:
1. You need not fear any longer nor be perplexed looking upon this aweinspiring form of Mine Free from fear and with a gladdened heart behold once again that other form of Mine
2. May you have no fear and may there be no bewilderment upon seeing this form of Mine so terrible Becoming free from fear and gladdened in mind once again behold this earlier form of Mine
3. Do not be afraid nor be bewildered on seeing such a terrible form of Mine with your fear dispelled and with a gladdened heart now behold again this former form of Mine

4. Let there be no distress or bewilderment in you upon seeing this terrifying and violent form of Mine be free from fear and with a cheerful heart behold again this form of Mine which is the same as before
5. Do not be afraid or confused by the terrible vision Put away your fear and with a joyful mind see Me again in My usual form
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.50 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 11.50 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इतयरजन वसदवसतथकतव सवक रप दरशयमस भय आशवसयमस च भतमन भतव पन समयवपरमहतम 1150
Translations:
1. Sanjaya said Having thus spoken to Arjuna Sri Krsna once more revealed His own form The Mahatman assuming a benign form again reassured him who had been struck with awe
2. Sanjaya said Thus having spoken to Arjuna in that manner Vasudeva showed His own form again And He the exalted One reassured this terrified one by again becoming serene in his form
3. Sanjaya said Having thus spoken to Arjuna Krishna again showed His own form The great Soul assuming His gentle form then consoled Arjuna who was terrified
4. Having said this to Arjuna Vasudeva revealed His own tiny form assuming His gentle body once again the Mighty Soul Krsna consoled the frightened Arjuna

5. Sanjaya continued Having thus spoken to Arjuna Lord Shri Krishna showed Himself again in His accustomed form and the Mighty Lord in gentle tones softly consoled him who had recently trembled with fear
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.51 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 11.51 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दषटवद मनष रप तवसमय जनरदन इदनमसम सवतत सचत परकत गत 1151
Translations:
1. Arjuna said Having beheld Your human and pleasing form O Krsna I am now composed in mind and have been restored to my normal nature
2. Arjuna said O Janardana having seen this serene human form of Yours I have now become calm in mind and restored to my own nature
3. Arjuna said Having seen this Thy gentle human form O Krishna now I am composed and have been restored to my own nature
4. Arjuna said On seeing this gentle human form of Yours O Janardana I have now regained my nature and collected my thinking faculty
5. Arjuna said Seeing You in Your gentle human form my Lord I am myself again calm once more
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.52 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.52 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सदरदरशमद रप दषटवनस यनमम दव अपयसय रपसय नतय दरशनकङकषण 1152
Translations:
1. The Lord said It is very hard to behold this form of Mine which you have seen Even the gods everlong to behold this form
2. The Blessed Lord said This form of Mine which you have seen is very difficult to behold even the gods are ever desirous of a vision of this form
3. The Blessed Lord said It is very hard indeed to see this form of Mine which thou hast seen even the gods are ever longing to behold it
4. The Bhagavat said This form of Mine which you have just observed is extremely difficult to behold even the gods are ever eager to behold it
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied It is difficult to see the vision of Me that thou hast seen Even the most powerful have longed for it in vain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.53 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.53 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नह वदरन तपस न दनन न चजयय शकय एववध दरषट दषटवनस म यथ 1153
Translations:
1. Sri Krishna says I cannot be seen in such a form as You have seen Me not by the Vedas nor by austerities nor by gifts nor by sacrifice
2. Not through the Vedas nor by austerity nor by gifts nor even by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as you have seen Me
3. Neither by the Vedas nor by austerity nor by gift nor by sacrifice can I be seen in this form as thou hast seen Me so easily
4. Neither by reciting the Vedas nor by observing austerity nor by giving gifts nor by performing sacrifice can I be seen in this way as you have seen Me now
5. It is not possible to see Me as you have done through studying the scriptures performing austerities giving gifts or making sacrifices
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.54 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.54 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: भकतय तवननयय शकय अहमववधऽरजन जञत दषट च तततवन परवषट च परतप 1154
Translations:
1. But by singleminded devotion O Arjuna it is possible to truly know to see and to enter into Me who am of this form O harasser of foes
2. But O Arjuna by singleminded devotion am I in this form able to be known and seen in reality and also entered into O destroyer of foes
3. But by singleminded devotion I can be known seen and entered into in reality O Arjuna
4. But through an unwavering devotion it is possible to know observe and even enter into Me O Arjuna O scorcher of foes
5. Only through tireless devotion can I be seen and known only in this way can a person become one with Me O Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 11.55 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 11.55 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मतकरमकनमतपरम मदभकत सङगवरजत नरवर सरवभतष य स ममत पणडव 1155
Translations:
1. Whoever works for Me regards Me as the highest and is devoted to Me free from attachment and without animosity towards any creature they come to Me O Arjuna
2. O son of Pandu he who works for Me accepts Me as the supreme goal is devoted to Me is devoid of attachment and free from enmity towards all beingshe attains Me
3. He who does all actions for Me who regards Me as the Supreme who is devoted to Me who is free from attachment who bears no enmity towards any creature he comes to Me O Arjuna
4. He who performs actions for Me who regards Me as his supreme goal who is devoted to Me who is free from attachment and who is free from hatred towards all beingshe attains Me O son of Pandu
5. He who does every action for My sake to whom I am the final goal who loves Me alone and hates no oneO My dearest son only he can realize Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.1 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 12.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एव सततयकत य भकतसतव परयपसत यचपयकषरमवयकत तष क यगवततम 121
Translations:
1. Arjuna said Which of these has greater knowledge of yogathose devotees who meditate on You ever integrated or those who meditate on the Imperishable and the Unmanifest

2. Arjuna said Which of these devotees who are ever dedicated and meditate on You and those who meditate on the Unmanifested and Immutable are the best experiencers of yoga here yoga means samadhi spiritual absorption
3. Arjuna said Which of them are better versed in Yogathose who steadfastly worship You or those who worship the imperishable and the unmanifested
4. Arjuna said Who are the best knowers of yoga those who are constantly attached to You and worship You or those who worship the motionless Unmanifest
5. Arjuna asked My Lord which are the better devotees who worship You those who try to know You as a Personal God or those who worship You as Impersonal and Indestructible
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मययवशय मन य म नतययकत उपसत शरदधय परयपतसत म यकततम मत 122
Translations:
1. The Lord said Those who ever integrated with Me and possessing supreme faith worship Me focusing their minds on Methese are considered by Me to be the highest among the yogis
2. The Blessed Lord said Those who meditate on Me fixing their minds on Me with steadfast devotion endowed with supreme faiththey are considered to be the most perfect yogis according to Me
3. The Blessed Lord said In My opinion those who fix their minds on Me worship Me ever steadfastly and are endowed with supreme faith are the best in Yoga

4. The Bhagavat said Those who causing their mind to enter into Me well remain permanently attached to Me and are endowed with an extraordinary faith worship Methey are considered by Me to be the best among the masters of Yoga
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied Those who keep their minds fixed on Me who worship Me always with unwavering faith and concentration are the very best
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य तवकषरमनरदशयमवयकत परयपसत सरवतरगमचनतय च कटसथमचल धरवम 123
Translations:
1. But those who meditate on the Imperishable the indefinable the unmanifest omnipresent unthinkable common to all beings immovable and constant
2. Those however who meditate in every way on the immutable the indefinable the unmanifest which is allpervasive incomprehensible unchanging immovable and constant
3. Those who worship the imperishable the indefinable the unmanifest the omnipresent the unthinkable the immovable and the eternal
4. Those who contemplate on the Unmanifest which is motionless undefinable allpervading unthinkable peaklike unmoving and fixed
5. Those who worship Me as the Indestructible the Undefinable the Omnipresent the Unthinkable the Primal the Immutable and the Eternal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सनयमयनदरयगरम सरवतर समबदधय त परपनवनत ममव सरवभतहत रत 124
Translations:
1. Having subdued all the senses being evenminded and engaged in the welfare of all beings they too come to Me only
2. By fully controlling all the organs and remaining evenminded at all times they engaged in the welfare of all beings attain Me alone
3. Having restrained all the senses being evenminded everywhere and intent on the welfare of all beings they verily come unto Me
4. Who by properly restraining the group of senseorgans remain equanimous at all stages and find pleasure in the welfare of all beingsthey attain nothing but Me
5. Subduing their senses viewing all conditions of life with an equal eye and working for the welfare of all beings they assuredly come to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कलशऽधकतरसतषमवयकतसकतचतसम अवयकत ह गतरदख दहवदभरवपयत 125
Translations:
1. Greater is the difficulty for those whose minds are thus attached to the unmanifest for the way of the unmanifest is hard to reach for embodied beings
2. For those whose minds are attached to the Unmanifested the struggle is greater for the Goal which is the Unmanifest is attained with difficulty by the embodied ones
3. Greater is their trouble whose minds are set on the unmanifested for the goal of the unmanifested is very hard for the embodied to reach
4. But the trouble is much greater for those who have their minds fixed on the Unmanifest for it is difficult for those with physical bodies to attain the Unmanifest goal
5. But they who fix their attention on the Absolute and Impersonal encounter greater hardships for it is difficult for those who have a body to realize Me as without one
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य त सरवण करमण मय सनयसय मतपर अननयनव यगन म धययनत उपसत 126
Translations:
1. For those who dedicate all their actions to Me hold Me as their supreme goal are intent on Me and worship Me meditating on Me with exclusive devotion
2. As for those who having dedicated all actions to Me and accepted Me as the supreme meditate on Me with singleminded concentration only
3. But to those who worship Me renouncing all actions in Me regarding Me as the supreme goal meditating on Me with singleminded yoga
4. On the other hand those who having renounced all their actions in Me have Me as their sole goal and revere Me meditating on Me through that Yoga alone which admits no other element but Me in it
5. Verily those who surrender their actions to Me who contemplate Me worship Me and meditate on Me alone with no thought other than Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तषमह समदधरत मतयससरसगरत भवम नचरतपरथ मययवशतचतसम 127
Translations:
1. Those whose minds are thus focused on Me I soon become their savior from the ocean of mortal life
2. O son of Prtha for those who have their minds absorbed in Me I become without delay the deliverer from the sea of the world which is fraught with death
3. To those whose minds are set on Me O Arjuna verily I soon become the savior out of the ocean of Samsara
4. Of them with their minds completely entered into Me I soon become a redeemer from the ocean of the cycle of death O son of Prtha
5. O Arjuna I rescue them from the ocean of life and death for their minds are fixed upon Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मययव मन आधतसव मय बदध नवशय नवसषयस मययव अत ऊरधव न सशय 128
Translations:
1. Focus your mind on Me alone and let your intellect enter into Me Then you will live in Me alone there is no doubt
2. Fix the mind on Me alone in Me alone let the intellect rest There is no doubt that hereafter you will dwell in Me alone
3. Fix your mind on Me and your intellect in Me Then you will certainly live in Me alone hereafter
4. Hence fix your mind on nothing but Me cause your thought to settle in Me Thus resorting to the best Yoga you will dwell in Me alone
5. Then let your mind cling only to Me let your intellect abide in Me and without doubt you will live hereafter in Me alone
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथ चतत समधत न शकनष मय सथरम अभयसयगन तत ममचछपत धनञजय 129
Translations:
1. If now you are unable to focus your mind on Me then seek to reach Me O Arjuna through the practice of repetition
2. If however you are unable to establish the mind steadily on Me then O Dhananjaya seek to attain Me through the yoga of practice
3. If you are unable to fix your mind steadily on Me then seek to reach Me through the yoga of constant practice O Arjuna
4. In case you are not able to cause your mind to enter completely into Me then O Dhananjaya Seek to attain Me through the practice of Yoga
5. But if you cannot fix your mind firmly on Me then my beloved friend try to do so through constant practice
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अभयसऽपयसमरथऽस मतकरमपरम भव मदरथमप करमण करवन सदधमवपसयस 1210
Translations:
1. If you are incapable of even this practice of repetition then devote yourself to My deeds For even by working for My sake you will attain perfection
2. If you are unable even to practice be intent on works for Me By undertaking works for Me as well you will attain perfection Identify with Brahman
3. If you are unable to practice even this Abhyasa Yoga be intent on doing actions for My sake even by doing actions for My sake you will attain perfection
4. If you are incapable of doing a steady practice then have Me as your chief aim in performing actions Even by performing actions for Me you shall attain success
5. And if you are not strong enough to practice concentration then devote yourself to My service do all your acts for My sake and you will still attain the goal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अथतदपयशकतऽस करत मदयगमशरत सरवकरमफलतयग तत कर यततमवन 1211
Translations:
1. If you are unable to do even thisthat is take refuge in My Yogathen with your selfcontrol renounce the fruits of every action
2. If you are unable to do even this then resort to the Yoga for Me and afterwards control your mind and renounce the results of all your works
3. If you are unable to do even this then resort to union with Me and renounce the fruits of all actions with selfcontrol
4. Now if you are not capable of doing this either then take refuge in My Yoga renouncing the fruits of all action with your mind subdued
5. And if thou art too weak to do even this then seek refuge in union with Me and with perfect selfcontrol renounce the fruits of thy actions
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरय ह जञनमभयसजजञनदधयन वशषयत धयनतकरमफलतयगसतयगचछनतरननतरम 1212
Translations:

1. Far better is knowledge of the Self than the repeated practice of remembrance of the Lord Better is meditation than this knowledge better is renunciation of the fruits of action than meditation From such renunciation peace ensues
2. Knowledge is certainly superior to practice meditation surpasses knowledge The renunciation of the results of works surpasses meditation From renunciation peace follows immediately
3. Better indeed is knowledge than practice better than knowledge is meditation better than meditation is the renunciation of the fruits of actions peace immediately follows renunciation
4. For knowledge is superior to practice due to knowledge meditation becomes preeminent from meditation arises the renunciation of the fruits of actions and peace follows renunciation
5. Knowledge is superior to blind action meditation is superior to knowledge renunciation of the fruit of action is superior to meditation and peace follows from renunciation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अदवषट सरवभतन मतर करण एव च नरमम नरहङकर समदखसख कषम 1213
Translations:
1. He who never hates any being who is friendly and compassionate who is free from the feelings of I and mine who looks upon all pain and pleasure equally and who is enduring
2. He who is not hateful towards any creature who is friendly and compassionate who has no concept of mine and no sense of egoism who remains the same in sorrow and happiness and who is forgiving
3. He who hates no creature is friendly and compassionate to all is free from attachment and egoism is balanced in pleasure and pain and is forgiving
4. He who is not a hater but a compassionate friend of every being who is free from the sense of mine and the sense of I who is evenminded in pain and pleasure and is endowed with forbearance
5. He who is incapable of hatred towards any being who is kind and compassionate free from selfishness without pride equable in pleasure and pain and forgiving
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सनतषट सतत यग यततम दढनशचय मययरपतमनबदधरय मदभकत स म परय 1214
Translations:
1. He who is content who meditates ever is selfrestrained and is firm in his convictions and has his mind and reason dedicated to Mehe is dear to Me
2. He who is ever content who is a yogi who has selfcontrol who has firm conviction and who has dedicated his mind and intellect to Mehe who is such a devotee of Mine is dear to Me
3. Ever content steady in meditation selfcontrolled possessing firm conviction with the mind and intellect dedicated to Me he My devotee is dear to Me
4. Who remains wellcontent and is a practitioner of Yoga at all times who is selfcontrolled and is firmly resolute and who has offered to Me their mind and intellectthat devotee of Mine is dear to Me
5. Always content selfcentered selfcontrolled resolute with mind and reason dedicated to Me such a devotee of Mine is beloved to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसमननदवजत लक लकननदवजत च य हरषमरषभयदवगरमकत य स च म परय 1215
Translations:
1. He from whom the world has no cause to be frightened who is not frightened by the world who is free from joy and impatience fear and aversion he is dear to me
2. He too owing to whom the world is not disturbed and who is not disturbed by the world who is free from joy impatience fear and anxiety is dear to me
3. He whom the world does not agitate and who cannot be agitated by the world and who is freed from joy anger fear and anxietyhe is dear to Me
4. He on account of whom the world does not become agitated who also does not become agitated on account of the world who is free from joy and impatience fear and anxietyhe is dear to Me
5. He who does not harm the world and whom the world cannot harm who is not carried away by any impulse of joy anger or fear such a one is My beloved
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनपकष शचरदकष उदसन गतवयथ सरवरमभपरतयग य मदभकत स म परय 1216
Translations:
1. He who is free from desires who is pure expert indifferent and free from agony who has renounced every undertakinghe is dear to me
2. He who has no desires who is pure dexterous impartial free from fear and who has renounced every undertakingsuch a devotee of Mine is dear to Me
3. He who is free from wants pure expert unconcerned and free from pain renouncing all undertakings and commencements he who is devoted to Me is dear to Me
4. He who does not expect anything who is pure dexterous unconcerned and untroubled and who has renounced all his undertakings all aroundthat devotee of Mine is dear to Me
5. He who expects nothing is pure watchful indifferent unruffled and renounces all initiativesuch a one is My beloved
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य न हषयत न दवषट न शचत न कङकषत शभशभपरतयग भकतमनय स म परय 1217
Translations:
1. He who does not rejoice nor hate nor grieve nor desire who renounces both good and evil and who is full of devotion to Mesuch a devotee is dear to Me
2. He who does not rejoice nor fret nor lament nor hanker who gives up both good and bad and is filled with devotionhe is dear to Me
3. He who neither rejoices nor hates nor grieves nor desires renouncing both good and evil and who is full of devotion is dear to Me
4. He who neither delights nor hates nor grieves nor craves who has renounced both the good and the bad results of actions and is full of devotion to Mehe is dear to Me
5. He who is beyond joy and hate who neither laments nor desires to whom good and evil fortunes are the same such a one is beloved to Me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 12.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 12.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

4. Those who as instructed above resort to this duty that leads to immortality who have faith in it and have Me as their only goalthose devotees are exceedingly dear to Me

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

4. It is without and within every being unmoving yet moving due to its subtle nature it is incomprehensible it exists far away yet is near

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

Translations:

2. Thus in brief the field Knowledge and the Knowable have been spoken of By understanding this My devotee becomes qualified for My state

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकत परष चव वदधयनद उभवप वकरशच गणशचव वदध परकतसभवन 1319
Translations:
1. Know that both Prakṛti and the Self Puruṣa are without beginning know that all modifications and the attributes are born from Prakṛti
2. Know both Nature and the individual soul to be verily without beginning know the modifications as well as the qualities to be born of Nature
3. Know that Nature matter and the Spirit are both beginningless and know also that all modifications and qualities are born from Nature
4. Both the Material Cause and the Soul are beginningless you should know this You should also know that the modifications and strands are born from the Material Cause
5. Know further that Nature and God have no beginning and that differences in character and quality originate from Nature only
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करयकरणकरततव हत परकतरचयत परष सखदखन भकततव हतरचयत 1320
Translations:
1. The Prakṛti is said to be the cause of agency to the body Kārya and senseorgans Karaṇa the Self is said to be the cause of experiencing pleasure and pain
2. With regard to the source of the body and its organs Nature is said to be the cause the soul is the cause so far as the enjoyment of happiness and sorrow is concerned
3. In the production of the effect and the cause Nature matter is said to be the cause in the experience of pleasure and pain the soul is said to be the one responsible
4. In creating the process of cause and effect the material cause is said to be the basis and in experiencing pleasure and pain the soul is said to be the basis
5. Nature is the law that generates cause and effect God is the source of all pleasure and pain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परष परकतसथ ह भङकत परकतजनगणन करण गणसङगऽसय सदसदयनजनमस 1321
Translations:
1. a Indeed the self seated in Prakrti experiences the Gunas born of Prakrti b Its attachment to these Gunas is the cause of birth in good and evil wombs
2. Since the soul is seated in Nature therefore it experiences the qualities born of Nature Contact with the qualities is the cause of its births in good and evil wombs
3. The soul seated in Nature experiences the qualities born of Nature attachment to the qualities is the cause of its birth in good and evil wombs
4. For the soul seated on the material cause enjoys the strands born of the material cause His attachment to the strands is the cause of his births in good and evil wombs
5. God dwelling in the heart of Nature experiences the qualities that Nature brings forth and His affinity towards these qualities is the reason for His living in a good or evil body
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

2. He who is the Witness the Permitter the Sustainer the Experiencer the great Lord and who is also spoken of as the transcendental Self is the supreme Person in this body
3. The Supreme Soul in this body is also called the observer the permitter the sustainer the enjoyer the great Lord and the Supreme Self
4. The Supreme Soul in this corporeal body is called the Spectator the Assenter the Supporter the Experiencer the Mighty Lord and also the Supreme Self
5. Thus in the body of man dwells the Supreme God He who sees permits upholds and enjoys is the Highest God and the Highest Self
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

Translations:

2. He who knows thus the Person Nature and Qualities will not be born again no matter how he may live
3. He who thus knows the Spirit and Matter together with their qualities in whatever condition he may be he is not reborn
4. He who knows in this manner the Soul and the Material Cause together with their strandshe is not reborn even though he may act in various ways
5. He who understands God and Nature along with her qualities whatever his condition in life may be will not come back to earth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धयननतमन पशयनत कचदतमनमतमन अनय सखयन यगन करमयगन चपर 1324
Translations:
1. Some perceive the Self within the self body through meditation of the mind others through Sankhya Yoga and still others through Karma Yoga
2. Some realize the Self in their intellect through meditation others through SankhyaYoga and still others through KarmaYoga with the help of the internal organ
3. Some behold the Self within themselves through meditation others through the Yoga of knowledge and still others through the Yoga of action
4. However some yogis perceive the Self as the Self within the self through meditation others through knowledgeyoga and still others through actionyoga
5. Some realize the Supreme by meditating with its aid on the Self within others by pure reason and still others by right action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनय तववमजननत शरतवऽनयभय उपसत तऽप चततरनतयव मतय शरतपरयण 1325
Translations:
1. But some who do not know this having heard from others worship accordinglythese too devoted to what they hear pass beyond death
2. Others again who do not know thus take to thinking after hearing from others they too who are devoted to hearing certainly overcome death
3. Others too who do not know thus worship having heard of It from others they too cross beyond death regarding what they have heard as the supreme refuge
4. But others who have no knowledge of this nature listen from others and reflect accordingly they too being devoted to what they have heard do cross over death
5. Others again having no direct knowledge but only hearing from others still worship and if they are true to the teachings they too will cross the sea of death
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यवतसञजयत कञचतसततव सथवरजङगमम कषतरकषतरजञसयगततदवदध भरतरषभ 1326
Translations:
1. Whatever being is born whether it be moving or stationary know O Arjuna that it is through the combination of the Ksetra body and Ksetrajna knower of the Field
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty whatever object moving or nonmoving comes into existence know that to be from the combination of the field and the Knower of the field
3. Wherever a being is born whether unmoving or moving know thou O best of the Bharatas Arjuna that it is from the union of the field and its knower
4. Whatever living being is born stationary or moving you should know that all this has a close connection with the Field and the Fieldsensitizer O best of the Bharatas
5. Wherever life is seen in things movable or immovable it is the result of the combination of Matter and Spirit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सम सरवष भतष तषठनत परमशवरम वनशयतसववनशयनत य पशयत स पशयत 1327
Translations:
1. Who sees the supreme ruler dwelling alike in all bodies and never perishing even when they perish he indeed sees
2. He who sees the Supreme Lord existing truly in all beings and the Imperishable among the perishable sees indeed
3. He who sees the Supreme Lord existing truly in all beings the imperishable within the perishable sees indeed
4. Whoever perceives the Supreme Lord as abiding and nonperishing in all beings even as they perish perceives properly
5. He who can see the Supreme Lord in all beings the Imperishable amidst the perishable he is the one who truly sees
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सम पशयनह सरवतर समवसथतमशवरम न हनसतयतमनऽऽतमन तत यत पर गतम 1328
Translations:
1. For seeing the ruler abiding alike in every place he does not injure himself with his own mind and therefore reaches the highest goal
2. Since by truly seeing God who is present everywhere alike he does not injure the Self by the Self he therefore attains the supreme Goal
3. For he who truly sees the same Lord dwelling everywhere does not destroy the Self by the self rather he attains the highest goal
4. Whoever perceiving the Lord as abiding in all alike does not harm themselves by themselves they attain on that account the Supreme Goal
5. Beholding the Lord equally in all things his actions do not mar his spiritual life but lead him to the heights of bliss
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकतयव च करमण करयमणन सरवश य पशयत तथऽऽतमनमकरतर स पशयत 1329
Translations:
1. He who sees that all acts are done universally by Prakrti alone and likewise that the self is not the doer he indeed sees
2. And he who sees actions being done in various ways by Nature itself and also the Self as the nonagent he sees
3. He sees who sees that all actions are performed solely by Nature and that the Self is without action

4. Whoever views all actions as being performed or all objects as being created by the Material Cause itself and at the same time views their own Self as a nonperformer or noncreator they view properly
5. He who understands that it is only the Law of Nature that brings action to fruition and that the Self never acts alone knows the Truth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद भतपथगभवमकसथमनपशयत तत एव च वसतर बरहम समपदयत तद 1330
Translations:
1. When he perceives the independent modes of existence of all beings centered in one and their expansion from it alone then he attains Brahman
2. When one realizes that the state of diversity of living things is rooted in the One and that their manifestation also comes from That then one becomes identified with Brahman
3. When a person sees all beings as resting in the One and emanating from the One alone they then become Brahman
4. When he perceives the mutual difference of beings as abiding in One and its expansion from That alone at that time he becomes the Brahman
5. He who sees the diverse forms of life all rooted in One and growing forth from Him shall indeed find the Absolute
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनदतवननरगणतवतपरमतमयमवयय शररसथऽप कनतय न करत न लपयत 1331
Translations:
1. This supreme Self though dwelling in the body is immutable O Arjuna being without beginning It neither acts nor is tainted as it is without Gunas
2. Being without beginning and without end O son of Kunti this immutable supreme Self does not act nor is It affected Also translated as taintedTr although It exists in the body
3. Being without beginning devoid of any qualities the Supreme Self imperishable though dwelling in the body O Arjuna neither acts nor is tainted
4. Because this is beginningless and because this has no qualities this Supreme Self is changeless and it neither acts nor gets stained by actions even though it dwells in the body O son of Kunti
5. The Supreme Spirit O Prince is without beginning has no qualities and is imperishable Though it is within the body it does not act nor is it affected by action
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यथ सरवगत सकषमयदकश नपलपयत सरवतरवसथत दह तथऽऽतम नपलपयत 1332
Translations:
1. As the allpervading ether is not tainted due to its subtlety even so the self abiding in the body is not tainted everywhere
2. As the allpervading space is not defiled due to its subtlety similarly the Self present everywhere in bodies is not defiled
3. As the allpervading ether is not tainted due to its subtlety so the Self seated everywhere in the body is not tainted either
4. Just as the allpervasive Ether is not stained due to its subtlety in the same way the Self abiding in the body everywhere is not stained
5. As space though present everywhere remains unaffected due to its subtlety so too the Self though present in all forms retains its purity unalloyed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यथ परकशयतयक कतसन लकमम रव कषतर कषतर तथ कतसन परकशयत भरत 1333
Translations:
1. As the one sun illuminates this entire world so does the Knower of the Field Ksetrin the self O Arjuna illuminate the entire Field the body
2. As the single sun illuminates this entire world similarly O descendant of the Bharata dynasty the Knower of the field illuminates the entire field
3. Just as the one sun illuminates the entire world so too does the Lord of the field Supreme Self illuminate the entire field O Arjuna
4. Just as a single sun illuminates this entire world so too does the Lord of the Field illuminate the entire Field O descendant of Bharata
5. As the one Sun illuminates the entire earth so the Lord illuminates the entire universe
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 13.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 13.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कषतरकषतरजञयरवमनतर जञनचकषष भतपरकतमकष च य वदरयनत त परम 1334
Translations:

1. Those who thus discern with the eye of knowledge the difference between the body or the Field Ksetra and the knower of the body or Fieldknower Ksetrajna and the means of deliverance from the manifested Prakrti they attain the Supreme
2. Those who through the eye of wisdom know the distinction between the field and the Knower of the field and the annihilation of the Matrix of beings reach the Supreme
3. They who by the eye of knowledge perceive the distinction between the field and its knower as well as the liberation from the Nature of being go to the Supreme
4. Those who thus understand with the knowledgeeye the inner Soul of the Field and the Fieldsensitizer as well as the deliverance from the Material Cause of the elementsthey attain the Supreme
5. Those who with the eyes of wisdom thus see the difference between Matter and Spirit and know how to liberate life from the Law of Nature attain the Supreme
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पर भय परवकषयम जञनन जञनमततमम यजजञतव मनय सरव पर सदधमत गत 141
Translations:
1. The Lord said I shall declare again another kind of knowledge It is the best of all forms of knowledge by knowing which all the sages have attained the state of perfection beyond this world
2. The Blessed Lord said I shall speak again of the supreme knowledge the best of all knowledge by realizing which all the contemplatives have reached the highest perfection from here
3. The Blessed Lord said I will again declare to thee that supreme knowledge the best of all knowledge having known which all the sages have gone to supreme perfection after this life
4. The Bhagavat said Further once again I shall explain the supreme knowledge the best among knowledges by knowing which all the seers have gone from here to the Supreme Success
5. Lord Shri Krishna continued Now I will reveal to you the wisdom that is beyond knowledge by attaining which the sages have reached perfection
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इद जञनमपशरतय मम सधरमयमगत सरगऽप नपजयनत परलय न वयथनत च 142
Translations:
1. Resorting to this knowledge partaking in My Nature they are not born at the time of creation nor do they suffer at the time of dissolution
2. Those who attain identity with Me by resorting to this Knowledge are not born even during creation nor do they suffer pain during dissolution
3. Those who having taken refuge in this knowledge have attained unity with Me are neither born at the time of creation nor disturbed at the time of dissolution
4. Holding onto this knowledge they have attained a state with attributes common to Me they are neither born at the time of creation nor grieve at the time of dissolution
5. Dwelling in wisdom and realizing My divinity they are not reborn when the universe is recreated at the beginning of every cycle nor are they affected when it is dissolved
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मम यनरमहदबरहम तसमन गरभ दधमयहम सभव सरवभतन तत भवत भरत 143
Translations:
1. My womb is the great Brahman ie Prakṛti In it I lay the germ From that O Arjuna comes the birth of all beings
2. My womb is the great sustainer In it I place the seed From that O scion of the Bharata dynasty all things are born
3. My womb is the great Brahma in it I place the germ thence O Arjuna is the birth of all beings
4. The mighty Brahman is a womb for Me and in it I lay seed from which is the birth of all beings O descendant of Bharata
5. The eternal cosmos is My womb in which I plant the seed from which all beings are born O prince
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवयनष कनतय मरतय समभवनत य तस बरहम महदयनरह बजपरद पत 144
Translations:
1. Whatever forms are produced in any womb O Arjuna the Prakṛti is their great womb and I am the sowing father
2. O son of Kunti whatever forms are born from all the wombs of them the great sustainer is the womb I am the father who deposits the seed
3. Whatever forms are produced O Arjuna in any womb whatsoever the great Brahma is their womb and I am the seedgiving father
4. O son of Kunti Whatever manifestations spring up in all the wombs the mighty Brahman is the womb and I am the father who sows the seed
5. O illustrious son of Kunti Through whatever wombs men are born it is the Spirit itself that conceives and I am their father
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सततव रजसतम इत गण परकतसभव नबधननत महबह दह दहनमवययम 145
Translations:
1. The Gunas of Sattva Rajas and Tamas arise from Prakrti and bind the immutable Self in the body O Arjuna
2. O mightyarmed one the qualities viz sattva rajas and tamas born of Nature are the immutable embodiments belonging to the body
3. These qualities O Arjuna born of Nature bind fast in the body of the embodied the indestructible purity passion and inertia
4. The strands namely the sattva rajas and tamas born from the prime cause the said mother bind the changeless embodied soul to the body O mightyarmed one
5. Purity passion and ignorance are the qualities that the law of nature brings forth They fetter the free spirit in all beings
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ततर सततव नरमलतवतपरकशकमनमयम सखसङगन बधनत जञनसङगन चनघ 146
Translations:
1. Of these sattva being without impurity is luminous and free from morbidity It binds O Arjuna through attachment to pleasure and knowledge

2. Among them sattva being pure and nirmala puretransparent ie capable of resisting any form of ignorance and hence an illuminator ie a revealer of Consciousness is harmless O sinless one it binds through attachment to happiness and attachment to knowledge
3. Of these sattva which is luminous and healthy due to its stainlessness binds one by attachment to happiness and knowledge O sinless one
4. Among them the Sattva being free from impurity is illuminating and healthgiving and it binds the embodied one with attachment to happiness and also with attachment to knowledge O sinless one
5. O Sinless One Of these purity being luminous strong and invulnerable binds one with its yearning for happiness and illumination
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रज रगतमक वदध तषणसङगसमदभवम तननबधनत कनतय करमसङगन दहनम 147
Translations:
1. Know O Arjuna that Rajas is of the nature of passion springing from thirst and attachment It binds the embodied self with attachment to action
2. Know rajas to be of the nature of passion born of hankering and attachment O son of Kunti it binds the embodied one through attachment to action
3. Know O Arjuna that Rajas is of the nature of passion the source of thirst and attachment it binds fast the embodied one by attachment to action
4. You should know that Rajas is of the nature of desire and is a source of cravingattachment and it binds the embodied one by attachment to action O son of Kunti
5. Passion engendered by thirst for pleasure and attachment binds the soul through its fondness for activities
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तमसतवजञनज वदध महन सरवदहनम परमदलसयनदरभसतननबधनत भरत 148
Translations:
1. Know that Tamas is born of false knowledge and deludes all embodied beings It binds O Arjuna with negligence indolence and sleep
2. On the other hand know tamas which deludes all embodied beings to be born of ignorance O scion of the Bharata dynasty it binds through inadvertence laziness and sleep
3. But know thou Tamas to be born of ignorance deluding all embodied beings it binds fast O Arjuna through heedlessness indolence and sleep
4. But you should also know that Tamas is born of ignorance and deludes all the Embodied it binds them with negligence laziness and sleep O descendant of Bharata
5. But Ignorance the product of darkness stupefies the senses of all embodied beings binding them with chains of folly indolence and lethargy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सततव सख सञजयत रज करमण भरत जञनमवतय त तम परमद सञजयतयत 149
Translations:
1. Sattva generates attachment to pleasure Rajas to action O Arjuna But Tamas obscuring knowledge generates attachment to negligence
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty sattva attaches one to happiness rajas to action while tamas obscuring knowledge leads to inadvertence as well
3. Sattva attaches to happiness Rajas to action O Arjuna while Tamas verily shrouding knowledge attaches to heedlessness
4. O descendant of Bharata The Sattva fully dominates the Embodied in the field of happiness the Rajas in action but the Tamas also totally dominates in the field of negligence by veiling knowledge
5. Purity brings happiness passion brings commotion and ignorance which obscures wisdom leads to a life of failure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रजसतमशचभभय सततव भवत भरत रज सततव तमशचव तम सततव रजसतथ 1410
Translations:
1. Prevailing over Rajas and Tamas Sattva predominates O Arjuna Prevailing over Tamas and Sattva Rajas predominates Prevailing over Rajas and Sattva Tamas predominates
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty sattva increases by subduing rajas and tamas rajas increases by overpowering sattva and tamas and tamas increases by dominating over sattva and rajas
3. Now O Arjuna Sattva prevails having overpowered Rajas and Tamas then Rajas having overpowered Sattva and Tamas and then Tamas having overpowered Sattva and Rajas

4. O descendant of Bharata The Sattva increases by overpowering the Rajas and the Tamas the Rajas increases by overpowering the Sattva and the Tamas and the Tamas increases likewise by overpowering the Sattva and the Rajas
5. O Prince Purity prevails when passion and ignorance are overcome passion when purity and ignorance are overcome and ignorance when it is overcome by purity and passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवदवरष दहऽसमनपरकश उपजयत जञन यद तद वदयदववदध सततवमतयत 1411
Translations:
1. When knowledge like light illuminates from all gateways ie the senses then one should know that Sattva prevails
2. When the illumination of knowledge radiates through all the doors of the senses in this body then one should know that sattva has greatly increased
3. When the wisdomlight shines through every gate of this body then it may be known that Sattva is predominant
4. When the knowledgelight arises in all the gates of this body then one should also know that the Sattva has predominantly increased
5. When the light of knowledge gleams forth from all the gates of the body then be sure that purity prevails
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: लभ परवततररमभ करमणमशम सपह रजसयतन जयनत ववदध भरतरषभ 1412
Translations:
1. Greed activity undertaking of work restlessness and longingthese arise O Arjuna when Rajas predominates
2. O best of the Bharata dynasty when rajas becomes predominant these come into being avarice restlessness undertaking of actions agitation and craving
3. Greed activity the undertaking of actions restlessness and longingthese arise when Rajas is predominant O Arjuna
4. Greed exertion undertaking of actions restlessness and cravingthese are born when Rajas predominates O chief of the Bharatas
5. O best of Indians Avarice the impulse to act and the beginning of action itself are all due to the dominance of passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अपरकशऽपरवततशच परमद मह एव च तमसयतन जयनत ववदध करननदन 1413
Translations:
1. Nonillumination inactivity negligence and even delusionthese arise O Arjuna when Tamas prevails
2. O descendant of the Kuru dynasty when tamas predominates these come into being without exception nondiscrimination inactivity inadvertence and delusion
3. Darkness inertia carelessness and delusionthese arise when Tamas is predominant O Arjuna
4. Absence of mental illumination lack of exertion negligence and mere delusionthese are born when Tamas predominates O darling of the Kurus
5. Darkness stagnation folly and infatuation are the results of the dominance of ignorance O joy of the Kuru clan
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यद सततव परवदध त परलय यत दहभत तदततमवद लकनमलनपरतपदयत 1414
Translations:
1. If the embodied self dissolves when Sattva prevails then it proceeds to the pure worlds of those who know the highest
2. When an embodied one undergoes death while sattva is exclusively predominant then he attains the untainted worlds of those who know the highest entities
3. If the embodied one meets death when Sattva is predominant then they attain the spotless worlds of the knowers of the Highest
4. But if the bodybearer dies when sattva is on the increase then he attains to the spotless worlds of those who know the highest
5. When purity prevails the soul upon leaving the body passes on to the pure regions where those who know the Highest reside
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रजस परलय गतव करमसङगष जयत तथ परलनसतमस मढयनष जयत 1415
Translations:

1. When Rajas is prevalent one meets with dissolution and is born among those attached to work Similarly when Tamas prevails one who has met with dissolution is born in the womb of beings lacking intelligence
2. When one dies while rajas predominates they are born among people attached to activity Similarly when one dies while tamas predominates they take birth among the stupid species
3. Meeting death in Rajas he is born among those who are attached to action and dying in Tamas he is born in the womb of the thoughtless
4. By meeting death when Rajas is on the increase he is born among those who are attached to action likewise by meeting death when Tamas is on the increase he is born in the wombs of the deluded
5. When passion prevails the soul is reborn among those who love activity when ignorance rules it enters the wombs of the ignorant
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करमण सकतसयह सततवक नरमल फलम रजससत फल दखमजञन तमस फलम 1416
Translations:
1. It is said that the fruit of a good deed is pure and of the nature of Sattva the fruit of Rajas is pain and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance
2. They say that the result of good work is pure and is born of Sattva But the result of Rajas is sorrow the result of Tamas is ignorance
3. They say that the fruit of good action is Sattvic and pure indeed the fruit of Rajas is pain and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance
4. It is said that the fruit of good action is spotless and of the Sattva nature whereas the fruit of Rajas is pain and the fruit of Tamas is ignorance
5. They say the fruit of a meritorious action is spotless and pure the outcome of passion is misery and of ignorance darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सततवतसञजयत जञन रजस लभ एव च परमदमह तमस भवतऽजञनमव च 1417
Translations:
1. From Sattva arises knowledge from Rajas arises greed from Tamas arises negligence and delusion and from it arises ignorance
2. From sattva is born knowledge and from rajas avarice indeed From tamas are born inadvertence delusion and ignorance to be sure
3. From Sattva arises knowledge and greed from Rajas heedlessness and delusion arise from Tamas and also ignorance
4. From Sattva arises wisdom from Rajas only greed and from Tamas arise negligence delusion and ignorance
5. Purity engenders wisdom passion avarice and ignorance folly infatuation and darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ऊरधव गचछनत सततवसथ मधय तषठनत रजस जघनयगणवततसथ अध गचछनत तमस 1418
Translations:
1. Those who rest in Sattva ascend upwards those who abide in Rajas stay in the middle and those abiding in the tendencies of Tamas descend downwards
2. People who conform to sattva go higher up those who conform to rajas stay in the middle those who conform to tamas who conform to the actions of the lowest quality go down
3. Those seated in Sattva ascend those of Rajasic nature dwell in the middle and those of Tamasic nature abiding in the function of the lowest Guna descend
4. Those established in Sattva ascend those given to Rajas remain in the middle those given to Tamas established in the tendencies of bad qualities descend
5. When purity is in the ascendant the man evolves when passion is in the ascendant he neither evolves nor degenerates when ignorance is in the ascendant he is lost
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ननय गणभय करतर यद दरषटनपशयत गणभयशच पर वतत मदभव सऽधगचछत 1419
Translations:
1. When the seer beholds no agent of action other than the Gunas and knows what is beyond the Gunas he attains to My state
2. When the witness sees none other than the qualities as the agent and knows that which is superior to the qualities he attains My nature
3. When the seer beholds no agent other than the Gunas and knows that which is higher than them he attains to My Being
4. When the Perceiver the Self finds no agent other than the Strands and realizes That which is beyond the Strands then he attains My state
5. As soon as one understands that it is only the qualities that act and nothing else and perceives that which is beyond they attain My divine nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: गणनतनततय तरनदह दहसमदभवन जनममतयजरदखरवमकतऽमतमशनत 1420
Translations:
1. The embodied Self crossing beyond these three Gunas that arise in the body and being freed from birth death aging and pain attains immortality
2. Having transcended these three qualities which are the origin of the body the embodied one becomes free from birth death old age and sorrows and experiences Immortality
3. The embodied one having crossed beyond these three Gunas from which the body is evolved is freed from birth death decay and pain and attains immortality
4. Transcending these three strands from which the body etc is born the embodied the soul is freed from birth death old age and sorrow and attains immortality
5. When the soul transcends the Qualities which are the real cause of physical existence then freed from birth and death from old age and misery he drinks the nectar of immortality
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.21 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 14.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करलगसतरनगणनतनतत भवत परभ कमचर कथ चतसतरनगणनतवरतत 1421
Translations:
1. Arjuna said What are the marks of a person who has transcended the three Gunas What is their behavior And how do they transcend the three Gunas
2. Arjuna said O Lord by what signs is one known who has gone beyond these three qualities What is their behavior and how do they transcend these three qualities
3. Arjuna said What are the marks of one who has transcended the three qualities O Lord What is their conduct and how do they go beyond these three qualities
4. Arjuna said O Master With what characteristic marks does he who has transcended these three strands exist What is his behavior How does he pass beyond these three strands
5. Arjuna asked My Lord how can one recognize someone who has transcended the qualities How do they act and live beyond them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परकश च परवतत च महमव च पणडव न दवषट समपरवततन न नवततन कङकषत 1422
Translations:
1. The Lord said He does not hate illumination nor activity nor even delusion O Arjuna while these prevail nor does He long for them when they cease
2. The Blessed Lord said O son of Pandu he neither dislikes illumination knowledge activity nor delusion when they appear nor does he long for them when they disappear
3. The Blessed Lord said When light activity and delusion are present he does not hate them nor does he long for them when they are absent
4. The Bhagavat said O son of Pandu He neither abhors nor craves for illumination exertion and delusion as and when they arise or cease to be
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied O Prince He who does not shun the quality that is present and does not long for that which is absent
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: उदसनवदसन गणरय न वचलयत गण वरतनत इतयव यऽवतषठत नङगत 1423
Translations:
1. He who sits like one unconcerned undisturbed by the Gunas who knows It is the Gunas that are in motion and so remains unshaken
2. He who sitting like one indifferent is not distracted by the three qualities he who thinking that the qualities alone act remains firm and does not move surely
3. He who seated like one unconcerned is not moved by the dualities and who knowing that the dualities are active is selfcentered and does not move
4. He who sitting like an unconcerned person is not perturbed by the strands who is ignorant of the existence of the strands or who remains simply aware that the strands alone exist who is not shaken
5. He who maintains an attitude of indifference who is not disturbed by the qualities who realizes that it is only they who act and remains calm
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: समदखसख सवसथ समलषटशमकञचन तलयपरयपरय धरसतलयननदतमससतत 1424
Translations:

1. He who is alike in pleasure and pain who dwells in his Self who looks upon a clod a stone and a piece of gold as of equal value who remains the same towards things dear and hateful and who is intelligent who regards both blame and praise of himself as equal

2. He to whom sorrow and happiness are alike who is established in his own Self to whom a lump of earth iron and gold are the same to whom the agreeable and the disagreeable are the same who is wise to whom censure and his own praise are the same

3. Who is the same in pleasure and pain who dwells in the Self to whom a clod of earth a stone and gold are all alike who is the same to the dear and the unfriendly who is firm and to whom censure and praise are one and the same

4. To whom pain pleasure and sleep are all the same to whom a cold a stone and a lump of gold are all the same to whom both the pleasant and unpleasant things are equal who is firmminded to whom blame and personal commendation are equal

5. Who accepts pain and pleasure as they come is centered in their Self to whom a piece of clay stone or gold are all the same who neither likes nor dislikes who is steadfast indifferent alike to praise or censure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मनपमनयसतलयसतलय मतररपकषय सरवरमभपरतयग गणतत स उचयत 1425
Translations:
1. He who is the same in honor and dishonor and the same to friend and foe and who has abandoned all enterpriseshe is said to have risen above the Gunas
2. He who is the same under honor and dishonor who is equitably disposed towards both friends and foes who has renounced all undertakingshe is said to have gone beyond dualities
3. Who is the same in honor and dishonor the same to friend and foe abandoning all undertakings he is said to have transcended the dualities
4. Who remains equal to honor and dishonor and equal to the sides of both the friend and the foe and who has given up all fruits of his actionshe is said to have transcended the bonds
5. Who looks equally upon honor and dishonor loves friends and foes alike and abandons all initiative such is he who transcends the qualities
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: म च यऽवयभचरण भकतयगन सवत स गणनसमततयतन बरहमभयय कलपत 1426
Translations:
1. And he who with unwavering devotion through Yoga serves Me he transcending the Gunas becomes fit for the state of Brahman
2. And he who serves Me through the unswerving Yoga of Devotion he having gone beyond these qualities qualifies for becoming Brahman
3. And he who serves Me with unwavering devotion he crossing beyond the dualities is fit for becoming Brahman
4. Whoever serves me alone with an unwavering devotion to yoga they will transcend these strands and become Brahman
5. And he who serves Me alone with unwavering devotion shall overcome the Qualities and become one with the Eternal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 14.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 14.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बरहमण ह परतषठऽहममतसयवययसय च शशवतसय च धरमसय सखसयकनतकसय च 1427
Translations:
1. For I am the ground of Brahman the immortal immutable and eternal Dharma and perfect bliss
2. For I am the Abode of Brahmanthe indestructible immutable eternal Dharma and absolute Bliss
3. For I am the abode of Brahman the immortal immutable and everlasting Dharma and absolute bliss
4. I am the support for the immortal and changeless Brahman and for its eternal attribute the unalloyed happiness
5. For I am the home of the spirit the continual source of immortality eternal righteousness and infinite joy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ऊरधवमलमधशखमशवतथ परहरवययम छनदस यसय परणन यसत वद स वदवत 151
Translations:
1. The Lord said They speak of an immutable Asvattha tree with its roots above and branches below its leaves are the Vedas He who knows it knows the Vedas

2. The Blessed Lord said They say that the peepul tree which has its roots upward and its branches downward and of which the Vedas are the leaves is imperishable He who realizes it is a knower of the Vedas
3. The Blessed Lord said They the wise speak of the indestructible peepul tree with its roots above and branches below whose leaves are the meters or hymns he who knows it is a knower of the Vedas

4. The Bhagavat said The scriptures speak of a nonperishing holy fig tree whose roots are high and branches are low and of which the Vedic hymns are the leaves He who knows this tree is the knower of the Vedas

5. Lord Shri Krishna continued This phenomenal creation which is both ephemeral and eternal is like a tree with its seed above in the Highest and its ramifications below on this earth The scriptures are its leaves and he who understands this knows
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अधशचरधव परसतसतसय शख गणपरवदध वषयपरवल अधशच मलनयनसनततन करमनबनधन मनषयलक 152
Translations:
1. a Its branches extend both above and below nourished by the Gunas Their shoots are sense objects b And their secondary roots extend downwards resulting in actions that bind in the world of humans

2. The branches of that Tree extending downwards and upwards are strengthened by the qualities and have senseobjects as their shoots And the roots which are followed by actions spread downwards in the human world According to AG and MS manusyaloke means a body distinguished by Brahminhood etc
3. Its branches spread below and above nourished by the Gunas its buds are senseobjects and its roots stretch forth below in the world of men originating action

4. Of which tree the branches spreading downward and upward welldeveloped with strands have sense objects as sprouts and its roots below in the human world stretching successively have actions as their subknots

5. Its branches shoot upwards and downwards deriving their nourishment from the qualities its buds are the objects of sense and its roots which follow the law causing mans regeneration and degeneration pierce downwards into the soil
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न रपमसयह तथपलभयत ननत न चदरन च सपरतषठ अशवतथमन सवरढमल मसङगशसतरण दढन छततव 153
Translations:
1. Its form is not perceived here nor its end nor its beginning nor its support Having cut off this firmlyrooted Asvattha with the strong axe of detachment
2. Its form is not perceived here in that way nor its end nor beginning nor its continuance After felling this Peepul tree whose roots are well developed with the strong sword of detachment
3. Its form is not perceived here as such nor its end origin foundation or resting place having cut asunder this firmly rooted peepul tree with the strong axe of nonattachment

4. The nature of this is not perceived in that way nor its end nor its beginning nor its center middle Cutting this holy fig tree with its firmly and variedly grown roots by means of the sharp or strong axe of nonattachment

5. In this world its true form is not known neither its origin nor its end and its strength is not understooduntil the tree with its roots striking deep into the earth is hewn down by the sharp axe of nonattachment
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तत पद ततपरमरगतवय यसमनगत न नवरतनत भय तमव चदय परष परपदय यत परवतत परसत परण 154
Translations:
1. Then one should seek that goal attaining which one never returns One should seek refuge with that Primal Person from whom this ancient activity streamed forth
2. Thereafter that state must be sought going where they do not return again I take refuge in that Primeval Person Himself from whom the eternal Manifestation has ensued
3. Then that goal should be sought for to which having gone none returns again I seek refuge in that Primeval Purusha from whence streamed forth the ancient activity or energy
4. Then that Abode must be sought having reached which one would not return The Yogin would attain nothing but that Primal Person from Whom the old activity world creation commences
5. Beyond lies the path from which once found there is no return This is the Primal God from whom this ancient creation has sprung
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नरमनमह जतसङगदष अधयतमनतय वनवततकम दवनदवरवमकत सखदखसजञ रगचछनतयमढ पदमवयय तत 155
Translations:

1. Without the delusion of perverse notions concerning the self victorious over the evil of attachment ever devoted to the Self turned away from desires and liberated from dualities such as pleasure and pain the undeluded go to that imperishable state

2. The wise ones free from pride and nondiscrimination who have conquered the evil of association hatred and love arising from association with foes and friends who are ever devoted to spirituality completely free from desires and free from the dualities of happiness and sorrow reach that undecaying state

3. Free from pride and delusion victorious over the evil of attachment dwelling constantly in the Self their desires having completely turned away freed from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain they the undeluded reach the eternal goal

4. Those who are rid of pride and delusion have put down the evils of attachment remain constantly in their own nature of the Self have their desires completely departed and are fully liberated from the pairs known as pleasures and painsthese undeluded men go to that changeless Abode

5. The wise attain Eternity when freed from pride and delusion they have conquered their love for the things of sense when renouncing desire and fixing their gaze on the Self they cease to be tossed to and fro by the opposing sensations such as pleasure and pain
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न तदभसयत सरय न शशङक न पवक यदगतव न नवरतनत तदधम परम मम 156
Translations:
1. That supreme light upon reaching which they do not return any more is Mine neither the sun nor the moon nor the fire illuminates It
2. Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire illuminates That That is My supreme Abode to which they do not return
3. Neither does the sun illuminate there nor the moon nor the fire having gone there they do not return that is My supreme abode
4. The sun does not illuminate That nor the moon nor the fire That is My Supreme Abode having gone to Which they Yogins never return
5. Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire shines there Those who go there never come back For O Arjuna that is my celestial home
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ममवश जवलक जवभत सनतन मनषषठननदरयण परकतसथन करषत 157
Translations:
1. An everlasting part of Myself having become the bound self in the world of life attracts the senses of which the mind is the sixth and which abide in Prakriti
2. It is indeed a part of Mine that becoming the eternal individual soul in the region of living beings draws the organs which have the mind as their sixth and which abide in Nature
3. An eternal portion of Myself having become a living soul in the world of life draws to itself the five senses with the mind as the sixth abiding in Nature
4. A portion of My own Self having become the eternal individual Soul in the world of the living beings draws into service the sense organs of which the sixth is the mind and which rest in Prakriti
5. It is only a very small part of My Eternal Self which is the life of the universe that draws around itself the six senses with the mind being the last which have their source in Nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरर यदवपनत यचचपयतकरमतशवर गहतवतन सयत वयरगनधनवशयत 158
Translations:
1. Whatever body its lord acquires and from whatever body it departs it goes on its way taking these senses as the wind carries scents from their places
