2. When the master leaves it and even when he assumes a body he departs taking these with him just as the wind carries away odours from their receptacles
3. When the Lord as the individual soul obtains a body and when He leaves it He takes these with Him just as the wind takes the scents from their seats flowers etc
4. Whatever body He attains to and also from whatever He goes up the Lord proceeds taking them with Him just as the wind takes odours from their receptacle
5. When the Supreme Lord enters a body or leaves it He gathers these senses together and travels with them as the wind gathers perfume while passing through the flowers
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरतर चकष सपरशन च रसन घरणमव च अधषठय मनशचय वषयनपसवत 159
Translations:
1. Presiding over the ear the eye the sense of touch the tongue the nose and the mind It experiences these objects of the senses
2. This one enjoys the objects by presiding over the ears eyes skin tongue nose and mind
3. Presiding over the ears eyes touch taste smell and mind it enjoys the objects of the senses
4. Presiding over the ear the eye the sense of touch the sense of taste the sense of smell and the mind He enjoys the sense objects
5. He is the perception of the ear the eye the touch the taste the smell and the mind and the enjoyment of the things they perceive is also His
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: उतकरमनत सथत वप भञजन व गणनवतम वमढ ननपशयनत पशयनत जञनचकषष 1510
Translations:
1. The deluded do not perceive it conjoined with the Gunas when departing staying or experiencing Those who have the eye of knowledge however see it
2. Persons who are diversely deluded do not see it even when it is leaving residing experiencing or in association with the qualities those with the eye of knowledge see
3. The deluded do not see Him who departs stays and enjoys but those who possess the eye of knowledge behold Him
4. The deluded do not perceive Him but the men of knowledgeeye do see Him as He dwells rises up or enjoys what is endowed with strands
5. The ignorant do not see that it is He who is present in life and departs at death or even that it is He who enjoys pleasure through the qualities Only the eye of wisdom sees this
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतनत यगनशचन पशयनतयतमनयवसथतम यतनतऽपयकततमन नन पशयनतयचतस 1511
Translations:
1. The striving yogis perceive it established in themselves But though striving those of unrefined minds devoid of intelligence do not perceive it
2. And the diligent yogis see this one existing within themselves The nondiscriminating ones who lack selfcontrol however do not see this one even though they are diligent
3. The yogis striving for perfection behold Him dwelling in the Self but the unrefined and unintelligent even though striving do not see Him
4. The exerting men of Yoga perceive Him dwelling in the Self however the unintelligent men with their uncontrolled selves do not perceive Him even though they exert
5. The saints with great effort find Him within themselves however the unintelligent despite their efforts cannot control their minds
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यददतयगत तज जगदभसयतऽखलम यचचनदरमस यचचगन तततज वदध ममकम 1512
Translations:
1. Know that the brilliance which illumines the whole universe in the sun the moon and fire is Mine
2. That light in the sun which illuminates the whole world that which is in the moon and that which is in fireknow that light to be Mine
3. That light which resides in the sun illuminating the whole world that which is in the moon and in the fireknow that light to be Mine
4. That light which is found in the sun which is in the moon and which is also in fireilluminating the entire worldknow that light to be of Mine
5. Remember that the light which proceeds from the sun illuminating the whole world and the light which is in the moon and that which is in the fire all are born of me
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: गमवशय च भतन धरयमयहमजस पषणम चषध सरव सम भतव रसतमक 1513
Translations:
1. And entering the earth I uphold all beings with My strength I nourish all herbs becoming the juicy Soma
2. And entering the Earth I sustain the beings through My power and nourish all the plants by becoming Soma which is of the nature of sap
3. Permeating the earth I support all beings with My energy and having become the watery moon I nourish all herbs
4. And penetrating the earth I support all beings with My energy being the sapfilled moon I nourish all plants
5. I enter this world and animate all My creatures with My vitality and by My cool moonbeams I nourish the plants
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अह वशवनर भतव परणन दहमशरत परणपनसमयकत पचमयनन चतरवधम 1514
Translations:
1. Becoming the digestive fire I function within the bodies of all living beings In union with the inward and outward breath I digest the four kinds of food
2. Taking the form of Vaisvanara and residing in the bodies of creatures I in association with Prana and Apana digest the four kinds of food
3. Having become the fire Vaisvanara I abide in the bodies of living beings and associated with the Prana and the Apana digest the fourfold food
4. Being the digestive fire dwelling within the bodies of living creatures and being in association with the upward and downward winds of the body I digest the four kinds of food
5. Becoming the fire of life I pass into their bodies and uniting with the vital streams of Prāṇa and Apāna I digest the various kinds of food
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवसय चह हद सननवषट मतत समतरजञनमपहन च वदशच सरवरहमव वदय वदनतकदवदवदव चहम 1515
Translations:

1. And I am seated in the hearts of all From Me come memory knowledge and their removal too Indeed I alone am to be known from all the Vedas I bring about the fruition of the Vedic rituals I alone am the knower of the Vedas

2. And I am seated in the hearts of all From Me come memory knowledge and their loss I alone am the object to be known through all the Vedas I am also the originator of the Vedanta and I Myself am the knower of the Vedas

3. And I am seated in the hearts of all from Me come memory and knowledge as well as their absence I am verily That which has to be known by all the Vedas I am indeed the author of the Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas

4. I am entered in the hearts of all from Me come the faculties of memory knowledge and discernment none but Me is to be known by means of all the Vedas and I am alone the author of the final part of the Vedas and also the author of the Vedas themselves
5. I am enthroned in the hearts of all memory wisdom and discrimination owe their origins to Me I am He Who is to be realized in the scriptures I inspire their wisdom and I know their truth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दववम परष लक कषरशचकषर एव च कषर सरवण भतन कटसथऽकषर उचयत 1516
Translations:
1. There are two kinds of persons spoken of in the sastra the perishable ksara and the imperishable aksara All beings are perishable while the imperishable is called the unchanging kutastha
2. There are two kinds of people in the world the mutable and the immutable The mutable consists of all things the one existing as Maya is referred to as the immutable
3. Two Purushas there are in this world the perishable and the imperishable All beings are perishable and the Kutasthathe unchangingis called the imperishable
4. There are two people in the world the perishable and the imperishable The perishable is all elements and the one that speaks is called the imperishable
5. There are two aspects in Nature the perishable and the imperishable All life in this world belongs to the former while the unchanging element belongs to the latter
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: उततम परषसतवनय परमतमतयदहत य लकतरयमवशय बभरतयवयय ईशवर 1517
Translations:
1. There is a Supreme Person other than these He is referred to as the Supreme Self Paramatma in all the Vedas He as the Immutable One and the Lord enters the threefold world and supports it
2. But the Supreme Person is different He is spoken of as the transcendental Self permeating the three worlds and upholding them and He is the imperishable God
3. But distinct is the Supreme Purusha called the highest Self indestructible and Lord who pervades the three worlds and sustains them
4. But the Highest Person distinct from both this is spoken of as the Supreme Self which being the changeless Lord sustains the triad of the world by entering into it
5. But I am higher than all the Supreme God the Absolute Self the Eternal Lord who pervades and upholds all the worlds
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसमतकषरमततऽहमकषरदप चततम अतऽसम लक वद च परथत परषततम 1518
Translations:
1. Because I transcend the perishable person and am also higher than the imperishable one therefore I am referred to in the Smrti and the Veda as the Supreme Person Purusotama
2. Since I am transcendental to the mutable and above even the immutable therefore I am well known in the world and in the Vedas as the supreme Person
3. As I transcend the perishable and am even higher than the imperishable I am declared to be the highest Purusha in the world and in the Vedas
4. Because I have transcended both the perishable and the imperishable I am acclaimed in the world and in the Vedas as the highest of persons
5. I am beyond comparison with the Eternal and the noneternal I am known as the Supreme Personality the Highest God by scriptures and sages
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य ममवमसममढ जनत परषततमम स सरववदभजत म सरवभवन भरत 1519
Translations:
1. He who without delusion thus knows Me as the Supreme Self knows all O Arjuna and worships Me in every way
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty he who being free from delusion knows Me the supreme Person thus is allknowing and adores Me with his whole being
3. He who undeluded knows Me as the highest Purusha he knowing all worships Me with his whole being heart O Arjuna
4. He who not being deluded thus knows Me as the Highest of personshe knows all and serves Me with his entire being O descendant of Bharata
5. He who with unclouded vision sees Me as the LordGod knows all that there is to be known and will always worship Me with his whole heart
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 15.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 15.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इत गहयतम शसतरमदमकत मयऽनघ एतदबदधव बदधमनसयतकतकतयशच भरत 1520
Translations:
1. Thus O sinless one this most mysterious doctrine has been imparted by Me By understanding this one will become truly wise and fulfill their duty
2. O sinless one I have thus uttered this most secret scripture Understanding this one becomes wise and fulfills their duties O scion of the Bharata dynasty
3. Thus I have imparted to you this most secret science O sinless one by knowing this one becomes wise and all their duties are accomplished O Arjuna

4. Thus O sinless one I have imparted to you the most secret scripture by understanding this let a man become wise and also become one who has accomplished what needs to be accomplished O descendant of Bharata
5. Thus O sinless one I have revealed this most mystic knowledge to thee He who understands it gains wisdom and attains the consummation of life
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.1 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अभय सततवसशदध जञनयगवयवसथत दन दमशच यजञशच सवधययसतप आरजवम 161
Translations:
1. The Lord said Fearlessness purity of mind devotion to meditation on the knowledge of the self almsgiving selfcontrol worship study of the Vedas austerity and uprightness
2. The Blessed Lord said Fearlessness purity of mind persistence in knowledge and yoga charity control of the external organs sacrifice scriptural study austerity and rectitude
3. The Blessed Lord said Fearlessness purity of heart steadfastness in knowledge and yoga almsgiving control of the senses sacrifice study of scriptures austerity and straightforwardness
4. The Bhagavat said Fearlessness complete purity of the Sattva steadfastness in knowledgeYoga charity selfrestraint Vedic sacrifice recitation of scriptures austerity and uprightness
5. Lord Shri Krishna continued Fearlessness clean living unceasing concentration on wisdom readiness to give selfcontrol a spirit of sacrifice regular study of the scriptures austerity candor
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अहस सतयमकरधसतयग शनतरपशनम दय भतषवललपतव मरदव हररचपलम 162
Translations:
1. Noninjury truth freedom from anger renunciation tranquility nonslandering of others compassion for all beings freedom from desire gentleness a sense of shame and freedom from fickleness
2. Noninjury truthfulness absence of anger renunciation control of the inner self absence of vilification kindness to creatures noncovetousness gentleness modesty and freedom from restlessness
3. Harmlessness truth absence of anger renunciation peacefulness absence of crookedness compassion for beings noncovetousness gentleness modesty and absence of fickleness
4. Harmlessness truth absence of anger renunciation absence of attachment absence of slander compassion for living beings and absence of greed gentleness modesty and absence of thoughtlessness
5. Harmlessness truth absence of wrath renunciation contentment straightforwardness compassion towards all uncovetousness courtesy modesty and constancy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तज कषम धत शचमदरह नतमनत भवनत समपद दवमभजतसय भरत 163
Translations:
1. Grandeur patience fortitude purity freedom from hatred and overpridethese O Arjuna belong to one who is born to a divine destiny
2. Vigour forgiveness fortitude purity freedom from malice absence of haughtinessthese O scion of the Bharata dynasty are the qualities of one born destined to have the divine nature
3. Vigor forgiveness fortitude purity absence of hatred absence of pridethese belong to one born for a divine state O Arjuna
4. Vital power forgiveness fortitude contentment absence of treachery and absence of excessive pridethese are in the person who is born for divine wealth O Descendant of Bharata
5. Valor forgiveness fortitude purity freedom from hate and vanitythese are the qualities of one who possesses the divine O Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दमभ दरपऽभमनशच करध परषयमव च अजञन चभजतसय परथ समपदमसरम 164
Translations:
1. Pompousness arrogance selfconceit wrath rudeness and ignorancethese O Arjuna belong to one who is born with a demonic destiny
2. O son of Prtha the attributes of one destined to have the demoniacal nature are religious ostentation pride haughtiness selfconceit anger rudeness and ignorance
3. Hypocrisy arrogance and selfconceit anger harshness and ignorancethese belong to one who is born for a demoniacal state O Partha
4. Ostentation arrogance pride anger and harshness as well as ignorance are characteristics of a person born of demoniac wealth O son of Prtha
5. Hypocrisy pride insolence cruelty and ignorance belong to those born of godless qualities
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दव समपदवमकषय नबनधयसर मत म शच समपद दवमभजतऽस पणडव 165
Translations:
1. The divine destiny is said to lead to liberation while the demoniac to bondage Do not grieve O Arjuna for you are born with a divine destiny
2. The divine nature is Liberation while the demoniacal is considered to be an inevitable bondage Do not grieve O son of Pandu You are destined to possess the divine nature
3. The divine nature is deemed conducive to liberation and the demonic to bondage Grieve not O Arjuna for you are born with divine endowments
4. The divine wealth is meant for total emancipation and the demoniac one for complete bondage Grieve not O son of Pandu for the divine wealth you are born
5. Godly qualities lead to liberation godless ones lead to bondage Do not be anxious Prince You have the Godly qualities
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दव भतसरग लकऽसमन दव आसर एव च दव वसतरश परकत आसर परथ म शरण 166
Translations:
1. There are two types of beings in this world the divine and the demoniac The divine have been described at length Hear from Me O Arjuna about the demoniac
2. In this world there are two kinds of creation of beings the divine and the demoniacal The divine has been spoken of elaborately Hear about the demoniacal from Me O son of Prtha
3. There are two types of beings in this world the divine and the demoniacal The divine has been described at length hear from Me O Arjuna about the demoniacal
4. There are two types of creations of beings in this world the divine and the demoniac The divine one has been described in detail hear now the demoniac one from Me O son of Prtha
5. No changes needed
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परवतत च नवतत च जन न वदरसर न शच नप चचर न सतय तष वदयत 167
Translations:
1. The demoniacal men neither know action nor renunciation They have no cleanliness nor right conduct nor truth
2. Neither do the demoniacal persons understand what should be done and what should not be done nor do they possess purity good conduct or truthfulness
3. The demoniacal do not know what to do and what to refrain from they have neither purity nor right conduct nor truth
4. The demoniac do not know the origin and the dissolution neither purity nor good conduct nor truth exists in them
5. The godless do not know how to act or renounce they lack both purity and truth They do not understand the right principles of conduct
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: असतयमपरतषठ त जगदहरनशवरम अपरसपरसमभत कमनयतकमहतकम 168
Translations:
1. They maintain The universe is without truth with no foundation and without a Lord Isvara What else could exist without mutual causation It has lust as its cause
2. They say that the world is unreal having no basis and without a God It is born of mutual union brought about by passion What other cause could there be
3. They say This universe is without truth without a moral basis without a God brought about by mutual union with lust as its cause what else
4. They say that this world is without truth has no basis and has no Lord this is not born on the basis of the mutual causeandeffectrelation of the things it has nothing beyond and has no cause
5. They say the universe is an accident with no purpose and no God Life is created through sexual union a product of lust and nothing else
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एत दषटमवषटभय नषटतमनऽलपबदधय परभवनतयगरकरमण कषयय जगतऽहत 169
Translations:
1. Holding this view these men of lost souls and feeble understanding do cruel deeds leading to the destruction of the world
2. Holding onto this view those of depraved character poor intellect and given to fearful and harmful actions wax strong and bring ruin to the world
3. Holding this view these ruined souls of small intellect and fierce deeds come forth as enemies of the world intent on its destruction
4. Clinging to this view the inauspicious men of ruined souls poor intellect and cruel deeds strive to destroy the world
5. Thinking thus these degraded souls these enemies of mankindwhose intelligence is negligible and whose deeds are monstrouscome into the world only to destroy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कममशरतय दषपर दमभमनमदनवत महदगहतवसदगरहनपरवरतनतऽशचवरत 1610
Translations:
1. Turning to insatiable desires seizing through delusion unjustly acquired wealth and following impious vows they act full of ostentation pride and arrogance
2. Giving themselves up to insatiable passion filled with vanity pride and arrogance adopting bad adjectives due to delusion and having impure resolves they engage in actions
3. Filled with insatiable desires full of hypocrisy pride and arrogance holding evil ideas due to delusion they work with impure intentions
4. Holding to their insatiable desires being possessed by hypocrisy avarice and pride and holding evil intentions these cruel men wander with impure resolves
5. Giving themselves up to insatiable passions hypocritically selfsufficiently and arrogantly cherishing false conceptions founded on delusion they work only to carry out their own unholy purposes
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: चनतमपरमय च परलयनतमपशरत कमपभगपरम एतवदत नशचत 1611
Translations:
1. Obsessed by unlimited cares that end with dissolution looking upon the enjoyment of desires as their highest aim and convinced that this is all
2. Beset with innumerable cares that end only with death holding that the enjoyment of desirable objects is the highest goal they feel sure that this is all
3. Giving themselves over to immeasurable cares that end only with death regarding the gratification of lust as their highest aim and feeling sure that that is all
4. Adhering to their ultimate and unending anxiety viewing the gratification of their desires as their highest goal believing that this is all that exists
5. Poring anxiously over evil resolutions that only end in death seeking only the gratification of desire as the highest goal and seeing nothing beyond
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आशपशशतरबदध कमकरधपरयण ईहनत कमभगरथमनययनरथसञचयन 1612
Translations:
1. Bound by hundreds of fetters of hopes given over to desire and anger they strive unjustly to gather wealth for the gratification of their desires
2. Bound by hundreds of shackles in the form of hope giving themselves wholly to passion and anger they endeavor to amass wealth through foul means for the enjoyment of desirable objects
3. Bound by a hundred ties of hope given over to lust and anger they strive to obtain hoards of wealth by unlawful means for sensual enjoyment
4. Bound by hundreds of ropes of longing devoted to their desire and anger they seek by unjust means hoards of wealth for the gratification of their desires
5. Caught in the toils of a hundred vain hopes enslaved by passion and wrath they accumulate hoards of unjust wealth only to indulge their sensual desires
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: इदमदय मय लबधमम परपसय मनरथम इदमसतदमप म भवषयत पनरधनम 1613
Translations:
1. This I have gained today and this desire I shall attain This wealth is mine and this will also be mine hereafter
2. I have gained this today I will acquire this desired object This is in hand and this wealth will also come to me
3. I have gained this today I will fulfill this desire of mine this is mine and this wealth will be mine in the future
4. I have gained this today I will attain this object of my desire in the future this is mine now and this wealth will also be mine soon
5. Today I have gained this tomorrow I will fulfill another desire this wealth is mine now and the rest will soon be mine
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अस मय हत शतररहनषय चपरनप ईशवरऽहमह भग सदधऽह बलवनसख 1614
Translations:
1. I have slain this enemy and I shall slay others too I am the Lord the enjoyer the successful one the strong one and the happy one
2. I have slain that enemy and I shall slay others too I am the Lord the Enjoyer firmly established powerful and joyous
3. I have slain that enemy and I shall slay others too I am the Lord I enjoy I am perfect powerful and happy
4. I have slain that enemy and I shall slay others too I am the Lord I am a man of pleasure I am victorious powerful and content
5. I have slain one enemy I will slay the others too I am worthy of enjoying I am the Almighty perfect powerful and happy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आढयऽभजनवनसम कऽनयऽसत सदश मय यकषय दसयम मदषय इतयजञनवमहत 1615
Translations:
1. I am wealthy and highborn who else is equal to me I shall sacrifice I shall give alms I shall rejoice Thus they think deluded by ignorance
2. I am wealthy and of noble birth who else is like me I shall perform sacrifices I shall give I shall rejoice thus they are deluded by a lack of discernment in diverse ways
3. I am wealthy and born into a noble family Who is my equal I shall perform sacrifices give charity and rejoice thus deluded by ignorance
4. I am wealthy I am of noble birth who else is equal to me I shall perform sacrifices I shall give gifts and I shall rejoice deluded by these wrong ideas
5. I am wealthy I am of noble birth who can compare with me I will sacrifice I will give I will payand I will enjoy Thus blinded by ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनकचततवभरनत महजलसमवत परसकत कमभगष पतनत नरकऽशच 1616
Translations:
1. Bewildered by numerous thoughts ensnared in the net of delusion addicted to sensual pleasures they fall into a wretched Naraka
2. Bewildered by numerous thoughts ensnared in the net of delusion and engrossed in the enjoyment of desirable objects they fall into a foul hell
3. Bewildered by many fancies entangled in the snare of delusion addicted to the gratification of lust they fall into a foul hell
4. Endowed with many thoughts highly confused enslaved by their delusion and addicted to the gratification of desires they fall into the hellish and foul
5. Perplexed by discordant thoughts entangled in the snares of desire infatuated by passion they sink into the depths of hell
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आतमसमभवत सतबध धनमनमदनवत यजनत नमयजञसत दमभनवधपरवकम 1617
Translations:
1. Selfconceited selfsufficient intoxicated by wealth and pride they perform sacrifices in name only ostentatiously and not according to the injunctions of the scriptures
2. Selfconceited haughty filled with pride and intoxicated by wealth they perform sacrifices that are only in name ostentatiously and without regard for the injunctions
3. Selfconceited stubborn filled with pride and intoxication of wealth they perform sacrifices in name only for ostentation contrary to scriptural ordinances
4. Selfconceited stubborn filled with pride and arrogance of wealth they pretend to perform sacrifices hypocritically not following the injunctions of the Vedas
5. Selfconceited stubborn wealthy proud and insolent they make a show of their patronage disregarding the norms of propriety
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अहङकर बल दरप कम करध च सशरत ममतमपरदहष परदवषनतऽभयसयक 1618
Translations:
1. Depending on their egoism power and pride as well as their desires and wrath these malicious men hate Me in their own bodies and in those of others
2. Resorting to egotism power arrogance passion and anger hating Me in their own and others bodies they become envious by nature
3. Given over to egoism power haughtiness lust and anger these malicious people hate Me in their own bodies and in the bodies of others
4. Clinging fast to egotism force pride craving and anger these envious people hate Me in the bodies of their own and of others
5. Puffed up by power and inordinate conceit swayed by lust and wrath these wicked people hate Me Who am within them as I am within all
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तनह दवषत कररनससरष नरधमन कषपमयजसरमशभनसरषवव यनष 1619
Translations:
1. Those who are hateful cruel the vilest and most inauspicious of mankind I hurl forever into the cycles of births and deaths into the wombs of demons
2. I cast forever those hateful cruel evildoers in the worlds the vilest of human beings verily into the demonic classes
3. Those cruel haters the worst among men in the world I hurl those evildoers into the wombs of demons only
4. These hateful cruel and base men I hurl incessantly into the inauspicious demoniac wombs alone in the cycle of birth and death
5. Those who thus hate Me who are cruel the dregs of mankind I condemn them to a continuous miserable and godless rebirth
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: असर यनमपनन मढ जनमन जनमन ममपरपयव कनतय तत यनतयधम गतम 1620
Translations:
1. Fallen into demoniac wombs in birth after birth these deluded men not attaining Me further sink down to the lowest level O Arjuna
2. Being born among the demoniacal species in birth after birth the souls without ever reaching Me O son of Kunti attain conditions lower than that
3. Entering into demoniacal wombs and deluded birth after birth they do not attain Me thus falling O Arjuna into a condition still lower than that
4. Having come to the demoniac womb birth after birth and not attaining Me at all these deluded persons therefore pass to the lowest state O son of Kunti
5. So reborn they spend life after life enveloped in delusion and they never reach Me O Prince but degenerate into even lower forms of life
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तरवध नरकसयद दवर नशनमतमन कम करधसतथ लभसतसमदतततरय तयजत 1621
Translations:
1. Desire wrath and greedthese are the three gateways to Naraka ruinous to the self Therefore one should abandon these three
2. This door to hell which destroys the soul is of three kindspassion anger and greed Therefore one should forsake these three
3. There are three gates to this hell destructive of the self lust anger and greed therefore one should abandon these three
4. To the hell there are three gates that ruin the Self desire anger and greed Hence one should avoid these three
5. The gates of hell are three lust wrath and avarice they destroy the Self so avoid them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एतरवमकत कनतय तमदवरसतरभरनर आचरतयतमन शरयसतत यत पर गतम 1622
Translations:
1. One who has been released from these three gates of darkness O Arjuna works for the benefit of the self thus they reach the supreme state
2. O son of Kunti a person who is free from these three doors to darkness strives for the good of the soul thereby they attain the highest Goal
3. A person who is liberated from these three gates of darkness O Arjuna practices what is beneficial for them and thus goes to the Supreme Goal
4. O son of Kunti A man who has deserted these three gates of darkness does what is good for his Self and thereby reaches the highest goal
5. These are the gates that lead to darkness if one avoids them they will ensure their own welfare and eventually attain liberation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य शसतरवधमतसजय वरतत कमकरत न स सदधमवपनत न सख न पर गतम 1623
Translations:
1. He who abandoning the injunctions of the Sastras acts under the influence of desire attains neither perfection nor pleasure nor the supreme state
2. Ignoring the precepts of the scriptures he who acts under the impulse of passion does not attain perfection nor happiness nor the supreme goal
3. He who having cast aside the ordinances of the scriptures acts under the impulse of desire does not attain perfection nor happiness nor the Supreme Goal
4. He who neglects the injunctions of the scriptures and acts according to his own will attains neither success nor happiness nor the highest goal of emancipation
5. But he who neglects the commands of the scriptures and follows the promptings of passion does not attain perfection happiness or the final goal
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 16.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 16.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमचछसतर परमण त करयकरयवयवसथत जञतव शसतरवधनकत करम करतमहरहस 1624
Translations:
1. Therefore let the Sastra be your authority for determining what should be done and what should not be done Knowing what is enjoined in the injunctions of the Sastra you should perform work here

2. Therefore the scripture is your authority as regards determining what should be done and what should not be done After understanding your duty as presented by scriptural injunction you ought to perform it here

3. Therefore let the scripture be thy authority in determining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done Having known what is said in the ordinance of the scriptures thou shouldst act in this world
4. Therefore by considering the scripture as your authority in determining what should be done and what should not be done you should perform actions laid down by the regulations of the scriptures
5. Therefore whenever there is doubt as to whether you should do something or not let the scriptures guide your conduct In the light of the scriptures you should labor your whole life
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.1 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 17.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: य शसतरवधमतसजय यजनत शरदधयऽनवत तष नषठ त क कषण सततवमह रजसतम 171
Translations:
1. Arjuna said Now O Krsna what is the position or basis of those who despite leaving aside the injunction of the Sastra still worship with faith Is it Sattva Rajas or Tamas

2. Arjuna said But O Krsna what is the state of those who endued with faith perform sacrifices distribute wealth etc in honour of gods and others while ignoring the injunctions of the scriptures Is it sattva rajas or tamas
3. Arjuna said What is the condition of those who disregarding the injunctions of the scriptures perform sacrifice with faithis it Sattva Rajas or Tamas O Krishna
4. Arjuna said What is the state of those who remain faithful but neglect the scriptural injunctionsis it sattva rajas or tamas O Krsna
5. Arjuna asked My Lord what is the condition of those who perform acts of sacrifice not according to the scriptures but with implicit faith Is it one of purity passion or ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तरवध भवत शरदध दहन स सवभवज सततवक रजस चव तमस चत त शरण 172
Translations:
1. The Lord said The faith of embodied beings is threefold born of their own nature and constituted of Sattva Rajas and Tamas Now listen to me about it
2. The Blessed Lord said That faith of the embodied beings born of their own nature is threefoldborn of sattva rajas and tamas Hear about it
3. The Blessed Lord said There are threefold faiths inherent in the nature of the embodied the sattvic pure the rajasic passionate and the tamasic dark Hear of them

4. The Bhagavat said The faith of the embodied persons is born of their nature and is of three kinds viz that which is made of Sattva that which is made of Rajas and that which is made of Tamas Listen about them
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied Man has an inherent faith in one or another of the qualitiespurity passion and ignorance Now listen
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सततवनरप सरवसय शरदध भवत भरत शरदधमयऽय परष य यचछरदध स एव स 173
Translations:
1. O Arjuna the faith of everyone is in accordance with their inner organ Antahkarana Each person consists of faith whatever their faith is that is who they are
2. O scion of the Bharata dynasty the faith of all beings is in accordance with their minds This person is composed of faith as the dominant factor He is indeed what his faith is
3. The faith of each is in accordance with their nature O Arjuna People consist of their faith as a persons faith is so are they
4. Corresponding to ones own sattva O descendant of Bharata everybody has faith The person predominantly consists of faith Whatever one has faith in that he becomes certainly
5. Every persons faith conforms to their nature By nature they are full of faith They are in fact what their faith makes them
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यजनत सततवक दवनयकषरकषस रजस परतनभतगणशचनय यजनत तमस जन 174
Translations:
1. The sattvic types worship the gods the rajasic types worship yaksas and rakshasas and the others the tamasic types worship the departed ancestors and hosts of bhutas
2. Those with sattva quality worship the gods those with rajas the demigods and demons and those with tamas ghosts and hosts of spirits
3. The sattvic or pure men worship the gods the rajasic or passionate worship the yakshas and rakshasas the others the tamasic or deluded people worship ghosts and hosts of naturespirits

4. The men of the Sattva strand perform sacrifices intending for the gods the men of the Rajas strand do so for the spirits and demons and the men of the Tamas strand perform sacrifices intending for the imps the dead and the ghosts
5. The pure worship the true God the passionate the powers of wealth and magic the ignorant the spirits of the dead and lower orders of nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अशसतरवहत घर तपयनत य तप जन दमभहङकरसयकत कमरगबलनवत 175
Translations:
1. Those men who practice terrible austerities not enjoined by the scriptures given to ostentation and conceit and prompted by the force of sensual desires and passions
2. Those persons who given to ostentation and pride and possessed of passion attachment and strength undertake severe austerities not sanctioned by the scriptures
3. Those men who practice terrific austerities not prescribed by the scriptures given to hypocrisy and egoism driven by the force of lust and attachment
4. Those men who practise terrible austerities not as enjoined in the scriptures who are bound by hypocrisy and conceit and are impelled by the force of passion for desired objects
5. Those who practice austerities not commanded by scripture who are enslaved to hypocrisy and egotism who are carried away by the fury of desire and passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करषयनत शररसथ भतगरममचतस म चवनतशररसथ तनवदधयसरनशचयन 176
Translations:
1. These foolish men who torture the group of elements in their bodies and Me who dwell within the bodyknow them to be demoniacal in their resolves
2. And those who being nondiscriminating torture all the organs in the body as well as even Me who reside in the bodyknow them to be possessed of demoniacal conviction
3. Know thou these to be of demonical resolves senselessly torturing all the elements in the body and Me who dwell in the body
4. Those who unintelligently emaciate the conglomeration of elements in their physical body and emaciate Me dwelling within the physical bodyknow them to be of a demoniac resolve
5. They are ignorant and torment the organs of the body harassing Me who lives within Know that they are devoted to evil
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आहरसतवप सरवसय तरवध भवत परय यजञसतपसतथ दन तष भदमम शरण 177
Translations:
1. Even the food that is dear to all is threefold so too are sacrifices austerities and gifts Listen to this distinction among them
2. Food which is dear to all is of three kinds likewise sacrifices austerity and charity are also of three kinds Listen to this classification of them
3. The food that is dear to each is threefold as well as sacrifice austerity and almsgiving Hear the distinction of these
4. Further the food which is dear to all is of three kinds So too are their sacrifice austerity and charity Listen to this distinction of them
5. The food that people enjoy is also threefold like the ways of sacrifice austerity and almsgiving Listen to the distinction
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: आयसततवबलरगयसखपरतववरधन रसय सनगध सथर हदय आहर सततवकपरय 178
Translations:
1. Foods that promote longevity intellectual alertness strength health pleasure and happiness and those that are sweet oily substantial and agreeable are dear to sattvic men
2. Foods that augment life firmness of mind strength health happiness and delight and which are succulent oleaginous substantial and agreeable are dear to one endowed with sattva
3. The foods that increase life purity strength health joy and cheerfulness good appetite which are savory oily substantial and agreeable are dear to the Sattvic pure people

4. The foods that increase life energy strength good health happiness and satisfaction which are delicious soft substantial and pleasant to the heart stomach they are dear to the men of the Sattva strand
5. The foods that prolong life increase purity vigour health cheerfulness and happiness are those that are delicious soothing substantial and agreeable these are loved by the pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कटवमललवणतयषणतकषणरकषवदहन आहर रजससयषट दखशकमयपरद 179
Translations:
1. Foods that are bitter sour very salty excessively heating highly pungent dry and burning are all dear to Rajasika men they cause pain sorrow and disease
2. Foods that are bitter sour salty very hot pungent dry and burning producing pain sorrow and disease are dear to one who has rajas
3. The foods that are bitter sour salty overly hot pungent dry and burning are liked by the Rajasic and are productive of pain grief and disease
4. The foods killed by men of the Rajas strand are those that are bitter sour salty very hot harsh dry and burning and which cause pain grief and disease
5. Those in whom passion is dominant like foods that are bitter sour salty overly hot pungent dry and burning These produce unhappiness repentance and disease
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतयम गतरस पत परयषत च यत उचछषटमप चमधय भजन तमसपरयम 1710
Translations:
1. That food which is stale tasteless putrid decayed rejected unclean is dear to those of a Tamasic nature
2. Food that is not properly cooked lacking in essence putrid and stale as well as that which is unfit for sacrifice is dear to one possessed of tamas
3. That which is stale tasteless putrid rotten rejected and impure is the food liked by the Tamasic
4. What is old tasteless foulsmelling and stale what is also left over after eating and is impure such food is dear to the men of Tamas Strand
5. The ignorant love food that is stale not nourishing putrid and corrupt the leftovers of others and unclean
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अफलकङकषभरयजञ वधदषट य इजयत यषटवयमवत मन समधय स सततवक 1711
Translations:
1. The sacrifice worship marked by Sattva is what is offered by those who desire no fruit and have the conviction that it should be performed as prescribed in the scriptures
2. That sacrifice which is in accordance with the injunctions performed by persons who do not hanker after results and with the mental conviction that it is surely obligatory is done through sattva
3. That sacrifice which is offered by men without desire for reward as enjoined by the ordinance scripture with a firm faith that doing so is their duty is Sattvic or pure
4. That sacrifice of the Sattva Strand is offered as prescribed by those who desire no reward steadying their minds with the thought that it is simply something to be offered
5. Sacrifice is pure when it is offered by one who does not covet the fruit of it when it is done according to the commands of scripture and with implicit faith that the sacrifice is a duty
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अभसधय त फल दमभरथमप चव यत इजयत भरतशरषठ त यजञ वदध रजसम 1712
Translations:
1. But that sacrifice which is offered with the intention of gaining a reward and for the sake of show know it O Arjuna to be Rajasic
2. But that sacrifice which is performed with an aim for a result as well as for showknow that sacrifice to be done through rajas O greatest among the descendants of Bharata
3. The sacrifice that is offered O Arjuna seeking a reward and for show know that to be a Rajasic Yajna
4. What is offered aiming at fruit and also only for the sake of displayknow that sacrifice to be of the Rajas strand and to be transitory and impermanent
5. Sacrifice which is performed for the sake of its results or for selfglorification that O best of Aryans is the product of passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वधहनमसषटनन मनतरहनमदकषणम शरदधवरहत यजञ तमस परचकषत 1713
Translations:

1. That sacrifice which is devoid of authority which uses offerings not sanctioned by the Sastras which is performed without recitation of hymns and devoid of gifts and faith that they say is marked by Tamas

2. They declare that sacrifice as done through tamas which is contrary to injunction in which no food is distributed in which no mantras are used in which no offerings are made to priests and which is devoid of faith
3. They declare that sacrifice to be Tamasic which is contrary to the ordinances of the scriptures in which no food is distributed and which is devoid of mantras gifts and faith

4. They declare that sacrifice to be of the Tamas Strand which is devoid of scriptural injunction in which there is no recitation of Vedic hymns where no food and sacrificial fee are distributed and which is completely devoid of faith
5. Sacrifice that is contrary to scriptural command that is unaccompanied by prayers or gifts of food or money and is without faiththat is the product of ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दवदवजगरपरजञपजन शचमरजवम बरहमचरयमहस च शरर तप उचयत 1714
Translations:
1. Worshiping the gods the twiceborn the preceptors the enlightened ones practicing purity uprightness continence and noninjurythese are called austerity of the body
2. The worship of gods twiceborns venerable persons and the wise purity straightforwardness celibacy and noninjurythese are said to be bodily austerities
3. Worship of the gods the twiceborn the teachers and the wise purity straightforwardness celibacy and noninjury are all called the austerities of the body
4. The worship of the gods the twiceborn the elders and the wise purity honesty continence and harmlessnessall this is said to be bodily austerity
5. Worship of God and the Master respect for the preacher and the philosopher purity rectitude continence and harmlessnessall of these constitute physical austerity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनदवगकर वकय सतय परयहत च यत सवधययभयसन चव वङमय तप उचयत 1715
Translations:
1. Speech that causes no shock is true pleasant and beneficial and also the practice of reciting scriptures is called the austerity of speech
2. That speech which causes no pain which is true agreeable and beneficial as well as the practice of studying the scripturesis said to be the austerity of speech
3. Speech that causes no excitement is truthful pleasant and beneficial the practice of studying the Vedas is called austerity of speech
4. The unoffending speech which is true pleasant and beneficial as well as the practice of regular recitation of the Vedasall this is said to be an austerity of the speechsense
5. Speech that hurts no one that is true pleasant to listen to and beneficial and the constant study of the scripturesthis is austerity of speech
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मनपरसद समयतव मनमतमवनगरह भवसशदधरतयतततप मनसमचयत 1716
Translations:
1. Serenity of mind benevolence silence selfcontrol and purity of mindthese are known as austerity of the mind
2. Tranquility of mind gentleness reticence withdrawal of the mind purity of heartthese are what are called mental austerities
3. Serenity of mind goodheartedness selfcontrol and purity of naturethis is called mental austerity
4. The serenity of mind the stillness the taciturnity the selfcontrol the purity of thoughtall this is called mental austerity
5. Serenity kindness silence selfcontrol and puritythese are the austerities of the mind
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरदधय परय तपत तपसतततरवध नर अफलकङकषभरयकत सततवक परचकषत 1717
Translations:
1. The threefold austerity practiced with supreme faith by men who desire no fruit and are devotedthey call it austerity of Sattva
2. When that threefold austerity is undertaken with supreme faith by people who do not hanker after results and are selfcontrolled they speak of it as being born of sattva
3. This threefold austerity practiced by steadfast men with the utmost faith desiring no reward is called Sattvic
4. This threefold austerity observed with best faith by men who are masters of Yoga and have no desire for its fruitsthey call it to be of the Sattva
5. These threefold austerities performed with faith and without thought of reward can truly be considered pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सतकरमनपजरथ तप दमभन चव यत करयत तदह परकत रजस चलमधरवम 1718
Translations:
1. That austerity practiced with ostentation for the sake of gaining respect praise and reverence is here said to be Rajasa It is unsteady and impermanent
2. That austerity which is undertaken for earning a name being honored and worshipped and also ostentatiouslythat is spoken of as born of rajas belonging to this world uncertain and transitory
3. The austerity that is practiced with the aim of gaining good reception honor and worship and with hypocrisy is said to be Rajasic unstable and transient
4. The austerity that is practiced for gaining respect honor and reverence and with sheer showthat is called here austerity of the Rajas and it is unstable and impermanent
5. Austerity coupled with hypocrisy or performed for the sake of selfglorification popularity or vanity comes from passion and its result is always uncertain and temporary
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मढगरहणतमन यतपडय करयत तप परसयतसदनरथ व तततमसमदहतम 1719
Translations:
1. That austerity which is practised from deluded notions by means of selftorture or to injure another is said to be Tamasika
2. That austerity which is undertaken with a foolish intent causing pain to oneself or for the destruction of others is said to be born of tamas
3. That austerity which is practised out of a foolish notion with selftorture or for the purpose of destroying another is declared to be of the Tamasic nature
4. What austerity is practised with foolish obstinacy and selftorture only in order to destroy othersthat is declared to be of the Tamas
5. Austerity done under delusion and accompanied with sorcery or the infliction of pain upon oneself or another may be assumed to spring from ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दतवयमत यददन दयतऽनपकरण दश कल च पतर च तददन सततवक समतम 1720
Translations:
1. Gifts given with the feeling that it is ones own duty to give to one who makes no return at the proper place and time to the deserving personthat is said to be sattvika
2. That gift is referred to as born of sattva which is given with the idea that it should be given to one who will not serve in return at the right place right time and to the right person
3. That gift which is given to one who does nothing in return knowing it to be a duty to give in a suitable place and time to a worthy person is held to be Sattvic

4. A gift that is given with the thought that one must give and is given in an appropriate place and at the right time to a worthy person who is unable to repaythat gift is considered to be of the Sattva
5. The gift that is given without thought of recompense believing it should be made in an appropriate place at an opportune time and to a deserving personsuch a gift is pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतत परतयपकररथ फलमददशय व पन दयत च परकलषट तददन रजस समतम 1721
Translations:
1. But that which is given as a consideration for something received or in expectation of a future reward or grudgingly is called a Rajasika gift
2. But the gift which is given expecting reciprocation or with a desire for its result and grudgingly is considered to be born of rajas
3. And that gift which is given with the intention of receiving something in return or expecting a reward or begrudgingly is considered to be Rajasic
4. But what is given in return for a favor or with the expectation of a reward and which is done with much agitationthat gift is said to be of the Rajas
5. That which is given for the sake of the results it will produce or with the hope of recompense or grudginglythat may truly be said to be the outcome of passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अदशकल यददनमपतरभयशच दयत असतकतमवजञत तततमसमदहतम 1722
Translations:
1. That gift which is given at the wrong place wrong time to an unworthy recipient without due respect and with contempt is called the gift of a Tamasa nature
2. The gift which is made at an inappropriate place and time and to undeserving persons without proper respect and with disdain is declared to be born of tamas
3. The gift that is given in the wrong place and at the wrong time to unworthy persons without respect or with insult is declared to be of a Tamasic nature
4. The gift which is given at an inappropriate place time and to unworthy persons and which is converted into a bad act and is disrespectedthat is declared to be of the Tamas
5. And that which is given at an inappropriate place or time or to one who is undeserving or with disrespect or contempt such a gift is the result of ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ततसदत नरदश बरहमणसतरवध समत बरहमणसतन वदशच यजञशच वहत पर 1723
Translations:
1. Om Tat Satthus Brahman is denoted by this threefold expression In the past Brahmanas the Vedas and sacrifices were ordained in association with these
2. Omtatsat this is considered to be the threefold designation of Brahman The Brahmanas Vedas and sacrifices were ordained by that in days of yore
3. Om Tat Sat This has been declared to be the triple designation of Brahman By that the Brahmanas the Vedas and the sacrifices were created formerly
4. OM TAT SAT This is held to be the threefold indication of Brahman Through this the Vedas and sacrifices were fashioned by Brahma in the past
5. Om Tat Sat is the triple designation of the Eternal Spirit by which the Vedic Scriptures ceremonials and sacrifices were ordained of old
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तसमदमतयदहतय यजञदनतपकरय परवरतनत वधनकत सतत बरहमवदनम 1724
Translations:
1. Therefore the Vedaenjoined sacrificial acts gifts and austerities expounded by the Vedas or those belonging to the first three stations are always begun after pronouncing Om at the beginning
2. Therefore acts of sacrifice charity and austerity as prescribed through injunctions of those who study and expound the Vedas always commence after uttering the syllable Om
3. Therefore with the utterance of Om the acts of sacrifice gift and austerity as enjoined in the scriptures are always begun by the students of Brahman
4. Therefore the scriptureprescribed acts of sacrifice gift and austerity of those who are habituated to having Brahmandiscourses invariably commence with the utterance of OM
5. Therefore all acts of sacrifice gifts and austerities prescribed by the scriptures are always begun by those who understand the Spirit with the word Om
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तदतयनभसनधय फल यजञतपकरय दनकरयशच ववध करयनत मकषकङकषभ 1725
Translations:
1. Acts of sacrifice austerity and various gifts are performed without aiming at a reward by those who seek release after pronouncing Tat
2. After uttering the word tat acts of sacrifice and austerity as well as various acts of charity are performed without regard for results by persons aspiring for liberation
3. Uttering Tat without aiming for the fruits are the acts of sacrifice austerity and the various acts of gifts performed by those seeking liberation
4. With the utterance of TAT and without aiming for the fruit those who seek emancipation perform acts of sacrifice austerity and various acts of giving
5. Those who desire deliverance begin their acts of sacrifice austerity or gift with the word Tat meaning That without thought of reward
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सदभव सधभव च सदतयततपरयजयत परशसत करमण तथ सचछबद परथ यजयत 1726
Translations:
1. This term Sat is used in the sense of existence and goodness And so also O Arjuna the word Sat is applied to an auspicious action
2. This word sat is used with regard to something coming into being and someone becoming good Likewise O son of Prtha the word sat is used with regard to an auspicious rite
3. The word Sat is used to refer to reality and goodness likewise O Arjuna the word Sat is used to refer to an auspicious act

4. In the sense of right or manifesting as being and in the sense of proper or manifesting perfectly this word SAT is employed Likewise the word SAT is used with regard to the praiseworthy act O son of Prtha
5. Sat means Reality or the highest Good and also O Arjuna it is used to denote an action of exceptional merit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यजञ तपस दन च सथत सदत चचयत करम चव तदरथय सदतयवभधयत 1727
Translations:
1. Devotion to sacrifice austerity and gifts is also referred to as Sat and thus any action for such purposes is known as Sat
2. And the steadfastness in sacrifice austerity and charity is spoken of as sat good And even the action meant for these is indeed called as sat
3. Steadfastness in sacrifice austerity and gift is also called Sat and action in connection with these or for the sake of the Supreme is also called Sat
4. Steadfastness is performing sacrifice austerity and giving gifts this act is hence justly called SAT
5. Conviction in sacrifice austerity and giving is also called Sat So too an action done only for the Lords sake
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 17.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 17.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अशरदधय हत दतत तपसतपत कत च यत असदतयचयत परथ न च ततपरतय न इह 1728
Translations:
1. Whatever offering or gift is made whatever austerity is practiced and whatever action is performed without faith that is called Asat O Arjuna It is of no value here or hereafter

2. O son of Prtha whatever is offered in sacrifice and given in charity as also whatever austerity is undertaken or whatever is done without faith is said to be of no avail And it is of no consequence after death nor here
3. Whatever is sacrificed given or performed and whatever austerity is practiced without faith it is called Asat O Arjuna it is of no value here or hereafter after death

4. Without faith whatever oblation is offered whatever gift is made whatever austerity is practiced and whatever action is undertaken that is called ASAT and it is of no avail after ones death or in this world

5. Whatever is done without faith whether it be sacrifice austerity gift or anything else is called Asat meaning Unreal for it is the negation of Sat O Arjuna Such an act has no significance here or hereafter
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.1 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 18.1 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सनयससय महबह तततवमचछम वदतम तयगसय च हषकश पथककशनषदन 181
Translations:
1. Arjuna said I desire to know the truth about renunciation and abnegation separately O Krishna
2. Arjuna said O mightyarmed Hrsikesa O slayer of Kesi I want to know separately the truth about sannyasa as well as about tyaga
3. Arjuna said O mightyarmed Hrishikesa I desire to know the essence or truth of renunciation and abandonment severally O slayer of Kesi
4. Arjuna said O Mightyarmed One I desire to know in detail the distinctive nature of renunciation and relinquishment O Hrsikesa O Slayer of Kesin
5. Arjuna asked O Mighty One I desire to know how relinquishment is distinguished from renunciation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.2 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.2 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कमयन करमण नयस सनयस कवय वद सरवकरमफलतयग परहसतयग वचकषण 182
Translations:
1. The Lord said The sages hold that Sannyasa is the giving up of all works motivated by desire and the wise declare Tyaga to be the abandonment of the fruits of all works
2. The Blessed Lord said The learned ones know sannyasa to be the giving up of actions done with a desire for reward The adepts call the abandonment of the results of all works tyaga
3. The Blessed Lord said The sages understand sannyasa to be the renunciation of action with desire the wise declare the abandonment of the fruits of all actions to be tyaga
4. The Bhagavat said The seers understand the act of renouncing desiremotivated actions as renunciation the experts declare the relinquishment of the fruits of all actions to be relinquishment
5. Lord Shri Krishna replied The sages say that renunciation is forgoing an action that springs from desire and relinquishing is the surrender of its fruit
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.3 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.3 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तयजय दषवदतयक करम परहरमनषण यजञदनतपकरम न तयजयमत चपर 183
Translations:
1. Some sages say that all actions should be abandoned as evil others declare that works such as sacrifices gifts and austerities should not be abandoned
2. Some learned persons say that action beset with evil as it is should be abandoned and others say that the practice of sacrifice charity and austerity should not be abandoned
3. Some philosophers declare that actions should be abandoned as evil while others declare that acts of sacrifice gift and austerity should not be relinquished
4. Certain wise men declare that the harmful action should be relinquished while others say that the actions of performing sacrifices giving gifts and observing austerities should not be relinquished
5. Some philosophers say that all action is evil and should be abandoned whereas others maintain that acts of sacrifice benevolence and austerity should not be given up
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.4 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.4 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नशचय शरण म ततर तयग भरतसततम तयग ह परषवयघर तरवध सपरकरतत 184
Translations:
1. Listen to My decision O Arjuna concerning abandonment for abandonment is declared to be of three kinds
2. O the most excellent among the descendants of Bharata hear from Me the firm conclusion regarding that tyaga For O greatest among men tyaga has been clearly declared to be of three kinds
3. Hear from Me the conclusion or the final truth about this abandonment O best of the Bharatas abandonment indeed O best of men has been declared to be of three kinds
4. O best of Bharatas descendants Listen to My considered view about relinquishing Indeed the act of relinquishing is rightly spoken to be threefold O best among men
5. O best of Indians Listen to my judgment regarding this problem It has a threefold aspect
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.5 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.5 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यजञदनतपकरम न तयजय करयमव तत यजञ दन तपशचव पवनन मनषणम 185
Translations:
1. a The acts of sacrifice gifts and austerities should not be relinquished but should be performed b For sacrifices gifts and austerities are the means of purification for the wise
2. The practice of sacrifice charity and austerity should not be abandoned it should certainly be undertaken Sacrifice charity and austerity are indeed the purifiers of the wise
3. Acts of sacrifice gift and austerity should not be abandoned but should be performed for sacrifice gift and austerity are the purifiers of the wise

4. The actions of Vedic sacrifice gift and austerity should not be relinquished and they must necessarily be performed for the men of wisdom the Vedic sacrifice gift and austerity are the means of purification
5. Acts of sacrifice benevolence and austerity should not be given up but should be performed for they purify the aspiring soul
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.6 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.6 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: एतनयप त करमण सङग तयकतव फलन च करतवयनत म परथ नशचत मतमततमम 186
Translations:
1. It is My decided and final view that even these acts should be done O Arjuna with relinquishment of attachment and the forsaking of the fruits thereof
2. But even these actions must be undertaken by renouncing attachment and hankering for results This is My firm and definitive conclusion O Partha
3. But even these actions should be performed leaving aside attachment and the desire for rewards O Arjuna this is my certain and most assured conviction
4. Even these actions should be performed by relinquishing attachment and the fruits thereof this is my considered best opinion O son of Prtha
5. But they should be done with detachment and without thought of reward This is my final judgement
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.7 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.7 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नयतसय त सनयस करमण नपपदयत महततसय परतयगसतमस परकरतत 187
Translations:
1. But the renunciation of obligatory acts is not proper abandonment of these through delusion is declared to be of a Tamasic nature
2. Abandoning ones daily obligatory acts is not justifiable giving up those through delusion is said to be rooted in tamas
3. Verily the renunciation of obligatory action is not proper the abandonment of the same out of delusion is declared to be Tamasic
4. The renunciation of the enjoined action does not make sense and completely relinquishing it out of ignorance is proclaimed on all sides as an act of Tamas Strand
5. It is not right to give up actions which are obligatory and if they are misunderstood it is the result of sheer ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.8 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.8 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: दखमतयव यतकरम कयकलशभयततयजत स कतव रजस तयग नव तयगफल लभत 188
Translations:
1. He who renounces acts that are painful out of fear of bodily suffering performs a Rajasika abandonment however he does not gain the fruit of such abandonment
2. Whatever action one may relinquish merely as being painful out of fear of physical suffering he having resorted to renunciation based on rajas will surely not acquire the fruits of renunciation
3. He who abandons action out of fear of bodily trouble because it is painful does not obtain the merit of renunciation by performing such Rajasic renunciation

4. He who would out of fear of bodily exertion relinquish an action just because it is painfulthat person having thus made relinquishment an act of the Rajas Strand would not at all gain the fruit of that relinquishment
5. To avoid an action out of fear of physical suffering as it is likely to be painful is to act out of passion and the benefit of renunciation will not be gained
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.9 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.9 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: करयमतयव यतकरम नयत करयतऽरजन सङग तयकतव फल चव स तयग सततवक मत 189
Translations:
1. When actions are performed as they ought to be done O Arjuna renouncing attachment and the results thereof such abandonment is regarded as Sattvika
2. Whatever obligatory duty is performed just because it is a bounden duty O Arjuna by giving up attachment and the results as wellthat renunciation is considered to be based on sattva
3. Whatever obligatory action is done O Arjuna merely because it ought to be done abandoning attachment and also the desire for reward that renunciation is regarded as sattvic pure
4. This is a thing to be performed just on that ground whatever usual action is performed relinquishing attachment and also fruit that act of relinquishment is deemed to be of the Sattva strand
5. He who performs an obligatory action believing it to be a duty that ought to be done without any personal desire to do the act or to receive any returnsuch renunciation is pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.10 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.10 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न दवषटयकशल करम कशल ननषजजत तयग सततवसमवषट मधव छननसशय 1810
Translations:
1. One who has abandoned imbued with Sattva wise and whose doubts have been dispelledsuch a person neither hates disagreeable acts nor clings to agreeable ones
2. The man of renunciation imbued with sattva wise and free from doubts does not hate unbecoming action nor does he become attached to becoming activity
3. The man of renunciation pervaded by purity intelligent and with his doubts cut asunder does not hate an unpleasant task nor is he attached to a pleasant one
4. The man of renunciation who is wellpossessed of Sattva is wise and has his doubts destroyedhe does not hate unskilled action and does not cling to skilled action

5. The wise man who has attained purity whose doubts are resolved who is filled with the spirit of selfabnegation does not shy away from action due to its pain nor does he crave it due to its pleasure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.11 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.11 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न ह दहभत शकय तयकत करमणयशषत यसत करमफलतयग स तयगतयभधयत 1811
Translations:
1. For it is impossible for one who has a body to completely abandon actions But he who gives up the results of his works is called the one who has abandoned them
2. Since it is not possible for one who holds onto a body to give up actions entirely therefore he who renounces the results of his actions is called a man of renunciation
3. Indeed it is not possible for an embodied being to completely abandon actions however he who relinquishes the rewards of actions is truly called a man of renunciation
4. Indeed to relinquish actions entirely is not possible for a bodybearing one but whoever relinquishes the fruits of actions he is said to be a man of true relinquishment
5. But since those still in the body cannot entirely avoid action in their case the abandonment of the fruit of action is considered as complete renunciation
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.12 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.12 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनषटमषट मशर च तरवध करमण फलम भवतयतयगन परतय न त सनयसन कवचत 1812
Translations:
1. Undesirable desirable and mixedthus threefold is the fruit of work that accrues after death to those who have not renounced but to those who have renounced none accrues whatsoever
2. The threefold results of actionsthe undesirable the desirable and the mixedaccrue after death to those who do not resort to renunciation but never to those who resort to renunciation
3. The threefold fruit of action evil good and mixed accrues after death to those who do not abandon it but never to those who do
4. The threefold fruit of actionthe undesired the desired and the mixedaccrues even after death to those who are not men of renunciation but never to those who are men of renunciation
5. For those who cannot renounce all desires the fruit of action hereafter is threefoldgood evil and partly good and partly evil But for him who has renounced there is none
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.13 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.13 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पञचतन महबह करणन नबध म सखय कतनत परकतन सदधय सरवकरमणम 1813
Translations:
1. Learn from Me O Arjuna these five causes for the accomplishment of all acts as described in Sankhyakrtantathe science of exact understanding of thingsfor the accomplishment of works
2. O mightyarmed one learn from Me these five factors for the accomplishment of all actions which have been spoken of in the Vedanta in which actions terminate
3. Learn from Me O mightyarmed Arjuna these five causes as declared in the Sankhya system for the accomplishment of all actions
4. O mightyarmed one Learn from Me these five causes declared in the conclusion of deliberations on proper knowledge for the accomplishment of all actions
5. I will now tell you O Mighty Man the five causes that must come together according to the final decision of philosophy for an action to be accomplished
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.14 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.14 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अधषठन तथ करत करण च पथगवधम ववधशच पथकचषट दव चवतर पञचमम 1814
Translations:
1. The seat of action the agent the various kinds of organs the different and distinctive functions of vital air and the fifth among theseDivinityall these
2. The locus the agent the different kinds of organs the many and varied activities and the divine are the fifth here
3. The body the doer the various senses the different functions of various kinds and the presiding deitythe fifth
4. The basis the agent the various instruments the distinct activities of various kinds and Destiny which is the fifth factor are all present
5. They have a body a personality physical organs and manifold activities and destinies
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.15 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.15 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शररवङमनभरयतकरम पररभत नर नययय व वपरत व पञचत तसय हतव 1815
Translations:
1. For whatever action a person undertakes with their body speech and mind whether right or wrong ie enjoined or forbidden by the Sastras the following five are its causes
2. Whatever action a person performs with their body speech and mind whether it be just or its opposite these five are the causes of it
3. Whatever action a person performs with their body speech and mind whether right or wrong these five are its causes
