4. O Arjuna Whatever action is undertaken with the body speech or mind whether it is lawful or otherwise its factors are these five
5. Whatever action a person performs whether by muscular effort speech or thought and whether it be right or wrong these five are the essential causes
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.16 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.16 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: ततरव सत करतरमतमन कवल त य पशयतयकतबदधतवनन स पशयत दरमत 1816
Translations:
1. Therefore he who due to his uncultivated understanding perceives only the self as the agent he of wicked mind does not truly perceive
2. Therefore anyone whose intellect is imperfect and perceives the Absolute Self as the agent does not perceive properly and has a distorted intellect
3. Now such being the case verily he who owing to an untrained understanding looks upon his Self which is isolated as the agent he of perverted intelligence does not see
4. But this being the case whoever views himself as the sole agent due to their imperfect intellectthey the defectiveminded ones do not view things rightly
5. But the fool who due to his immature judgment supposes that it is his own Self alone that acts perverts the truth and does not see rightly
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.17 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.17 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यसय नहकत भव बदधरयसय न लपयत हतवप स इमललकनन हनत न नबधयत 1817
Translations:
1. He who is free from the notion of I am the doer and whose understanding is not tainted does not slay even though he slays all these men nor is he bound
2. He who has no feeling of egoism whose intellect is untainted he does not kill nor does he become boundeven by killing these creatures
3. He who is free from the egoistic notion whose intelligence is not tainted by good or evil though he slays these people he does not slay nor is he bound by the action
4. He whose mental disposition is not dominated by the sense of I and whose intellect is not stainedhe even if he slays these worlds does not really slay any and is not fettered
5. He who has no pride and whose intellect is unalloyed by attachment even though he kills these people he does not kill them and his act does not bind him
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.18 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.18 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जञन जञय परजञत तरवध करमचदन करण करम करतत तरवध करमसगरह 1818
Translations:
1. Knowledge the object of knowledge and the knower are the threefold incitements to action The instrument the act and the agent are the threefold constituents of action
2. Knowledge the object of knowledge and the knowerthese are the threefold inducements to action The comprehension of actions comes under three heads the instruments the object and the subject
3. Knowledge the knowable and the knower form the threefold impulse for action the organ the action and the agent form the threefold basis of action

4. The instrument of knowledge the object of knowledge and the knowing subjectthe prompting in action consisting of these threefold elementsis the proper grasping of action with threefold elements namely the instrument the object and the agent
5. Knowledge the knower and the object of knowledge are the three incentives to action and the act the actor and the instrument are the threefold constituents
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.19 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.19 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: जञन करम च करत च तरधव गणभदत परचयत गणसखयन यथवचछण तनयप 1819
Translations:
1. Knowledge action and agent are declared in the science of the Gunas to be of three kinds according to the difference in the Gunas Listen to them also as they are
2. Knowledge action and agent are stated in the teaching about the gunas to be only of three kinds according to the differences of the gunas Hear about them also as they are
3. Knowledge action and actor are declared in the science of the Gunas Sankhya philosophy to be of three kinds only according to the distinction of the Gunas Of these hear duly

4. The instrument of knowledge the object and the agent are just three kinds due to the differences in the strandsthus it is declared in enumerating the strands You must also listen to these from Me as they are
5. The knowledge the action and the doer differ according to their qualities Listen to this as well
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.20 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.20 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सरवभतष यनक भवमवययमकषत अवभकत वभकतष तजजञन वदध सततवकम 1820
Translations:
1. Know that knowledge to be sattvika by which one sees in all beings an immutable existence undivided even in the divided
2. Know that knowledge to originate from sattva through which one sees a single undecaying undivided entity in all the diversified things
3. That by which one sees the indestructible Reality in all beings not separate in any of themknow that knowledge to be Sattvic

4. That instrument of knowledge by means of which one perceives the singular immutable existence in all beings the unclassified among the classified onesthat you must know to be born of the Sattva Strand
5. That knowledge which sees the One Indestructible in all beings the One Indivisible in all separate lives can be truly called Pure Knowledge
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.21 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.21 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: पथकतवन त यजजञन ननभवनपथगवधन वतत सरवष भतष तजजञन वदध रजसम 1821
Translations:
1. But that knowledge which sees all beings due to their individual nature as distinct entitiesknow that knowledge to be Rajasic
2. But know that knowledge to originate from rajas which amidst all things apprehends the different entities of various kinds as distinct as possessing distinct selves
3. But that knowledge which sees in all beings various entities of distinct kinds as being distinct from one another know thou that knowledge to be Rajasic
4. That instrument of knowledge by means of which one considers the varied natures of different sorts in all beings as truly distinctthat is regarded to be of the Rajas strand
5. The knowledge that perceives the manifold existence in all beings as separate comes from passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.22 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.22 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतत कतसनवदकसमनकरय सकतमहतकम अतततवरथवदलप च तततमसमदहतम 1822
Translations:
1. But that knowledge is declared to be Tamasika which clings to one single act as if it were the whole which is not based on reason and which is false and insignificant
2. But that knowledge is said to be born of tamas which is confined to one form as if it were all which is irrational unconcerned with truth and trivial
3. But that which clings to one single effect as if it were the whole without reason without any foundation in Truth and is trivialthat is declared to be Tamasic

4. That instrumentofknowledge because of which one not realizing the whole gets indulged without reason in a particular activity and which is unconcerned with the real nature of things and is insignificantthat is declared to be of the Tamas Strand
5. But that which clings blindly to one idea as if it were all without logic truth or insight has its origin in darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.23 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.23 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: नयत सङगरहतमरगदवषत कतम अफलपरपसन करम यतततसततवकमचयत 1823
Translations:
1. That obligatory act is said to be Sattvika which is done without attachment without desire or aversion and by one who seeks no fruit
2. The daily obligatory action which is performed without attachment and without likes or dislikes by one who does not hanker for rewards is said to be born of sattva
3. An action that is ordained free from attachment done without love or hatred and without desire for reward is declared to be Sattvic

4. The object that has been acquired with determination without attachment and without desire or hatred by one who does not crave to reap the fruit of their action that is declared to be of the Sattva Strand
5. An obligatory action done by one who is disinterested who neither likes nor dislikes it and gives no thought to the consequences that may follow such an action is Pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.24 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.24 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतत कमपसन करम सहङकरण व पन करयत बहलयस तदरजसमदहतम 1824
Translations:
1. But that act is said to be Rajasic which is performed with great effort by one who seeks to gratify their desires and under the prompting of egoism
2. But that action is said to be born of rajas which is done by one desirous of results or by one who is egotistical and which is highly strenuous
3. But that action which is done by one longing for the fulfillment of desires or gain with egotism or with much effort is declared to be Rajasic passionate
4. The object which is abundant in afflictions and which is further desired by one who craves to attain the desired thing with the feeling of Ithat is considered to be of the Rajas Strand
5. But even though an action involves the most strenuous endeavor yet if the doer is seeking to gratify their desires and is filled with personal vanity it may be assumed to originate in passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.25 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.25 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अनबनध कषय हसमनपकषय च परषम महदरभयत करम यततततमसमचयत 1825
Translations:
1. That act is said to be Tamasika which is undertaken through delusion without regard to consequences loss injury and ones own capacity
2. That action is said to be born of tamas which is undertaken out of delusion without consideration of its consequences loss harm and ability
3. That action which is undertaken from delusion without regard for the consequences loss injury and ones own ability is declared to be Tamasic dark
4. The object which is gained due to ignorance without considering the result the loss the injury to others and ones own strengththat is declared to be of the Tamas Strand
5. An action undertaken through delusion with no regard to the spiritual implications the capacity of the doer or the potential harm that may follow is assumed to be a product of ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.26 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.26 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: मकतसङगऽनहवद धतयतसहसमनवत सदधयसदधयरनरवकर करत सततवक उचयत 1826
Translations:
1. That agent is said to be Sattvika who is free from attachment who does not boast of himself who is endowed with steadiness and zeal and who is unaffected by success and failure

2. Ast introduces this verse with Idanim kartrbhedah ucyate Now is being stated the distinctions among the agentsTr The agent who is free from attachment Attachment to results or the idea of agentship not egotistical endowed with fortitude and diligence and unperturbed by success and failure is said to be possessed of sattva
3. An agent who is free from attachment nonegoistic endowed with firmness and enthusiasm and unaffected by success or failure is considered to be of a Sattvic pure nature

4. The agent who is free from attachment who does not make any speech of egoism who is full of contentment and enthusiasm and who does not change mentally in success or failurethat agent is said to be of the Sattva nature
5. But when a person has no sentiment or ego when they possess courage and confidence not caring whether they succeed or fail then their action arises from purity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.27 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.27 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रग करमफलपरपसरलबध हसतमकऽशच हरषशकनवत करत रजस परकरतत 1827
Translations:
1. That doer is called Rajasika who is passionate who seeks the fruits of his actions who is greedy harmful and impure and who is swayed by delight and grief
2. The agent who has attachment who is desirous of the results of actions covetous cruel by nature unclean and subject to joy and sorrow is declared to be possessed of rajas
3. Passionate desiring to obtain the reward of their actions greedy cruel impure moved by joy and sorrow such an agent is said to be Rajasic

4. The agent who is a man of passion who craves the fruit of his actions and is avaricious who is injurious by nature impure and is overpowered by joy and griefthat agent is proclaimed to be of the Rajas Strand
5. In one who is impulsive greedy looking for reward violent impure and torn between joy and sorrow it may be assumed that Passion is predominant
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.28 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.28 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अयकत परकत सतबध शठ नषकतकऽलस वषद दरघसतर च करत तमस उचयत 1828
Translations:
1. That doer is said to be Tamasika who is unqualified unrefined obstinate depraved dishonest indolent despondent and lethargic
2. The agent who is unsteady naive inflexible deceitful wicked lazy sullen and procrastinating is said to be possessed of tamas
3. Unsteady vulgar inflexible deceitful malicious lazy despondent and procrastinatingsuch an agent is called Tamasic
4. The agent who does not exert is vulgar obstinate and deceitful they are a man of wickedness and are lazy sorrowful and procrastinatingsuch an agent is said to be of the Tamas strand
5. While he whose purpose is weak who is lowminded stubborn dishonest malicious lazy despondent and procrastinatinghe may be assumed to be in darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.29 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.29 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बदधरभद धतशचव गणतसतरवध शरण परचयमनमशषण पथकतवन धनञजय 1829
Translations:
1. Hear now O Arjuna the threefold division of Buddhi reason and Dhrti fortitude according to the Gunas to be fully and severally set forth
2. O Dhananjaya listen to the classification of the intellect and fortitude which is threefold according to the gunas while it is being stated in detail and separately
3. Hear thou the threefold division of intellect and firmness according to the Gunas as I declare them fully and distinctly O Arjuna
4. You must listen to Me as I expound the threefold division of the intellect and content both completely and individually based on the Strands O Dhananjaya
5. Reason and conviction are threefold according to the quality that is dominant I will explain them fully and separately O Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.30 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.30 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: परवतत च नवतत च करयकरय भयभय बनध मकष च य वतत बदध स परथ सततवक 1830
Translations:
1. That Buddhi O Arjuna which knows activity and renunciation what should be done and what should not be done fear and fearlessness bondage and releasethat Buddhi is Sattvika
2. O Partha that intellect is born of sattva which understands action and withdrawal duty and what is not duty the sources of fear and fearlessness as well as bondage and freedom
3. The intellect which knows the path of work and renunciation what should be done and what should not be done fear and fearlessness bondage and liberationthat intellect is Sattvic pure O Arjuna

4. The intellect which knows the activity and the cessation from activity the proper and improper actions the fear and nonfear and the bondage and emancipationthat intellect is considered to be of the Sattva Strand
5. That intellect which understands the creation and dissolution of life what actions should be done and what not which discriminates between fear and fearlessness bondage and deliverance is Pure
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.31 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.31 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यय धरममधरम च करय चकरयमव च अयथवतपरजनत बदध स परथ रजस 1831
Translations:
1. The Buddhi that gives an erroneous knowledge of Dharma and Adharma its opposite and also of what should be done and what should not be done O Arjuna is Rajasic
2. O Partha that intellect is born of rajas with which one wrongly understands virtue and vice as well as what ought to be done and ought not to be done
3. That by which one wrongly understands dharma and adharma and also what ought to be done and what ought not to be donethat intellect O Arjuna is rajasic passionate
4. The intellect by means of which one decides incorrectly between the righteous and the unrighteous and what is a proper action and an improper onethat intellect is of the Rajas O son of Prtha
5. The intellect that does not understand what is right and wrong and what should be done and not done is under the sway of Passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.32 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.32 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: अधरम धरममत य मनयत तमसऽऽवत सरवरथनवपरतशच बदध स परथ तमस 1832
Translations:
1. That Buddhi O Arjuna which enveloped in darkness regards Adharma as Dharma and reverses every value is Tamasika
2. O Partha that intellect is born of tamas which being covered by darkness considers vice as virtue and indeed perceives all things contrary to what they are
3. That intellect O Arjuna which is enveloped in darkness and sees Adharma as Dharma and all things perverted is Tamasic dark
4. The intellect which containing darkness ignorance conceives the unrighteous one as righteous and all things topsyturvythat intellect is deemed to be of the Tamas Strand
5. And that which shrouded in Ignorance thinks wrong to be right and sees everything perversely O Arjuna that intellect is ruled by Darkness
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.33 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.33 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: धतय यय धरयत मनपरणनदरयकरय यगनवयभचरणय धत स परथ सततवक 1833
Translations:
1. That fortitude by which one maintains the unwavering activities of the mind and vital force and these senseorgans through yogathat fortitude is of the nature of sattva
2. O Partha the firmness that is unfailing through concentration with which one restrains the functions of the mind vital forces and the organsthat firmness is born of sattva
3. The unwavering firmness through which Yoga restrains the functions of the mind lifeforce and sensesthat firmness O Arjuna is Sattvic pure
4. The unfailing content by which one restrains through Yoga the activities of the mind the living breath and the senses is considered to be of the Sattva strand
5. The conviction and steady concentration with which the mind the energy and the senses are controlledO Arjunaare the result of purity
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.34 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.34 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यय त धरमकमरथन धतय धरयतऽरजन परसङगन फलकङकष धत स परथ रजस 1834
Translations:
1. That dhṛti O Arjuna by which one who is desirous of fruits longs for them with intense attachment and holds fast to duty desire and wealththat dhṛti is rajasika
2. But O Partha the steadfastness with which one holds on to righteousness covetable things and wealth desiring their fruits as the occasion for each arises that steadfastness is born of rajas
3. But that O Arjuna by which one holds fast to Dharma duty enjoyment of pleasures and earning of wealth on account of attachment and desire for rewardthat firmness O Arjuna is Rajasic passionate
4. O Arjuna The contentment by which one restrains ones bounden duty pleasure and wealth and consequently desires the fruits of actionthat contentment is of the Rajas strand O son of Prtha
5. The conviction that always holds fast to rituals selfinterest and wealth for the sake of what they may bring forth comes from passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.35 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.35 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यय सवपन भय शक वषद मदमव च न वमञचत दरमध धत स परथ तमस 1835
Translations:
1. That Dhrti by which a foolish person does not give up sleep fear grief depression and passion O Arjuna is of the nature of Tamas
2. That firmness is considered to be born of tamas due to which a person with a corrupt intellect does not give up sleep fear sorrow despondency as well as sensuality
3. That firmness O Arjuna by which a stupid man does not abandon sleep fear grief despair and conceit is Tamasic
4. The contentment where a foolish man does not give up his sleep fear grief despondency and arrogancethat contentment is deemed to be of the Tamas Strand
5. And that which clings perversely to false idealism fear grief despair and vanity is the product of ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.36 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.36 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सख तवदन तरवध शरण म भरतरषभ अभयसदरमत यतर दखनत च नगचछत 1836
Translations:
1. Now hear from Me O Arjuna the threefold division of pleasure৷৷ that in which one rejoices through long practice and in which one comes to the end of pain

2. Now hear from Me O scion of the Bharata dynasty as regards the three kinds of joy That in which one delights due to habit and certainly attains the cessation of sorrows S and SS take the second line of this verse along with the next verse referring to sattvika happinessTr
3. And now O Arjuna hear from Me of the threefold pleasure in which one rejoices through practice and surely comes to the end of pain
4. O best among the Bharatas Now you must also listen to Me about the threefold happiness wherein one delights in practice and attains the end of suffering
5. Hear further the three kinds of pleasure That which increases day by day delivers one from misery
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.37 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.37 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यततदगर वषमव परणमऽमतपमम ततसख सततवक परकतमतमबदधपरसदजम 1837
Translations:
1. That which at first is like a poison but becomes like an elixir in the end born from the serene state of mind focused on the Selfsuch pleasure is said to be Sattvika
2. That which is like poison in the beginning but is likened to nectar in the end and which arises from the purity of ones intellectthat joy is spoken of as born of sattva
3. That which is like poison at first but in the end like nectarthat happiness is declared to be sattvic born of the purity of ones own mind due to selfrealization
4. The happiness which is like poison in the moment but is like nectar in the resultthat happiness born of serenity of the soul and intellect you must know to be of the sattva strand
5. Which at first seems like poison but afterwards acts like nectarthat pleasure is pure for it is born of wisdom
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.38 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.38 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वषयनदरयसयगदयततदगरऽमतपमम परणम वषमव ततसख रजस समतम 1838
Translations:
1. That pleasure which arises from the contact of senses with their objects which is like elixir at first but like poison in the end is said to be Rajasic
2. That joy is referred to as born of rajas which arising from the contact of the organs and their objects is like nectar in the beginning but like poison in the end
3. That happiness which arises from the contact of the senses with the objects which is initially like nectar but eventually like poison is said to be Rajasic

4. The happiness which is like nectar at its due time due to the contact between the senses and senseobjects but which is like poison at the time of its resultthat is considered to be of the Rajas Strand
5. That which at first is like nectar as the senses revel in their objects but in the end acts like poisonthat pleasure arises from Passion
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.39 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.39 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यदगर चनबनध च सख महनमतमन नदरलसयपरमदतथ तततमसमदहतम 1839
Translations:
1. That pleasure which at the beginning and end deludes the self through sleep sloth and error is declared to be tamasic
2. That joy is said to be born of tamas which both in the beginning and in the end is delusive to oneself and arises from sleep laziness and inadvertence
3. That happiness which at first as well as in the end deludes the self and which arises from sleep indolence and heedlessnessthat is declared to be Tamasic
4. The happiness that both initially and subsequently is of the nature of deluding the Self and which results from sleep indolence and heedlessnessthat is stated to be of the Tamas Strand
5. While the pleasure that drugs the senses from start to finish which springs from indolence lethargy and follythat pleasure flows from Ignorance
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.40 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.40 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न तदसत पथवय व दव दवष व पन सततव परकतजरमकत यदभ सयततरभरगण 1840
Translations:
1. There is no creature on earth or among the gods in heaven that is exempt from these three Gunas born of Prakrti
2. There is no entity in the world nor among the gods in heaven that can be free from these three gunas born of Nature
3. There is no being on earth or in heaven among the gods that is liberated from the three qualities born of Nature
4. Whether on earth or among the gods in heaven there is not a single being that is free from these three strands born of material nature
5. There is nothing anywhere on earth or in the higher worlds that is free from the three Qualities for they are born of Nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.41 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.41 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: बरहमणकषतरयवश शदरण च परतप करमण परवभकतन सवभवपरभवरगण 1841
Translations:
1. The duties of the Brahmanas Ksatriyas Vaisyas and Sudras are clearly divided O Arjuna according to their Gunas born of their nature
2. O scorcher of enemies the duties of the Brahmanas Ksatriyas Vaisyas and Sudras have been fully classified according to the gunas born from Nature
3. Of Brahmanas Kshatriyas Vaisyas and Sudras O Arjuna the duties are distributed according to the qualities born of their own nature
4. The duties of the Brahmanas the Kshatriyas the Vaishyas and the Shudras are properly classified according to the qualities that are the sources of their nature O scorcher of foes
5. O Arjuna The duties of spiritual teachers the soldiers the traders and the servants have all been fixed according to the dominant quality in their nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.42 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.42 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शम दमसतप शच कषनतररजवमव च जञन वजञनमसतकय बरहमकरम सवभवजम 1842
Translations:
1. The control of the senses and mind austerity purity forbearance uprightness knowledge special knowledge and faithall these constitute the duty of a Brahmana born of his inherent nature

2. The natural duties of the Brahmanas are the control of the internal and external organs austerity purity forgiveness straightforwardness knowledge as well as wisdom Knowledge refers to the understanding of subjects presented by the scriptures wisdom means making them matters of ones own experience and faith
3. Serenity selfrestraint austerity purity forgiveness and uprightness as well as knowledge realization and belief in God are the duties of Brahmanas born of their own nature
4. Quietude selfcontrol purity forbearance straightforwardness knowledge wisdom and faith in another world are the duties of the Brahmanas born of their nature
5. Serenity selfrestraint austerity purity forgiveness uprightness knowledge wisdom and faith in Godthese constitute the duty of a spiritual teacher
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.43 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.43 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरय तज धतरदकषय यदध चपयपलयनम दनमशवरभवशच कषतर करम सवभवजम 1843
Translations:
1. Valor invincibility steadiness dexterity and not fleeing in battle generosity and regality are the duties of a Kshatriya born of his inherent nature
2. The natural duties of the Ksatriyas are heroism boldness fortitude capability and not retreating from battle generosity and lordliness
3. Prowess splendor firmness dexterity and not fleeing from battle generosity and lordliness are the duties of the Kshatriyas born of their own nature
4. Heroic deeds fiery energy firmness dexterity and also nonattachment form the duties of the Ksatriyas born of their nature in battle giving gifts and overlordship
5. Valor glory firmness skill generosity steadiness in battle and the ability to rulethese constitute the duty of a soldier They flow from his own nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.44 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.44 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: कषगरकषयवणजय वशयकरम सवभवजम परचरयतमक करम शदरसयप सवभवजम 1844
Translations:
1. Agriculture cattlebreeding and trade are the duties of the Vaisya born of their nature And the duty of a Sudra is one of service born of their nature
2. The natural duties of the Vaisyas are agriculture cattlerearing and trading The natural duty of the Sudras too is in the form of service
3. Agriculture cattlerearing and trade are the duties of the Vaisya merchant born of their own nature and service is the duty of the Sudra servantclass born of their own nature
4. Ploughing tending to cattle and trading are the actions of the Vaisyas born of their nature The action of service is of the Sudras born of their nature
5. Agriculture the protection of cows and trade are the duties of a trader in accordance with his nature The duty of a servant is to serve which also agrees with his nature
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.45 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.45 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: सव सव करमणयभरत ससदध लभत नर सवकरमनरत सदध यथ वनदत तचछण 1845
Translations:
1. Devoting oneself to ones duty one attains perfection Hear now how one devoted to their own duty attains perfection
2. Being devoted to his own duty one attains complete success Hear how one devoted to their own duty achieves success
3. Each person devoted to their own duty attains perfection How they attain perfection while being engaged in their own duty hear now
4. A person devoted to their own respective action attains success How one attains success through devotion to their own action that you must hear from Me
5. Perfection is attained when one diligently attends to their duty Listen and I will tell you how it is attained by one who always minds their own duty
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.46 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.46 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यत परवततरभतन यन सरवमद ततम सवकरमण तमभयरचय सदध वनदत मनव 1846
Translations:
1. From whom all beings arise and by whom all this is pervadedby worshipping Him with ones own duty one reaches perfection
2. A human being achieves success by adoring through his own duties Him from whom is the origin of creatures and by whom all this is pervaded
3. He from whom all the beings have evolved and by whom all this is pervaded worshipping Him with his own duty one attains perfection
4. From whence the activities of the beings arise and by which this universe is pervadedworshipping That by ones own prescribed action one attains success
5. One reaches perfection by dedicating their actions to God Who is the source of all being and fills everything
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.47 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.47 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरयनसवधरम वगण परधरमतसवनषठतत सवभवनयत करम करवननपनत कलबषम 1847
Translations:
1. Better is ones own duty though ill done than the duty of another though wellperformed When one does the duty ordained by their own nature they incur no stain
2. Ones own duty though defective is superior to anothers duty well performed By performing a duty according to ones own nature one does not incur sin
3. Better is ones own duty even if it is destitute of merits than the duty of another well performed He who does the duty ordained by his own nature incurs no sin
4. Better is ones own prescribed duties born of ones nature even though it is devoid of excellence than anothers duty well executed the doer of duty dependent on ones own nature does not incur sin

5. It is better to do ones own duty however defective it may be than to follow the duty of another however well one may perform it He who does his duty as his own nature reveals it never commits a sin
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.48 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.48 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.49 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.49 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.50 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.50 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.51 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.51 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.52 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.52 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.53 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.53 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.54 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.54 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.55 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.55 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.56 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.56 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.57 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.57 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.58 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.58 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.59 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.59 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.60 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.60 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.61 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.61 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.62 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.62 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.63 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.63 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.64 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.64 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.65 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.65 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.66 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.66 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.67 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.67 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.68 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.68 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

3. He who with supreme devotion to Me teaches this supreme secret to My devotees shall undoubtedly come to Me

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.69 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.69 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: न च तसमनमनषयष कशचनम परयकततम भवत न च म तसमदनय परयतर भव 1869

1. There is no one among men who does dearer service to Me than him Nor shall there be anyone on earth dearer to Me than him

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.70 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.70 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

3. And he who studies this sacred dialogue of ours by him I shall have been worshipped through the sacrifice of wisdom such is my conviction
4. Whoever would learn this sacred dialogue of both of us by them I am delighted through the knowledgesacrifice This is my opinion

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.71 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.71 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:
Sanskrit: शरदधवननसयशच शरणयदप य नर सऽप मकत शभललकनपरपनयतपणयकरमणम 1871

<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.72 spoken by भगवान. [/INST] Verse 18.72 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by भगवान, is as follows:

3. Has this been heard O Arjuna with onepointed focus Has the delusion of your ignorance been destroyed O Dhananjaya
4. O son of Prtha Have you heard this with an attentive mind O Dhananjaya Has your strong delusion born of ignorance been completely destroyed
5. O Arjuna have you listened attentively to My words Has your ignorance and delusion gone
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.73 spoken by अर्जुन. [/INST] Verse 18.73 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by अर्जुन, is as follows:

2. Arjuna said O Acyuta my delusion has been destroyed and my memory has been regained through Your grace I stand with my doubt removed I shall follow Your instructions
3. Arjuna said My delusion has been destroyed for I have gained my knowledge memory through Your grace O Krishna I am now free from doubts I will act according to Your word
4. Arjuna said My delusion is destroyed I have regained my recollection through your grace O Acyuta I stand firm free of doubts I shall execute your command
5. Arjuna replied My Lord O Immutable One My delusion has fled By Your Grace O Changeless One the light has dawned My doubts have been dispelled and I stand before You ready to do Your will
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.74 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 18.74 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:

1. Sanjaya said Thus I have heard this wondrous dialogue between Vasudeva and the greatminded Arjuna which makes my hair stand on end
2. Sanjaya said I thus heard this conversation between Vasudeva and the greatsouled Partha which is unique and makes ones hair stand on end
3. Sanjaya said Thus I have heard this wonderful dialogue between Krishna and the highsouled Arjuna which causes ones hair to stand on end
4. Sanjaya said Thus I have heard this wonderful and thrilling dialogue between Vasudeva and the mightyminded son of Prtha
5. Sanjaya said I heard this rare wonderful and soulstirring discourse between Lord Shri Krishna and the greatsouled Arjuna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.75 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 18.75 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: वयसपरसदचछरतवनतदगहयमह परम यग यगशवरतकषणतसकषतकथयत सवयम 1875
Translations:
1. By the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme mystery of Yoga declared in person by Krsna the Lord of Yoga
2. Through the favor of Vyasa I heard this secret concerning the supreme Yoga from Krishna the Lord of Yogas while He Himself was speaking
3. Through the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme and most secret Yoga directly from Krishna the Lord of Yoga Himself declaring it
4. Through the grace of Vyasa I have heard this highly secret supreme Yoga from Krsna the Lord of Yogis while He was imparting it personally
5. Through the blessing of the sage Vyasa I heard this secret and noble science from the lips of its Master Lord Shri Krishna
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.76 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 18.76 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: रजनससमतय ससमतय सवदमममदभतम कशवरजनय पणय हषयम च महरमह 1876
Translations:
1. O King remembering again and again this wondrous and auspicious dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna I rejoice time and again
2. And O King while repeatedly remembering this unique sacred dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna I rejoice every moment
3. O King remembering this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna I continually rejoice
4. O King By repeatedly recollecting this wonderful pious dialogue between Kesava and Arjuna I am filled with delight again and again
5. O King The more I think of that marvelous and holy discourse the more I lose myself in joy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.77 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 18.77 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: तचच ससमतय ससमतय रपमतयदभत हर वसमय म महन रजन हषयम च पन पन 1877
Translations:
1. And remembering over and over again that most marvelous form of Hari great is my amazement O King and I rejoice again and again
2. O King repeatedly recollecting that greatly extraordinary form of Hari I am struck with wonder and rejoice again and again
3. And remembering again and again that most wonderful form of Hari I am filled with great wonder O King and I rejoice again and again
4. O great king On recalling in my mind that extremely wonderful supreme form of Hari I am amazed and experience joy again and again
5. As I recall again and again the exceeding beauty of the Lord I am filled with amazement and joy
<s>[INST] Provide the meaning and context of Bhagavad Gita verse 18.78 spoken by सञ्जय. [/INST] Verse 18.78 of the Bhagavad Gita, spoken by सञ्जय, is as follows:
Sanskrit: यतर यगशवर कषण यतर परथ धनरधर ततर शररवजय भतरधरव नतरमतरमम 1878
Translations:
1. Wherever there is Sri Krsna the Lord of Yoga and Arjuna the archer there is always fortune victory wealth and sound morality This is my firm conviction
2. Where there is Krishna the Lord of yogas and where there is Partha the wielder of the bow there is fortune victory prosperity and unfailing prudence Such is my conviction
3. Wherever Krishna the Lord of Yoga is and wherever Arjuna the wielder of the bow is there is prosperity victory happiness and a firm policy this is my conviction
4. Where Krishna the Lord of Yogis remains where the son of Prtha holds his bow there lies fortune victory prosperity and firm justiceso I believe
5. Wherever Lord Shri Krishna the Prince of Wisdom and Arjuna the Great Archer are I am certain that good fortune victory happiness and righteousness will follow
